general.g_text: 한국 사람들은 추운 겨울이 오기 전에 김장을 합니다. ㉠ 김장은 겨울 동안 먹을 김치를 한꺼번에 모두 만드는 것을 말합니다. ㉡ 보통 11월~12월에 만들어서 다음 해 봄까지 먹습니다. ㉢ 몇 달 동안 먹을 반찬이기 때문에 김장 김치를 맛있게 만드는 것은 매우 중요합니다. ㉣ 날씨가 추운 북쪽 지방에서는 김치를 덜 맵고 덜 짜게 만들고, 날씨가 더운 남쪽 지방에서는 오래 보관하기 위해 더 맵고 더 짜게 만듭니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Người Hàn Quốc làm Gimjang - việc muối kimchi trước khi mùa đông lạnh đến. ㉠ Kimjang là việc làm kimchi trong một lần để ăn trong suốt mùa đông. ㉡ Thông thường sẽ làm vào tháng 11~12 rồi ăn đến mùa xuân năm sau. ㉢ Kimchi là món ăn phụ sẽ được ăn trong một vài tháng nên việc làm kimchi kimjang ngon rất quan trọng. ㉣ Ở phía Bắc có thời tiết lạnh, kimchi được làm ít cay và ít mặn hơn, còn ở phía Nam có thời tiết nóng, kimchi được làm cay hơn và mặn hơn để có thể bảo được quản lâu.
g_text_translate.en: Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.it:
I coreani fanno il "kimjang" prima che arrivi il freddo inverno. ㉠ 'Kimjang' si riferisce a fare tutto il kimchi per l'inverno in una volta. ㉡ Di solito viene prodotto tra novembre e dicembre e consumato fino alla primavera successiva. ㉢ È molto importante rendere delizioso il kimchi perché è un contorno che verrà mangiato per diversi mesi. ㉣ Nelle regioni settentrionali dove il clima è freddo, il kimchi è reso meno piccante e meno salato, e nelle regioni meridionali dove il clima è caldo, è reso più piccante e salato per conservarlo a lungo.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les Coréens pratiquent le « kimjang » avant l'arrivée du froid hivernal. ㉠ « Kimjang » fait référence à la fabrication simultanée de tous les kimchi pour l'hiver. ㉡ Il est généralement fabriqué entre novembre et décembre et consommé jusqu'au printemps suivant. ㉢ Il est très important de rendre le kimchi délicieux car c'est un plat d'accompagnement qui se mangera pendant plusieurs mois. ㉣ Dans les régions du nord où il fait froid, le kimchi est rendu moins épicé et moins salé, et dans les régions du sud où il fait chaud, il est rendu plus épicé et salé pour le conserver longtemps.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.es:
Los coreanos hacen 'kimjang' antes de que llegue el frío invierno. ㉠ 'Kimjang' se refiere a hacer todo el kimchi para el invierno a la vez. ㉡ Se suele hacer entre noviembre y diciembre y se come hasta la primavera siguiente. ㉢ Es muy importante hacer que el kimchi sea delicioso porque es una guarnición que se comerá durante varios meses. ㉣ En las regiones del norte, donde el clima es frío, el kimchi se hace menos picante y menos salado, y en las regiones del sur, donde el clima es cálido, se hace más picante y salado para conservarlo durante mucho tiempo.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.id:
Orang Korea melakukan 'kimjang' sebelum musim dingin tiba. ㉠ 'Kimjang' berarti membuat semua kimchi untuk musim dingin sekaligus. ㉡ Biasanya dibuat antara November dan Desember dan dimakan hingga musim semi berikutnya. ㉢ Sangat penting untuk membuat kimchi enak karena merupakan lauk yang akan dimakan selama beberapa bulan. ㉣ Di wilayah utara yang cuacanya dingin, kimchi dibuat tidak terlalu pedas dan kurang asin, dan di wilayah selatan yang cuacanya panas, dibuat lebih pedas dan asin agar tahan lama.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.ja:
韓国人は寒い冬が来る前に「キムジャン」をします。 ㉠「キムジャン」とは、冬のキムチを一度に作ること。 ㉡ 11月から12月にかけて作られ、翌春まで食べられる。 ㉢ キムチは数ヶ月間食べるおかずなので、おいしく作ることがとても重要です。 ㉣ 寒冷な北部ではキムチを辛さ控えめに、南部の暑い地方ではキムチを辛く塩辛くして日持ちを良くする。

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.de:
Koreaner machen „Kimjang“, bevor der kalte Winter kommt. ㉠ „Kimjang“ bezieht sich auf die Zubereitung des gesamten Kimchi für den Winter auf einmal. ㉡ Es wird normalerweise zwischen November und Dezember hergestellt und bis zum folgenden Frühling gegessen. ㉢ Es ist sehr wichtig, Kimchi köstlich zuzubereiten, da es eine Beilage ist, die mehrere Monate lang gegessen wird. ㉣ In den nördlichen Regionen, in denen das Wetter kalt ist, wird Kimchi weniger scharf und weniger salzig gemacht, und in den südlichen Regionen, in denen das Wetter heiß ist, wird es würziger und salziger gemacht, um es lange zu halten.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
韩国人会在寒冷的冬天到来之前做“泡菜”。 ㉠ 'Kimjang' 指的是一次制作所有冬天的泡菜。 ㉡ 通常在11月到12月之间制作，一直吃到次年春天。 ㉢ 把泡菜做得好吃很重要，因为它是一道要吃几个月的配菜。 ㉣ 在天气寒冷的北方地区，泡菜做得不辣不咸，而在天气炎热的南方地区，泡菜则做得更辣、更咸，以便长期保存。

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
韓國人會在寒冷的冬天到來之前做“泡菜”。 ㉠ 'Kimjang' 指的是一次製作所有冬天的泡菜。 ㉡ 通常在11月到12月之間製作，一直吃到次年春天。 ㉢ 把泡菜做得好吃很重要，因為它是一道要吃幾個月的配菜。 ㉣ 在天氣寒冷的北方地區，泡菜做得不辣不咸，而在天氣炎熱的南方地區，泡菜則做得更辣、更咸，以便長期保存。

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.ru:
Корейцы делают «кимчжан» перед наступлением холодной зимы. ㉠ «Кимчжан» означает приготовление всего кимчи на зиму сразу. ㉡ Обычно его готовят с ноября по декабрь и едят до следующей весны. ㉢ Очень важно сделать кимчи вкусным, потому что это гарнир, который можно есть в течение нескольких месяцев. ㉣ В северных регионах, где погода холодная, кимчи делают менее острым и менее соленым, а в южных регионах, где погода жаркая, его делают более острым и соленым, чтобы сохранить его в течение длительного времени.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.th:
คนเกาหลีทำ 'กิมจัง' ก่อนฤดูหนาวจะมาถึง ㉠ 'คิมจัง' หมายถึงการทำกิมจิทั้งหมดสำหรับฤดูหนาวในคราวเดียว ㉡ โดยปกติจะทำระหว่างเดือนพฤศจิกายนถึงธันวาคม และรับประทานจนถึงฤดูใบไม้ผลิถัดไป ㉢ การทำกิมจิให้อร่อยนั้นสำคัญมากเพราะเป็นเครื่องเคียงที่ต้องรับประทานได้นานหลายเดือน ㉣ ในพื้นที่ภาคเหนือที่อากาศเย็น กิมจิจะถูกทำให้เผ็ดน้อยลงและเค็มน้อยลง และในพื้นที่ทางใต้ที่อากาศร้อน จะถูกทำให้เผ็ดและเค็มมากขึ้นเพื่อเก็บไว้เป็นเวลานาน

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.pt:
Os coreanos fazem 'kimjang' antes que o inverno frio chegue. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refere-se a fazer todo o kimchi para o inverno de uma vez. ㉡ Geralmente é feito entre novembro e dezembro e consumido até a primavera seguinte. ㉢ É muito importante deixar o kimchi delicioso porque é um acompanhamento que vai durar vários meses. ㉣ Nas regiões do norte, onde o clima é frio, o kimchi é menos picante e menos salgado, e nas regiões do sul, onde o clima é quente, é mais picante e salgado para mantê-lo por muito tempo.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.hi:
कड़ाके की सर्दी आने से पहले कोरियाई लोग 'किमजंग' करते हैं। ㉠ 'किमजंग' का तात्पर्य सर्दियों के लिए एक ही बार में सारी किमची बनाने से है। ㉡ यह आमतौर पर नवंबर और दिसंबर के बीच बनाया जाता है और अगले वसंत तक खाया जाता है। ㉢ किमची को स्वादिष्ट बनाना बहुत ज़रूरी है क्योंकि यह एक साइड डिश है जिसे कई महीनों तक खाया जा सकता है। ㉣ उत्तरी क्षेत्रों में जहां मौसम ठंडा है, किमची को कम मसालेदार और कम नमकीन बनाया जाता है, और दक्षिणी क्षेत्रों में जहां मौसम गर्म है, इसे लंबे समय तक रखने के लिए अधिक मसालेदार और नमकीन बनाया जाता है।

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.tr:
Koreliler soğuk kış gelmeden önce 'kimjang' yaparlar. ㉠ 'Kimjang' kış için tüm kimchi'nin bir kerede yapılmasını ifade eder. ㉡ Genellikle kasım-aralık ayları arasında yapılır ve bir sonraki bahara kadar yenir. ㉢ Kimchi'yi lezzetli hale getirmek çok önemlidir çünkü birkaç ay yenilecek bir garnitürdür. ㉣ Havanın soğuk olduğu kuzey bölgelerde kimchi daha az baharatlı ve daha az tuzlu yapılırken, havanın sıcak olduğu güney bölgelerde ise daha uzun süre saklanabilmesi için daha baharatlı ve tuzlu hale getiriliyor.

#######

Koreans do 'kimjang' before the cold winter arrives. ㉠ 'Kimjang' refers to making all the kimchi for the winter at once. ㉡ It is usually made between November and December and eaten until the following spring. ㉢ It is very important to make kimchi delicious because it is a side dish that will be eaten for several months. ㉣ In the northern regions where the weather is cold, kimchi is made less spicy and less salty, and in the southern regions where the weather is hot, it is made more spicy and salty to keep it for a long time.
g_text_translate.ne: चिसो जाडो आउनुअघि कोरियालीहरूले 'किमजाङ' गर्छन्। ㉠ 'किमजाङ' भन्नाले जाडोको लागि सबै किम्ची एकै पटक बनाउनु हो। ㉡ यो सामान्यतया नोभेम्बर र डिसेम्बर बीच बनाइन्छ र अर्को वसन्त सम्म खाइन्छ। ㉢ किम्चीलाई स्वादिष्ट बनाउन धेरै महत्त्वपूर्ण छ किनभने यो एक साइड डिश हो जुन धेरै महिनासम्म खाइन्छ। ㉣ उत्तरी क्षेत्रमा मौसम चिसो हुने ठाउँमा किम्चीलाई कम मसलादार र कम नुनिलो बनाइन्छ भने दक्षिणी क्षेत्रमा मौसम तातो भएकाले यसलाई लामो समयसम्म राख्नको लागि बढी मसलादार र नुनिलो बनाइन्छ।
g_text_translate.km: ជនជាតិកូរ៉េធ្វើ 'kimjang' មុនពេលរដូវរងាត្រជាក់មកដល់។ ㉠ 'Kimjang' សំដៅលើការធ្វើគីមឈីទាំងអស់សម្រាប់រដូវរងារក្នុងពេលតែមួយ។ ㉡ ជាធម្មតាវាត្រូវបានធ្វើឡើងនៅចន្លោះខែវិច្ឆិកា និងខែធ្នូ ហើយបរិភោគរហូតដល់រដូវផ្ការីកបន្ទាប់។ ㉢ វាមានសារៈសំខាន់ខ្លាំងណាស់ក្នុងការធ្វើឱ្យគីមឈីមានរសជាតិឆ្ងាញ់ព្រោះវាជាម្ហូបចំហៀងដែលនឹងត្រូវញ៉ាំជាច្រើនខែ។ ㉣ នៅតំបន់ភាគខាងជើងដែលមានអាកាសធាតុត្រជាក់ គីមឈីត្រូវបានគេធ្វើមិនសូវហឹរ និងប្រៃតិច ហើយនៅតំបន់ភាគខាងត្បូងដែលមានអាកាសធាតុក្តៅ គីមឈីត្រូវបានធ្វើឱ្យមានរសជាតិហឹរ និងប្រៃជាងមុន ដើម្បីរក្សាវាឱ្យបានយូរ។
g_text_translate.si: සීතල ශීත කාලය පැමිණීමට පෙර කොරියානුවන් 'කිම්ජැං' කරති. ㉠ 'Kimjang' යන්නෙන් අදහස් කරන්නේ ශීත ඍතුව සඳහා සියලුම කිම්චි එකවර සෑදීමයි. ㉡ එය සාමාන්‍යයෙන් නොවැම්බර් සහ දෙසැම්බර් අතර සාදනු ලබන අතර ඊළඟ වසන්තය දක්වා අනුභව කරනු ලැබේ. ㉢ මාස කිහිපයක් ආහාරයට ගන්නා අතුරු කෑමක් නිසා කිම්චි රසවත් කිරීම ඉතා වැදගත් වේ. ㉣ සීතල කාලගුණය පවතින උතුරු ප්‍රදේශ වල කිම්චි කුළුබඩු සහ ලුණු අඩුවෙන් සාදන අතර කාලගුණය උණුසුම් වන දකුණු ප්‍රදේශ වල එය දිගු කාලයක් තබා ගැනීම සඳහා කුළුබඩු සහ ලුණු වැඩි කර ඇත.
g_text_translate.bn: ঠান্ডা শীত আসার আগেই কোরিয়ানরা 'কিমজাং' করে। ㉠ 'কিমজাং' বলতে একযোগে শীতের জন্য সমস্ত কিমচি তৈরি করাকে বোঝায়। ㉡ এটি সাধারণত নভেম্বর থেকে ডিসেম্বরের মধ্যে তৈরি করা হয় এবং পরবর্তী বসন্ত পর্যন্ত খাওয়া হয়। ㉢ কিমচিকে সুস্বাদু করা খুবই গুরুত্বপূর্ণ কারণ এটি একটি সাইড ডিশ যা কয়েক মাস ধরে খাওয়া হবে। ㉣উত্তর অঞ্চলে যেখানে আবহাওয়া ঠান্ডা, সেখানে কিমচি কম মশলাদার এবং কম নোনতা তৈরি করা হয় এবং দক্ষিণ অঞ্চলে যেখানে আবহাওয়া গরম, সেখানে এটি দীর্ঘ সময়ের জন্য রাখার জন্য আরও মশলাদার এবং নোনতা করা হয়।
g_text_translate.my: ချမ်းအေးတဲ့ ဆောင်းရာသီမရောက်ခင်မှာ ကိုရီးယားလူမျိုးတွေက ကင်ဂျန်ကို လုပ်ကြတယ်။ ㉠ 'Kimjang' သည် ဆောင်းရာသီအတွက် ကင်မ်ချီအားလုံးကို တစ်ပြိုင်နက် ပြုလုပ်ခြင်းကို ရည်ညွှန်းသည်။ ㉡ ပုံမှန်အားဖြင့် နိုဝင်ဘာလမှ ဒီဇင်ဘာလအတွင်း ပြုလုပ်ကြပြီး နွေဦးပေါက်သည်အထိ စားသုံးကြသည်။ ㉢ ကင်မ်ချီကို အရသာရှိအောင်ချက်ဖို့က လနဲ့ချီပြီး စားရမယ့် အရံဟင်းဖြစ်လို့ပါ။ ㉣ ရာသီဥတုအေးတဲ့ မြောက်ပိုင်းဒေသတွေမှာ ကင်မ်ချီကို အစပ်နည်းပြီး အငန်နည်းပြီး ရာသီဥတုပူတဲ့ တောင်ပိုင်းဒေသတွေမှာ အစပ်ပိုငန်အောင် လုပ်ထားပါတယ်။

content.q_text:
다음 문장이 들어갈 가장 알맞은 곳을 ㉠~㉣ 중에서 고르십시오.

"그런데 김장 김치는 지역에 따라 만드는 방법이 조금씩 다릅니다."

explain.vi:
Chọn vị trí từ ㉠~㉣ để điền câu sau vào chỗ trống.

"Tuy nhiên, tùy từng vùng mà phương pháp làm kimchi kimjang cũng sẽ khác nhau một chút."

1. ㉠
2. ㉡
3. ㉢
4. ㉣
-------------------- 
조금씩: Từng chút, từng xíu một
김장: Kimjang, việc muối kimchi

N + 에 따라: Theo, theo như, tùy vào.
A + 게: Một cách (Biến tính từ thành trạng từ).

Sau ㉣ là 2 cách làm kim chi ít cay/ít muối hoặc nhiều cay/nhiều muối hơn nên đáp án đúng là 4.
explain.en:
Choose the most appropriate place for the following sentence from <g>㉠</g>to <g>㉣</g>.

"However, the way to make kimchi kimjang differs slightly depending on the region."

1. ㉠
2. ㉡
3. ㉢
4. ㉣
--------------------
<g>조금씩:</g> Gradually, little by little
<g>김장:</g> Kimchi-making for the winter

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> According to.
<g>A + 게:</g> Turn adjectives into adverbs.

Behind the <g>㉣</g> position are two different ways of making kimchi by region, so the correct answer is 4.

explain.vi:
Chọn giải thích đúng về kimchi kimjang.

1. Là kimchi được ăn từ tháng 11~12.
2. Là kimchi được cho nhiều bột ớt.
3. Là kimchi được làm để ăn trong suốt mùa đông.
4. Là kimchi làm vào mùa xuân để ăn quanh năm.
-------------------- 
동안: Trong, trong khi

V + 기 전에: Trước khi.
V + 기 위해(서): Để (làm gì).

Câu 3 là đáp án đúng vì kimchi kimjang là kimchi được làm để ăn đến tận mùa xuân sang năm.
explain.en:
Choose the one that fits the description of kimjang kimchi.

1. It refers to kimchi to be eaten in November-December.
2. It refers to kimchi with a lot of red pepper powder.
3. It refers to kimchi made to eat during the winter.
4. It refers to kimchi made in the spring to be eaten throughout the year.
--------------------
<g>동안:</g> While

<g>V + 기 전에:</g> Before.
<g>V + 기 위해(서):</g> For the sake of, in order to.

3 is the correct answer because kimchi kimjang is kimchi made to be eaten until next spring.
explain.it:

3. Si riferisce al kimchi fatto per mangiare durante l'inverno.
4. Si riferisce al kimchi preparato in primavera da consumare durante tutto l'anno.
--------------------
<g>동안:</g>Mentre

<g>V + 기 전에:</g>Prima delle.
<g>V + 기 위해(서):</g>Per amore di, per.

3 è la risposta corretta perché il kimchi kimjang è il kimchi fatto per essere mangiato fino alla prossima primavera.

explain.km:
ជ្រើសរើសមួយណាដែលសាកសមនឹងការពិពណ៌នារបស់គីមជាំងគីមឈី។

1. វា​សំដៅ​ទៅ​លើ​គីមឈី​ដែល​ត្រូវ​បាន​គេ​បរិភោគ​ក្នុង​ខែ​វិច្ឆិកា​ដល់​ខែ​ធ្នូ​។
2. វាសំដៅទៅលើគីមឈីដែលមានម្សៅម្រេចក្រហមច្រើន។
3. វា​សំដៅ​ទៅ​លើ​គីមឈី​ដែល​ធ្វើ​ឡើង​សម្រាប់​បរិភោគ​ក្នុង​រដូវ​រងា​។
4. វា​សំដៅ​ទៅ​លើ​គីមឈី​ដែល​ធ្វើ​ឡើង​នៅ​រដូវ​ផ្ការីក​ដែល​ត្រូវ​បាន​គេ​បរិភោគ​ពេញ​មួយ​ឆ្នាំ​។
--------------------
<g>동안:</g> ខណៈ

<g>V + 기 전에:</g> មុន
<g>V + 기위해(서):</g> ដើម្បីជាប្រយោជន៍។

3 គឺជាចំលើយដ៏ត្រឹមត្រូវ ព្រោះគីមឈី គីមជាំង គឺជាគីមឈីដែលផលិតសម្រាប់បរិភោគរហូតដល់រដូវផ្ការីកក្រោយ។
explain.si:
kimjang kimchi හි විස්තරයට ගැලපෙන එකක් තෝරන්න.

1. එය නොවැම්බර්-දෙසැම්බර් මාසවල ආහාරයට ගත යුතු කිම්චි ගැන සඳහන් කරයි.
2. එය රතු ගම්මිරිස් කුඩු ගොඩක් සමග කිම්චි ගැන සඳහන් කරයි.
3. එය ශීත ඍතුවේ දී කෑමට සාදන ලද කිම්චි ගැන සඳහන් කරයි.
4. එය වසර පුරා ආහාරයට ගැනීම සඳහා වසන්තයේ සාදන ලද කිම්චි ගැන සඳහන් කරයි.
----------------------
<g>동안:</g> අතර

<g>V + 기 전에:</g> පෙර.
<g>V + 기 위해(서):</g> පිණිස, පිණිස.

3 නිවැරදි පිළිතුර වන්නේ කිම්චි කිම්ජං කිම්චි යනු ලබන වසන්තය දක්වා ආහාරයට ගැනීම සඳහා වන බැවිනි.
explain.bn:
কিমজাং কিমচির বর্ণনার সাথে মানানসই একটি বেছে নিন।

1. এটি নভেম্বর-ডিসেম্বরে খাওয়া কিমচি বোঝায়।
2. এটা অনেক লাল মরিচ গুঁড়ো দিয়ে কিমচি বোঝায়।
3. এটি শীতকালে খাওয়ার জন্য তৈরি কিমচি বোঝায়।
4. এটি সারা বছর খাওয়ার জন্য বসন্তে তৈরি কিমচি বোঝায়।
------------------
<g>동안:</g> যখন

<g>V + 기 전에:</g> আগে।
<g>V + 기 위해(서):</g> খাতিরে, করার জন্য।

3 সঠিক উত্তর কারণ কিমচি কিমজাং কিমচি তৈরি করা হয় যা পরবর্তী বসন্ত পর্যন্ত খেতে হবে।
explain.my:
Kimjang Kimchi ၏ ဖော်ပြချက်နှင့် ကိုက်ညီမည့် တစ်ခုကို ရွေးချယ်ပါ။

1. နိုဝင်ဘာလမှ ဒီဇင်ဘာလတွင် စားရမည့် ကင်မ်ချီကို ရည်ညွှန်းသည်။
2. ငရုတ်ကောင်းမှုန့်များစွာပါသော ကင်မ်ချီကို ရည်ညွှန်းသည်။
၃။ ဆောင်းရာသီမှာ စားဖို့လုပ်ထားတဲ့ ကင်မ်ချီကို ရည်ညွှန်းပါတယ်။
4. ၎င်းသည် တစ်နှစ်ပတ်လုံးစားရန် နွေဦးတွင်ပြုလုပ်သော ကင်မ်ချီကို ရည်ညွှန်းသည်။
--------------------
<g>동안:</g> နေစဉ်

<g>V + 기 전에:</g> အရင်က။
<g>V + 기 위해(서):</g> အလို့ငှာ၊

3 က ကင်မ်ချီကင်ဂျန်ကို လာမယ့်နွေဦးအထိ စားဖို့လုပ်ထားတဲ့ ကင်မ်ချီဖြစ်လို့ အဖြေမှန်ပါ။

general.g_text: 옛날 한국 사람들은 쌀로 만든 밥을 주로 먹었습니다. 그렇지만 생일이나 결혼식 등 잔치가 있을 때 특별한 음식으로 국수를 먹었습니다. 지금은 보통 때에도 여러 가지 국수를 즐겨 먹습니다. 따뜻한 국물과 함께 먹는 온면, 칼국수, 국물 없이 비벼 먹는 비빔국수, 차갑게 먹는 냉면, 콩을 갈아서 만든 콩국수 등 여러 종류의 국수가 많이 있습니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ngày xưa người Hàn Quốc chủ yếu ăn cơm làm từ gạo. Nhưng người Hàn Quốc ăn mì như một món ăn đặc biệt vào những bữa tiệc như kết hôn hay sinh nhật. Bây giờ những lúc bình thường người Hàn cũng thường thức nhiều loại mì. Có nhiều loại mì như mì nóng ăn với nước dùng ấm, mì làm thủ công, mì trộn ăn không kèm nước dùng, mì lạnh ăn lạnh, mì đậu nành làm từ đậu xay.
g_text_translate.en: In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.it:
Ai vecchi tempi, i coreani mangiavano principalmente riso a base di riso. Tuttavia, i noodles venivano consumati come cibo speciale in occasione di feste come compleanni e matrimoni. Ora, anche nei giorni normali, si divertono a mangiare una varietà di noodles. Esistono molti tipi di tagliatelle, come tagliatelle calde con zuppa calda, tagliatelle tritate, tagliatelle miste senza zuppa, tagliatelle fredde servite fredde e tagliatelle di fagioli fatte con fagioli macinati.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.fr:
Autrefois, les Coréens mangeaient principalement du riz à base de riz. Cependant, les nouilles étaient consommées comme aliment spécial lors de fêtes telles que les anniversaires et les mariages. Maintenant, même les jours normaux, ils aiment manger une variété de nouilles. Il existe de nombreux types de nouilles, telles que les nouilles chaudes avec une soupe chaude, les nouilles hachées, les nouilles mélangées sans soupe, les nouilles froides servies froides et les nouilles aux haricots à base de haricots moulus.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.es:
En los viejos tiempos, los coreanos comían principalmente arroz hecho de arroz. Sin embargo, los fideos se comían como comida especial en fiestas como cumpleaños y bodas. Ahora, incluso en días normales, disfrutan comiendo una variedad de fideos. Hay muchos tipos de fideos, como fideos calientes con sopa tibia, fideos picados, fideos mixtos sin sopa, fideos fríos servidos fríos y fideos con frijoles hechos con frijoles molidos.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.id:
Dulu, orang Korea kebanyakan makan nasi yang terbuat dari beras. Namun, mie dimakan sebagai makanan khusus di pesta-pesta seperti ulang tahun dan pernikahan. Kini, di hari-hari biasa pun, mereka menikmati makan aneka mi. Ada banyak jenis mie, seperti mie panas dengan kuah hangat, mie cincang, mie campur tanpa kuah, mie dingin disajikan dingin, dan mie kacang yang dibuat dengan kacang tanah.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.ja:
昔、韓国人は主に米から作った米を食べていました。しかし、誕生日や結婚式などのパーティーでは特別な食べ物として麺類が食べられていました。今では、平日でもいろいろな麺を食べて楽しんでいます。温かいスープの温かい麺、細切り麺、汁なしのまぜ麺、冷やして食べる冷やし麺、豆をすりつぶした豆麺など、麺の種類も豊富です。

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.de:
Früher aßen die Koreaner hauptsächlich Reis aus Reis. Nudeln wurden jedoch als besonderes Essen auf Partys wie Geburtstagen und Hochzeiten gegessen. Jetzt essen sie auch an normalen Tagen gerne verschiedene Nudeln. Es gibt viele Arten von Nudeln, wie z. B. heiße Nudeln mit warmer Suppe, gehackte Nudeln, gemischte Nudeln ohne Suppe, kalte Nudeln, die kalt serviert werden, und Bohnennudeln aus gemahlenen Bohnen.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
过去，韩国人主要吃用大米制成的米饭。然而，在生日和婚礼等聚会上，面条作为一种特殊食品被食用。现在，即使在平常的日子里，他们也喜欢吃各种各样的面条。面的种类有很多种，有热汤面、剁碎面、无汤拌面、冷面、豆角面等。

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
過去，韓國人主要吃用大米製成的米飯。然而，在生日和婚禮等聚會上，麵條作為一種特殊食品被食用。現在，即使在平常的日子裡，他們也喜歡吃各種各樣的麵條。面的種類有很多種，有熱湯麵、剁碎面、無湯拌麵、冷面、豆角面等。

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.ru:
В старину корейцы в основном ели рис, приготовленный из риса. Однако лапшу ели как особую еду на таких вечеринках, как дни рождения и свадьбы. Теперь даже в обычные дни они с удовольствием едят разнообразную лапшу. Существует много видов лапши, например, горячая лапша с теплым супом, нарезанная лапша, смешанная лапша без супа, холодная лапша, подаваемая в холодном виде, и фасолевая лапша, приготовленная из молотых бобов.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.th:
ในสมัยก่อนคนเกาหลีกินข้าวที่ทำจากข้าวเป็นหลัก อย่างไรก็ตาม บะหมี่ถูกรับประทานเป็นอาหารพิเศษในงานปาร์ตี้ เช่น วันเกิดและงานแต่งงาน ปัจจุบันนี้แม้ในวันปกติ พวกเขาก็ยังสนุกกับการกินบะหมี่หลากหลายชนิด บะหมี่มีหลายประเภท เช่น บะหมี่ร้อนพร้อมซุปอุ่น บะหมี่สับ บะหมี่รวมแบบไม่ใส่ซุป บะหมี่เย็นเสิร์ฟเย็น และบะหมี่ถั่วที่ทำจากถั่วบด

#######

g_text_translate.pt:
Antigamente, os coreanos comiam principalmente arroz feito de arroz. No entanto, o macarrão era consumido como alimento especial em festas como aniversários e casamentos. Agora, mesmo em dias normais, eles gostam de comer uma variedade de macarrão. Existem muitos tipos de macarrão, como macarrão quente com sopa quente, macarrão picado, macarrão misto sem sopa, macarrão frio servido frio e macarrão de feijão feito com feijão moído.

#######

Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.hi:
पुराने दिनों में, कोरियाई लोग मुख्य रूप से चावल से बने चावल खाते थे। हालाँकि, नूडल्स को जन्मदिन और शादियों जैसी पार्टियों में एक विशेष भोजन के रूप में खाया जाता था। अब आम दिनों में भी वे तरह-तरह के नूडल्स खाने का आनंद लेते हैं। नूडल्स कई प्रकार के होते हैं, जैसे गर्म सूप के साथ गर्म नूडल्स, कटे हुए नूडल्स, बिना सूप के मिश्रित नूडल्स, ठंडे परोसे जाने वाले ठंडे नूडल्स और पिसी हुई फलियों से बने बीन नूडल्स।

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.tr:
Eski günlerde Koreliler çoğunlukla pirinçten yapılan pilavı yerlerdi. Ancak erişte doğum günü, düğün gibi partilerde özel bir yiyecek olarak yenirdi. Artık normal günlerde bile çeşitli erişteleri yemekten keyif alıyorlar. Sıcak çorbalı sıcak erişte, doğranmış erişte, çorbasız karışık erişte, soğuk servis edilen soğuk erişte ve öğütülmüş fasulyeden yapılan fasulye eriştesi gibi birçok erişte türü vardır.

#######

In the old days, Koreans mainly ate rice made from rice. However, noodles were eaten as a special food at parties such as birthdays and weddings. Now, even on normal days, they enjoy eating a variety of noodles. There are many types of noodles, such as hot noodles with warm soup, chopped noodles, mixed noodles without soup, cold noodles served cold, and bean noodles made with ground beans.
g_text_translate.ne: पुरानो दिनहरूमा, कोरियनहरू मुख्यतया चामलबाट बनेको चामल खान्थे। यद्यपि, चाउचाउलाई जन्मदिन र विवाह जस्ता पार्टीहरूमा विशेष खानाको रूपमा खाइन्छ। अहिले सामान्य दिनमा पनि विभिन्न किसिमका चाउचाउ खान रमाइलो गर्छन् । त्यहाँ धेरै प्रकारका चाउचाउहरू छन्, जस्तै तातो सूपको साथ तातो चाउचाउ, काटिएको चाउचाउ, सूप बिना मिश्रित चाउचाउ, चिसो सर्भ गरिएको चिसो चाउचाउ, र भुइँको सिमीले बनाइएको बीन चाउचाउ।
g_text_translate.km: នៅសម័យបុរាណ ប្រជាជនកូរ៉េភាគច្រើនបរិភោគអង្ករដែលធ្វើពីអង្ករ។ ទោះជាយ៉ាងណាក៏ដោយ គុយទាវត្រូវបានបរិភោគជាអាហារពិសេសក្នុងពិធីជប់លៀង ដូចជាថ្ងៃខួបកំណើត និងពិធីមង្គលការជាដើម។ ឥឡូវនេះ សូម្បីតែថ្ងៃធម្មតាក៏ពួកគេចូលចិត្តញ៉ាំគុយទាវជាច្រើនប្រភេទ។ គុយទាវមានច្រើនប្រភេទ ដូចជា គុយទាវក្តៅ ស៊ុបក្តៅ មីឆា មីលាយគ្មានស៊ុប មីត្រជាក់បម្រើត្រជាក់ និងគុយទាវធ្វើជាមួយសណ្ដែកដី។
g_text_translate.si: පැරණි දිනවල කොරියානුවන් ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් අනුභව කළේ සහල් වලින් සාදන ලද සහල් ය. කෙසේ වෙතත් උපන් දින මංගල්‍ය වැනි සාදවලදී විශේෂ ආහාරයක් ලෙස නූඩ්ල්ස් ආහාරයට ගත්තා. දැන් සාමාන්‍ය දිනවල පවා නොයෙකුත් නූඩ්ල්ස් කෑමට ඔවුහු ප්‍රිය කරති. උණුසුම් සුප් සමඟ උණුසුම් නූඩ්ල්ස්, කැඩුණු නූඩ්ල්ස්, සුප් නොමැතිව මිශ්‍ර නූඩ්ල්ස්, සීතල සේවය කරන සීතල නූඩ්ල්ස් සහ බිම් බෝංචි වලින් සාදන ලද බෝංචි නූඩ්ල්ස් වැනි නූඩ්ල්ස් වර්ග බොහොමයක් තිබේ.
g_text_translate.bn: পুরানো দিনে, কোরিয়ানরা প্রধানত চাল দিয়ে তৈরি চাল খেত। যাইহোক, জন্মদিন এবং বিবাহের মতো পার্টিতে নুডুলস একটি বিশেষ খাবার হিসাবে খাওয়া হত। এখন সাধারণ দিনেও তারা বিভিন্ন ধরনের নুডুলস খেয়ে আনন্দ পায়। অনেক ধরনের নুডলস আছে, যেমন গরম স্যুপের সাথে গরম নুডলস, কাটা নুডুলস, স্যুপ ছাড়া মিশ্রিত নুডুলস, ঠান্ডা পরিবেশন করা ঠান্ডা নুডুলস এবং মটরশুটি দিয়ে তৈরি বিন নুডলস।

content.q_text: 잘못 설명한 것을 고르십시오.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án có phần giải thích sai.

1. Mì đậu nành được làm bằng cách xay gạo.
2. Mì lạnh được dùng với nước dùng lạnh.
3. Mì nóng được dùng với nước dùng ấm.
4. Mì trộn ăn không kèm nước dùng.
-------------------- 
국물: Nước canh, nước dùng
갈다: Xay
콩: Đỗ, đậu

A + (으)ㄴ + N: Định ngữ của tính từ.

Đáp án 1 là đáp án đúng vì mì đậu nành được làm từ đậu xay, không phải gạo.
explain.en:
Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.
4. Mix noodles are eaten without soup.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> Broth, soup
<g>갈다:</g> To grind
<g>콩:</g> Bean, soy

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

1 is the correct answer because bean noodles are made from ground beans, not rice.
explain.it:
Scegli quello che è descritto in modo errato.

1. I noodles di fagioli sono fatti macinando il riso.
2. I noodles freddi vengono serviti con zuppa fredda.
3. I noodles caldi vengono con una zuppa calda.
4. I noodles misti vengono consumati senza zuppa.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g>Brodo, zuppa
<g>갈다:</g>Per macinare
<g>콩:</g>Fagiolo, soia

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g>Modificatore sostantivo (per aggettivi).

1 è la risposta corretta perché i noodles di fagioli sono fatti con fagioli macinati, non con riso.

#######

Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.
4. Mix noodles are eaten without soup.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> Broth, soup
<g>갈다:</g> To grind
<g>콩:</g> Bean, soy

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

1 is the correct answer because bean noodles are made from ground beans, not rice.
explain.fr:
Choisissez celui dont la description est incorrecte.

1. Les nouilles aux haricots sont faites en broyant du riz.
2. Les nouilles froides sont servies avec une soupe froide.
3. Les nouilles chaudes sont accompagnées d'une soupe chaude.
4. Les nouilles mélangées se mangent sans soupe.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g>Bouillon, soupe
<g>갈다:</g>Broyer
<g>콩:</g>Haricot, soja

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g>Modificateur de nom (pour les adjectifs).

1 est la bonne réponse car les nouilles aux haricots sont faites de haricots moulus et non de riz.

#######

Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.
4. Mix noodles are eaten without soup.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> Broth, soup
<g>갈다:</g> To grind
<g>콩:</g> Bean, soy

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

1 is the correct answer because bean noodles are made from ground beans, not rice.
explain.es:
Elija el que se describe incorrectamente.

1. Los fideos de frijoles se hacen moliendo arroz.
2. Los fideos fríos se sirven con sopa fría.
3. Los fideos tibios vienen con sopa tibia.
4. Los fideos mixtos se comen sin sopa.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g>caldo, sopa
<g>갈다:</g>Para moler
<g>콩:</g>frijol, soya

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g>Modificador de sustantivo (para adjetivos).

1 es la respuesta correcta porque los fideos con frijoles están hechos de frijoles molidos, no de arroz.

#######

Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.
4. Mix noodles are eaten without soup.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> Broth, soup
<g>갈다:</g> To grind
<g>콩:</g> Bean, soy

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

1 is the correct answer because bean noodles are made from ground beans, not rice.
explain.id:
Pilih salah satu yang salah dijelaskan.

1. Mi kacang dibuat dengan cara menggiling beras.
2. Mie dingin disajikan dengan kuah dingin.
3. Mie hangat datang dengan kuah hangat.
4. Mi campur dimakan tanpa kuah.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g>Kaldu, sup
<g>갈다:</g>Untuk menggiling
<g>콩:</g>Kacang, kedelai

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g>Pengubah kata benda (untuk kata sifat).

1 adalah jawaban yang benar karena mie kacang terbuat dari kacang tanah, bukan beras.

#######

Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.
4. Mix noodles are eaten without soup.
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> Broth, soup
<g>갈다:</g> To grind
<g>콩:</g> Bean, soy

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

1 is the correct answer because bean noodles are made from ground beans, not rice.
explain.ja:
誤って記述されているものを選択してください。

1. 豆麺は米をすりつぶして作ります。
2. 冷麺は冷製スープ付き。
3.温かい麺に温かいスープが付いてきます。
4.ミックス麺は汁なしで食べます。
--------------------
<g>국물:</g>出汁、スープ
<g>갈다:</g>挽く
<g>콩:</g>豆、大豆

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g>名詞修飾子 (形容詞用)。

豆麺は米ではなく挽いた豆で作るので1が正解です。

#######

Choose the one that is incorrectly described.

1. Bean noodles are made by grinding rice.
2. Cold noodles are served with cold soup.
3. Warm noodles come with warm soup.

explain.km:
ជ្រើសរើសមួយដែលត្រូវបានពិពណ៌នាមិនត្រឹមត្រូវ។

1. គុយទាវធ្វើដោយកិនស្រូវ។
2. គុយទាវត្រជាក់ត្រូវបានបម្រើជាមួយស៊ុបត្រជាក់។
3. គុយទាវមកជាមួយស៊ុបក្តៅៗ។
4. គុយទាវ​លាយ​ត្រូវ​បរិភោគ​ដោយ​មិន​មាន​ស៊ុប។
--------------------
<g>국물:</g> ទំពាំងបាយជូរ ស៊ុប
<g>갈다៖</g> ដើម្បីកិន
<g>콩:</g> សណ្តែកសៀង

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (សម្រាប់ adjective)។

1 គឺជាចម្លើយដែលត្រឹមត្រូវ ព្រោះគុយទាវធ្វើពីសណ្តែកដី មិនមែនអង្ករទេ។
explain.si:
වැරදි ලෙස විස්තර කර ඇති එකක් තෝරන්න.

1. බීන් නූඩ්ල්ස් හදන්නේ සහල් අඹරලා.
2. සීතල සුප් සමග සීතල නූඩ්ල්ස් පිරිනමනු ලැබේ.
3. උණුසුම් නූඩ්ල්ස් උණුසුම් සුප් සමග පැමිණේ.
4. මිශ්ර නූඩ්ල්ස් සුප් නොමැතිව අනුභව කරනු ලැබේ.
----------------------
<g>국물:</g> සුප් හොද්ද, සුප්
<g>갈다:</g> ඇඹරීමට
<g>콩:</g> බෝංචි, සෝයා

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> නාම පද වෙනස් කරන්නා (විශේෂණ සඳහා).

1 නිවැරදි පිළිතුර වන්නේ බෝංචි නූඩ්ල්ස් සහල් නොව බිම බෝංචි වලින් සාදන බැවිනි.
explain.bn:
ভুল বর্ণনা করা হয় যে এক চয়ন করুন.

1. শিম নুডুলস চাল পিষে তৈরি করা হয়।
2. ঠান্ডা নুডলস ঠান্ডা স্যুপের সাথে পরিবেশন করা হয়।
3. উষ্ণ নুডলস উষ্ণ স্যুপ সঙ্গে আসা.
4. মিক্স নুডলস স্যুপ ছাড়া খাওয়া হয়।
-----------------
<g>국물:</g> ঝোল, স্যুপ
<g>갈다:</g> পিষতে
<g>콩:</g> বিন, সয়া

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> বিশেষ্য পরিবর্তনকারী (বিশেষণগুলির জন্য)।

1 সঠিক উত্তর কারণ বিন নুডুলস চাল নয়, মাটির মটরশুটি থেকে তৈরি করা হয়।

general.g_text: 한국에서는 집 안에 들어갈 때 신발을 신고 들어가면 안 돼요. 현관에서 신발을 벗고 거실로 들어가야 합니다. 그리고 빨간색 펜으로 살아있는 사람의 이름을 쓰면 안 돼요. 빨간색은 죽은 사람의 이름을 쓸 때 사용하기 때문이지요. 그리고 한국에는 나이 많은 사람을 높게 대접하는 문화가 있습니다. 그래서 지하철이나 버스에서 노인에게 자리를 양보하고, 식탁에서도 나이가 많은 사람이 먼저 식사를 시작한 후에 젊은 사람이 먹습니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ở Hàn Quốc, không được đi giày vào nhà. Phải cởi giày ở ngoài rồi mới đi vào phòng khách. Và không được viết tên của người vẫn còn sống bằng bút đỏ. Vì bút đỏ được dùng để viết tên cho người chết. Và ở Hàn Quốc có văn hóa đối đãi cao với người lớn tuổi. Vì vậy người Hàn Quốc nhường ghế cho người già trên xe bus hay tàu điện ngầm và ở trên bàn ăn, người lớn ăn trước rồi người trẻ mới được ăn.
g_text_translate.en: In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.it:
In Corea non puoi indossare le scarpe quando entri in una casa. Devi toglierti le scarpe all'ingresso ed entrare nel soggiorno. E non scrivere il nome di una persona viva con una penna rossa. Perché il rosso è usato per scrivere i nomi dei morti. E in Corea c'è una cultura che tratta molto bene le persone anziane. Ecco perché la gente cede il posto agli anziani in metropolitana o in autobus, ea tavola mangia prima l'anziano e poi il più giovane.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.fr:
En Corée, vous ne pouvez pas porter de chaussures en entrant dans une maison. Vous devez enlever vos chaussures à l'entrée et entrer dans le salon. Et n'écrivez pas le nom d'une personne vivante avec un stylo rouge. Parce que le rouge sert à écrire les noms des morts. Et en Corée, il y a une culture qui traite hautement les personnes âgées. C'est pourquoi les gens cèdent leur place aux personnes âgées dans le métro ou le bus, et à table, la personne âgée mange en premier et ensuite la personne plus jeune.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.es:
En Corea, no puedes usar zapatos al entrar a una casa. Debes descalzarte en la entrada y entrar al salón. Y no escribas el nombre de una persona viva con un bolígrafo rojo. Porque el rojo se usa para escribir los nombres de los muertos. Y en Corea, hay una cultura que trata muy bien a las personas mayores. Por eso la gente cede sus asientos a los mayores en el metro o en el autobús, y en la mesa primero come el mayor y luego come el más joven.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.id:
Di Korea, Anda tidak bisa memakai sepatu saat memasuki rumah. Anda harus melepas sepatu Anda di pintu masuk dan memasuki ruang tamu. Dan jangan menulis nama orang yang masih hidup dengan pena merah. Karena warna merah digunakan untuk menuliskan nama orang yang meninggal. Dan di Korea, ada budaya yang sangat memperlakukan orang tua. Itulah mengapa orang-orang menyerahkan tempat duduknya kepada orang tua di kereta bawah tanah atau bus, dan di meja, orang yang lebih tua makan terlebih dahulu, kemudian orang yang lebih muda makan.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.ja:
韓国では、家に入るときに靴を履いてはいけません。玄関で靴を脱いでリビングに入る必要があります。そして生きている人の名前を赤ペンで書かないでください。赤は死者の名前を書くために使われるからです。そして韓国には、年上の人を大切にする文化があります。そのため、地下鉄やバスでお年寄りに席を譲り、テーブルではお年寄りが先に食べ、次に若い人が食べます。

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.de:
In Korea dürfen Sie beim Betreten eines Hauses keine Schuhe tragen. Sie müssen Ihre Schuhe am Eingang ausziehen und das Wohnzimmer betreten. Und schreiben Sie nicht den Namen einer lebenden Person mit einem roten Stift. Weil Rot verwendet wird, um die Namen der Toten zu schreiben. Und in Korea gibt es eine Kultur, die ältere Menschen hoch behandelt. Deshalb geben die Leute in der U-Bahn oder im Bus ihren Platz für ältere Menschen ab, und am Tisch isst zuerst der Ältere und dann der Jüngere.

g_text_translate.th:
ในเกาหลี คุณไม่สามารถสวมรองเท้าเมื่อเข้าบ้าน คุณต้องถอดรองเท้าที่ทางเข้าแล้วเข้าไปในห้องนั่งเล่น และอย่าเขียนชื่อคนมีชีวิตด้วยปากกาสีแดง เพราะสีแดงใช้เขียนชื่อผู้ตาย และในเกาหลีก็มีวัฒนธรรมที่ปฏิบัติต่อผู้สูงอายุอย่างสูง นั่นเป็นสาเหตุที่ผู้คนยอมสละที่นั่งให้กับผู้สูงอายุบนรถไฟใต้ดินหรือรถบัส และที่โต๊ะ ผู้สูงอายุจะกินก่อน จากนั้นผู้เยาว์จะกิน

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. g_text_translate.pt:
Na Coreia, você não pode usar sapatos ao entrar em uma casa. Você deve tirar os sapatos na entrada e entrar na sala. E não escreva o nome de uma pessoa viva com caneta vermelha. Porque o vermelho é usado para escrever os nomes dos mortos. E na Coreia, existe uma cultura que trata bem os idosos. É por isso que as pessoas cedem seus lugares aos idosos no metrô ou no ônibus e, à mesa, o mais velho come primeiro e depois o mais jovem come.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.hi:
कोरिया में आप घर में प्रवेश करते समय जूते नहीं पहन सकते। आपको प्रवेश द्वार पर अपने जूते उतारने होंगे और लिविंग रूम में प्रवेश करना होगा। और किसी जीवित व्यक्ति का नाम लाल पेन से ना लिखें. क्योंकि लाल रंग का प्रयोग मृतकों के नाम लिखने के लिए किया जाता है। और कोरिया में, एक ऐसी संस्कृति है जो वृद्ध लोगों के साथ बहुत अच्छा व्यवहार करती है। यही कारण है कि लोग मेट्रो या बस में बुजुर्गों के लिए अपनी सीटें छोड़ देते हैं और मेज पर पहले बड़ा व्यक्ति खाता है और फिर छोटा व्यक्ति खाता है।

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kore'de bir eve girerken ayakkabı giyemezsiniz. Girişte ayakkabılarınızı çıkarıp oturma odasına girmelisiniz. Ve yaşayan bir insanın adını kırmızı kalemle yazmayın. Çünkü kırmızı ölenlerin isimlerini yazmak için kullanılıyor. Kore'de yaşlılara çok iyi davranan bir kültür var. Bu yüzden metro ve otobüslerde insanlar yerlerini yaşlılara bırakıyor ve masada önce yaşlı olan, sonra genç olan yemek yiyor.

#######

In Korea, you cannot wear shoes when entering a house. You must take off your shoes at the entrance and enter the living room. And don't write the name of a living person with a red pen. Because red is used to write the names of the dead. And in Korea, there is a culture that treats older people highly. That's why people give up their seats to the elderly on the subway or bus, and at the table, the older person eats first and then the younger person eats.
g_text_translate.ne: कोरियामा घरभित्र पस्दा जुत्ता लगाउन मिल्दैन । तपाईंले प्रवेशद्वारमा आफ्नो जुत्ता फुकाल्नु पर्छ र बैठक कोठामा प्रवेश गर्नुपर्छ। अनि जिउँदो मान्छेको नाम रातो कलमले नलेख्नुहोस्। किनभने मृतकको नाम लेख्न रातो रङ प्रयोग गरिन्छ। र कोरियामा, त्यहाँ एक संस्कृति छ जसले वृद्धहरूलाई उच्च व्यवहार गर्दछ। त्यसैले मानिसहरूले सबवे वा बसमा बुढापाकाहरूलाई आफ्नो सिट छोड्छन्, र टेबुलमा, वृद्ध व्यक्तिले पहिले खान्छ र त्यसपछि कान्छो व्यक्तिले खान्छ।
g_text_translate.km: នៅប្រទេសកូរ៉េ អ្នកមិនអាចពាក់ស្បែកជើងពេលចូលផ្ទះបានទេ។ អ្នកត្រូវតែដោះស្បែកជើងរបស់អ្នកនៅច្រកចូល ហើយចូលទៅក្នុងបន្ទប់ទទួលភ្ញៀវ។ ហើយ​កុំ​សរសេរ​ឈ្មោះ​មនុស្ស​រស់​ដោយ​ប៊ិច​ក្រហម។ ព្រោះ​ពណ៌​ក្រហម​ប្រើ​សម្រាប់​សរសេរ​ឈ្មោះ​អ្នក​ស្លាប់។ ហើយ​នៅ​កូរ៉េ​មាន​វប្បធម៌​មួយ​ដែល​ប្រព្រឹត្ត​ចំពោះ​មនុស្ស​ចាស់​យ៉ាង​ខ្លាំង។ នោះហើយជាមូលហេតុដែលមនុស្សបោះបង់ចោលកៅអីរបស់ពួកគេដល់មនុស្សចាស់នៅលើរថភ្លើងក្រោមដីឬឡានក្រុងហើយនៅតុមនុស្សចាស់ញ៉ាំមុនហើយបន្ទាប់មកមនុស្សវ័យក្មេងញ៉ាំ។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියාවේ නිවසකට ඇතුළු වන විට පාවහන් පැළඳිය නොහැක. ඔබ ඇතුල් වන ස්ථානයේ සපත්තු ගලවා විසිත්ත කාමරයට ඇතුළු විය යුතුය. ඒවගේම රතු පෑනකින් ජීවත් වෙන කෙනෙක්ගෙ නම ලියන්න එපා. මක්නිසාද යත් මියගිය අයගේ නම් ලිවීමට රතු පැහැය භාවිතා කරන බැවිනි. ඒවගේම කොරියාවේ වැඩිහිටියන්ට ඉහළින් සලකන සංස්කෘතියක් තියෙනවා. උමං මාර්ගයේ හෝ බස් රථයේ වැඩිහිටියන්ට මිනිසුන් තම ආසනය අතහැර දමා මේසයේ සිටින විට, වැඩිහිටි පුද්ගලයා පළමුව ආහාර ගන්නා අතර පසුව බාලයා අනුභව කරයි.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়াতে, আপনি একটি বাড়িতে প্রবেশ করার সময় জুতা পরতে পারবেন না। প্রবেশদ্বারে আপনার জুতা খুলে বসার ঘরে প্রবেশ করতে হবে। আর লাল কলম দিয়ে জীবিত ব্যক্তির নাম লিখবেন না। কারণ মৃতদের নাম লিখতে লাল ব্যবহার করা হয়। এবং কোরিয়াতে, এমন একটি সংস্কৃতি রয়েছে যা বয়স্কদের সাথে খুব বেশি আচরণ করে। এই কারণেই লোকেরা পাতাল রেল বা বাসে বয়স্কদের কাছে তাদের আসন ছেড়ে দেয় এবং টেবিলে, বয়স্ক ব্যক্তি প্রথমে খায় এবং তারপরে ছোট ব্যক্তি খায়।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားမှာ အိမ်ထဲဝင်ရင် ဖိနပ်မစီးရဘူး။ ဝင်ပေါက်မှာ ဖိနပ်ချွတ်ပြီး ဧည့်ခန်းထဲဝင်ရမယ်။ အနီရောင် ဘောပင်ဖြင့် အသက်ရှင်သူ၏ အမည်ကို မရေးပါနှင့်။ အဘယ်ကြောင့်ဆိုသော် အနီရောင်သည် သေဆုံးသူများ၏ အမည်များကို ရေးသားရန် အသုံးပြုသောကြောင့် ဖြစ်သည်။ ကိုရီးယားမှာ သက်ကြီးရွယ်အိုတွေကို လွန်စွာဆက်ဆံတဲ့ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုတစ်ခုရှိပါတယ်။ အဲဒါကြောင့် လူတွေက မြေအောက်ရထား ဒါမှမဟုတ် ဘတ်စ်ကားပေါ်မှာ သက်ကြီးရွယ်အိုတွေကို ထိုင်ခုံတွေပေးပြီး စားပွဲမှာ အသက်ကြီးသူက အရင်စားပြီးမှ အငယ်က စားတယ်။

1. Ria: Escrevi o nome do meu amigo com uma caneta vermelha.
2. Bambang: Calcei os sapatos e entrei no quarto do meu amigo.
3. Ricky: Cedi meu lugar para uma senhora idosa no metrô.
4. Wangli: Eu estava com fome e comecei a comer antes da minha avó.
--------------------
<g>양보하다:</g>Oferecer
<g>신다:</g>Para vestir, vestir

#######

Who followed this article well?

1. Ria: I wrote my friend's name with a red pen.
2. Bambang: I put on my shoes and went into my friend's room.
3. Ricky: I gave up my seat to an old woman on the subway.
4. Wangli: I was hungry and started eating before my grandmother.
--------------------
<g>양보하다:</g> To offer
<g>신다:</g> To wear, put on
explain.hi:
इस लेख का अच्छे से पालन किसने किया?

1. रिया: मैंने अपने दोस्त का नाम लाल पेन से लिखा।
2. बंबांग: मैंने अपने जूते पहने और अपने दोस्त के कमरे में चला गया।
3. रिकी: मैंने मेट्रो में अपनी सीट एक बूढ़ी औरत को दे दी।
4. वांगली: मुझे भूख लगी थी और मैंने अपनी दादी से पहले खाना शुरू कर दिया।
------------------------
<g>양보하다:</g>प्रस्ताव देना
<g>신다:</g>पहनना, पहनना

#######

Who followed this article well?

1. Ria: I wrote my friend's name with a red pen.
2. Bambang: I put on my shoes and went into my friend's room.
3. Ricky: I gave up my seat to an old woman on the subway.
4. Wangli: I was hungry and started eating before my grandmother.
--------------------
<g>양보하다:</g> To offer
<g>신다:</g> To wear, put on
explain.tr:
Bu makaleyi kim iyi takip etti?

1. Ria: Arkadaşımın adını kırmızı kalemle yazdım.
2. Bambang: Ayakkabılarımı giydim ve arkadaşımın odasına girdim.
3. Ricky: Metroda koltuğumu yaşlı bir kadına verdim.
4. Wangli: Acıktım ve büyükannemden önce yemek yemeye başladım.
--------------------
<g>양보하다:</g>Teklif etmek
<g>신다:</g>Giymek, giymek

explain.ne:
यो लेख कसले राम्रोसँग पछ्यायो?

१. रिया: मैले मेरो साथीको नाम रातो कलमले लेखेँ।
२. बम्बाङ: मैले जुत्ता लगाएर साथीको कोठामा गएँ।
3. रिकी: मैले सबवेमा एक वृद्ध महिलालाई मेरो सिट छोडें।
4. वाङ्ली: मलाई भोक लाग्यो र हजुरआमाको अगाडि खाना खान थाले।
----------------------------------
<g>양보하다:</g> प्रस्ताव गर्न
<g>신다:</g> लगाउन, लगाउनुहोस्
explain.km:
តើអ្នកណាតាមដានអត្ថបទនេះបានល្អ?

១.រីយ៉ា៖ ខ្ញុំសរសេរឈ្មោះមិត្តរបស់ខ្ញុំដោយប្រើប៊ិចក្រហម។
2. Bambang: ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ពាក់​ស្បែក​ជើង​របស់​ខ្ញុំ​ហើយ​ចូល​ទៅ​ក្នុង​បន្ទប់​មិត្ត​ភក្តិ​របស់​ខ្ញុំ​។
3. Ricky: ខ្ញុំបានលះបង់កៅអីរបស់ខ្ញុំទៅស្ត្រីចំណាស់ម្នាក់នៅលើរថភ្លើងក្រោមដី។
4. Wangli: ខ្ញុំឃ្លាន ហើយចាប់ផ្តើមញ៉ាំមុនពេលជីដូនរបស់ខ្ញុំ។
--------------------
<g>양보하다៖</g> ដើម្បីផ្តល់ជូន
<g>신다:</g> ដើម្បីពាក់ សូមពាក់
explain.si:
මෙම ලිපිය හොඳින් අනුගමනය කළේ කවුද?

1. රියා: මම මගේ මිතුරාගේ නම රතු පෑනකින් ලිව්වා.
2. බම්බන්ග්: මම මගේ සපත්තු දාගෙන මගේ මිතුරාගේ කාමරයට ගියා.
3. රිකී: මම උමං මාර්ගයේ මහලු කාන්තාවකට මගේ ආසනය ලබා දුන්නා.
4. වැන්ග්ලි: මම බඩගිනි වෙලා ආච්චිට කලින් කන්න පටන් ගත්තා.
----------------------
<g>양보하다:</g> පිරිනැමීමට
<g>신다:</g> ඇඳීමට, අඳින්න
explain.bn:
যারা এই নিবন্ধটি ভাল অনুসরণ করেছেন?

রিয়া: আমি লাল কলম দিয়ে আমার বন্ধুর নাম লিখেছি।
2. বামবাং: আমি আমার জুতা পরে আমার বন্ধুর ঘরে গিয়েছিলাম।
3. রিকি: আমি পাতাল রেলে একজন বৃদ্ধ মহিলার কাছে আমার আসন ছেড়ে দিয়েছি।
4. ওয়াংলি: আমি ক্ষুধার্ত ছিলাম এবং আমার দাদীর আগে খেতে শুরু করেছি।
-----------------
<g>양보하다:</g> অফার করতে
<g>신다:</g> পরতে, পরুন
explain.my:
ဒီဆောင်းပါးကို ဘယ်သူတွေ ကောင်းကောင်း လိုက်နာခဲ့လဲ။

1. Ria: ငါ့သူငယ်ချင်း နာမည်ကို အနီရောင် ဘောပင်နဲ့ ရေးခဲ့တယ်။
2. Bambang - ဖိနပ်ကိုစီးပြီး သူငယ်ချင်းရဲ့အခန်းထဲကို ၀င်သွားတယ်။
3. Ricky- မြေအောက်ရထားပေါ်မှာ အဘွားကြီးတစ်ယောက်အတွက် ကျွန်တော့်ထိုင်ခုံကို စွန့်လိုက်တယ်။
4. Wangli- ငါဗိုက်ဆာပြီး ငါ့အဖွားရှေ့မှာ ထမင်းစားတယ်။
--------------------
<g>양보하다:</g> ကမ်းလှမ်းရန်
<g>신다:</g> ဝတ်ရန်၊ ဝတ်ပါ။

general.g_text: 한국에는 ‘무궁화호’, ‘새마을호’, ‘케이티엑스(KTX)’라고 하는 기차가 있습니다. 이런 기차들을 타면 한국의 여러 도시나 지방에 갈 수 있습니다. 이 중에서 제일 빠른 기차는 케이티엑스입니다. 시속 200~300km로 달립니다. 무궁화호나 새마을호를 타면 서울에서 부산까지 4~5시간 걸리지만 케이티엑스는 2시간 40분밖에 걸리지 않습니다. 그렇지만 케이티엑스 역은 주로 큰 도시에만 있고 요금도 비쌉니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ở Hàn Quốc có tàu hỏa 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul' và 'KTX'. Nếu đi những tàu hỏa này thì có thể đi tới nhiều thành phố hoặc các vùng ở Hàn Quốc. Trong số những tàu này thì tàu nhanh nhất là tàu KTX. Tàu KTX chạy với tốc độ 200~300km một giờ. Nếu đi tàu Mugunghwa hoặc Saemaul thì sẽ mất 4~5 tiếng để đi từ Seoul đến Busan, nhưng tàu KTX chỉ mất 2 tiếng 40 phút. Nhưng ga tàu KTX chỉ có ở các thành phố lớn và giá vé cũng đắt.
g_text_translate.en: In Korea, there are trains called 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul', and 'KTX'. You can go to various cities and provinces in Korea by taking these trains. Among them, the fastest train is KTX. It runs at 200-300 km/h. It takes 4-5 hours from Seoul to Busan by taking the Mugunghwa or Saemaeul trains, whereas the KTX takes only 2 hours and 40 minutes. However, KTX stations are mainly located in large cities and the fare is expensive.
g_text_translate.it:
In Corea ci sono treni chiamati "Mugunghwa", "Saemaeul" e "KTX". Puoi andare in varie città e province della Corea prendendo questi treni. Tra questi, il treno più veloce è KTX. Funziona a 200-300 km/h. Ci vogliono 4-5 ore da Seoul a Busan prendendo i treni Mugunghwa o Saemaeul, mentre il KTX impiega solo 2 ore e 40 minuti. Tuttavia, le stazioni KTX si trovano principalmente nelle grandi città e la tariffa è costosa.

#######

In Korea, there are trains called 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul', and 'KTX'. You can go to various cities and provinces in Korea by taking these trains. Among them, the fastest train is KTX. It runs at 200-300 km/h. It takes 4-5 hours from Seoul to Busan by taking the Mugunghwa or Saemaeul trains, whereas the KTX takes only 2 hours and 40 minutes. However, KTX stations are mainly located in large cities and the fare is expensive.

En Corée, il existe des trains appelés « Mugunghwa », « Saemaeul » et « KTX ». Vous pouvez vous rendre dans différentes villes et provinces de Corée en empruntant ces trains. Parmi eux, le train le plus rapide est le KTX. Il roule à 200-300 km/h. Il faut 4-5 heures de Séoul à Busan en prenant les trains Mugunghwa ou Saemaeul, alors que le KTX ne prend que 2 heures et 40 minutes. Cependant, les stations KTX sont principalement situées dans les grandes villes et le tarif est cher.

g_text_translate.hi:
कोरिया में, 'मुगुनघ्वा', 'सैमेउल' और 'केटीएक्स' नाम से ट्रेनें हैं। आप इन ट्रेनों से कोरिया के विभिन्न शहरों और प्रांतों में जा सकते हैं। इनमें सबसे तेज़ ट्रेन KTX है. यह 200-300 किमी/घंटा की रफ्तार से चलती है। सियोल से बुसान तक मुगुनघ्वा या सैमेउल ट्रेन लेने में 4-5 घंटे लगते हैं, जबकि केटीएक्स में केवल 2 घंटे 40 मिनट लगते हैं। हालाँकि, KTX स्टेशन मुख्य रूप से बड़े शहरों में स्थित हैं और किराया महंगा है।

#######

In Korea, there are trains called 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul', and 'KTX'. You can go to various cities and provinces in Korea by taking these trains. Among them, the fastest train is KTX. It runs at 200-300 km/h. It takes 4-5 hours from Seoul to Busan by taking the Mugunghwa or Saemaeul trains, whereas the KTX takes only 2 hours and 40 minutes. However, KTX stations are mainly located in large cities and the fare is expensive.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kore'de 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul' ve 'KTX' adında trenler var. Bu trenlere binerek Kore’nin çeşitli şehir ve illerine gidebilirsiniz. Bunlar arasında en hızlı tren KTX’tir. 200-300 km/saat hızla çalışır. Seul'den Busan'a Mugunghwa veya Saemaeul trenleriyle 4-5 saat sürerken, KTX sadece 2 saat 40 dakika sürüyor. Ancak KTX istasyonları çoğunlukla büyük şehirlerde bulunmaktadır ve ücretler pahalıdır.

#######

In Korea, there are trains called 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul', and 'KTX'. You can go to various cities and provinces in Korea by taking these trains. Among them, the fastest train is KTX. It runs at 200-300 km/h. It takes 4-5 hours from Seoul to Busan by taking the Mugunghwa or Saemaeul trains, whereas the KTX takes only 2 hours and 40 minutes. However, KTX stations are mainly located in large cities and the fare is expensive.
g_text_translate.ne: कोरियामा 'मुगुङ्वा', 'साइमाउल' र 'केटीएक्स' नामक रेलहरू छन्। तपाईं यी रेलहरू लिएर कोरियाका विभिन्न शहर र प्रान्तहरूमा जान सक्नुहुन्छ। ती मध्ये, सबैभन्दा छिटो रेल KTX हो। यो 200-300 km/h मा चल्छ। सियोल देखि बुसान सम्म मुगुन्घवा वा साईमाउल ट्रेन चढ्दा ४-५ घण्टा लाग्छ, जबकि KTX ले मात्र २ घण्टा ४० मिनेट लाग्छ। यद्यपि, KTX स्टेशनहरू मुख्यतया ठूला शहरहरूमा अवस्थित छन् र भाडा महँगो छ।
g_text_translate.km: នៅប្រទេសកូរ៉េ មានរថភ្លើងដែលហៅថា 'Mugunghwa', 'Saemaeul' និង 'KTX'។ អ្នកអាចទៅទីក្រុង និងខេត្តផ្សេងៗក្នុងប្រទេសកូរ៉េដោយជិះរថភ្លើងទាំងនេះ។ ក្នុងចំណោមពួកគេ រថភ្លើងលឿនបំផុតគឺ KTX ។ វារត់ក្នុងល្បឿន 200-300 គីឡូម៉ែត្រក្នុងមួយម៉ោង។ វាត្រូវចំណាយពេល 4-5 ម៉ោងពីទីក្រុងសេអ៊ូលទៅប៊ូសានដោយជិះរថភ្លើង Mugunghwa ឬ Saemaeul ចំណែកឯ KTX ចំណាយពេលត្រឹមតែ 2 ម៉ោង 40 នាទីប៉ុណ្ណោះ។ ទោះជាយ៉ាងណាក៏ដោយ ស្ថានីយ៍ KTX ភាគច្រើនមានទីតាំងនៅទីក្រុងធំៗ ហើយថ្លៃសំបុត្រធ្វើដំណើរគឺថ្លៃ។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියාවේ 'මුගුන්ග්වා', 'සේමයුල්' සහ 'කේටීඑක්ස්' යනුවෙන් හඳුන්වන දුම්රිය තිබේ. මෙම දුම්රියෙන් ඔබට කොරියාවේ විවිධ නගර සහ පළාත් වෙත යා හැකිය. ඒ අතරින් වේගවත්ම දුම්රිය KTX වේ. එය පැයට කිලෝමීටර 200-300 ක වේගයෙන් ධාවනය වේ. Mugunghwa හෝ Saemaeul දුම්රියෙන් සෝල් සිට බුසාන් දක්වා පැය 4-5 ක් ගත වන අතර KTX සඳහා ගත වන්නේ පැය 2 යි විනාඩි 40 ක් පමණි. කෙසේ වෙතත්, KTX ස්ථාන ප්රධාන වශයෙන් විශාල නගරවල පිහිටා ඇති අතර ගාස්තුව මිල අධික වේ.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়াতে 'মুগুংঘওয়া', 'সাইমাইউল' এবং 'কেটিএক্স' নামে ট্রেন রয়েছে। এই ট্রেনগুলি নিয়ে আপনি কোরিয়ার বিভিন্ন শহর ও প্রদেশে যেতে পারেন। এর মধ্যে দ্রুততম ট্রেন হল KTX। এটি 200-300 km/h বেগে চলে। সিউল থেকে বুসানে মুগুংওয়া বা সায়েমাউল ট্রেনে 4-5 ঘন্টা সময় লাগে, যেখানে KTX সময় লাগে মাত্র 2 ঘন্টা 40 মিনিট। যাইহোক, কেটিএক্স স্টেশনগুলি প্রধানত বড় শহরগুলিতে অবস্থিত এবং ভাড়া ব্যয়বহুল।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားတွင် 'Mugunghwa'၊ 'Saemaeul' နှင့် 'KTX' ဟုခေါ်သော ရထားများရှိသည်။ ဤရထားဖြင့် ကိုရီးယားရှိ မြို့များနှင့် ပြည်နယ်အသီးသီးသို့ သွားနိုင်သည်။ ၎င်းတို့အနက်အမြန်ဆုံးရထားမှာ KTX ဖြစ်သည်။ 200-300 km/h နှုန်းနဲ့ မောင်းနှင်နိုင်ပါတယ်။ Seoul မှ Busan သို့ Mugunghwa သို့မဟုတ် Saemaeul ရထားများစီးခြင်းဖြင့် 4-5 နာရီကြာသည်၊ KTX သည် 2 နာရီနှင့် 40 မိနစ်သာကြာသည်။ သို့သော်လည်း KTX ဘူတာများသည် အဓိကအားဖြင့် မြို့ကြီးများတွင် တည်ရှိပြီး ယာဉ်စီးခသည် စျေးကြီးသည်။

general.g_text: 한국에는 ‘벼룩시장’, ‘교차로’, ‘가로수’ 같이 보통신문보다 크기가 작은 신문이 있습니다. 일할 사람을 구하는 회사가 여기에 광고를 내기 때문에 직업을 찾는 사람이 이런 신문을 이용할 수 있습니다. 그리고 중고 자동차나 가구, 가전제품을 이 신문을 통해 사고 팔기도 합니다. 집도 구할 수 있습니다. 이 신문은 버스 정류장이나 지하철역 근처에 있는데, 무료로 가져갈 수 있습니다. 이러한 신문을 <u>㉠<생활 정보지></u>라고 합니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ở Hàn Quốc có những tờ báo có kích thước nhỏ hơn các tờ báo thông thường như 'Chợ đồ cũ', 'Giao lộ', 'Rặng cây bên đường'. Những công ty tìm kiếm người làm đăng quảng cáo ở đây nên những người đang tìm kiếm việc làm cũng có thể sử dụng các báo này. Và người ta mua và bán những chiếc xe, đồ nội thất và đồ điện gia dụng cũ thông qua tờ báo này. Cũng có thể tìm được nhà thông qua những tờ báo này. Những tờ báo này được đặt gần bến xe buýt hoặc ga tàu điện ngầm, mọi người có thể lấy chúng miễn phí. Những tờ báo này được gọi là <u>㉠<Tờ thông tin đời sống></u>.
g_text_translate.en: In Korea, there are newspapers that are smaller than ordinary newspapers such as 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' and 'Street Tree'. Companies looking for people to work advertise here, so people looking for jobs can use these newspapers. And people buy and sell used cars, furniture, and appliances through these newspapers. You can even find houses. These newspapers are located near bus stops or subway stations, and you can take them for free. These newspapers are called <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u>.
g_text_translate.it:
In Corea ci sono giornali più piccoli dei normali giornali come "Flea Market", "Crossroads" e "Street Tree". Le aziende che cercano persone con cui lavorare fanno pubblicità qui, quindi le persone in cerca di lavoro possono utilizzare questi giornali. E la gente compra e vende auto usate, mobili ed elettrodomestici attraverso questi giornali. Puoi persino trovare case. Questi giornali si trovano vicino alle fermate degli autobus o alle stazioni della metropolitana e puoi prenderli gratuitamente. Questi giornali si chiamano <u><g>㉠</g><Documento Informativo Vivente></u>.

#######

In Korea, there are newspapers that are smaller than ordinary newspapers such as 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' and 'Street Tree'. Companies looking for people to work advertise here, so people looking for jobs can use these newspapers. And people buy and sell used cars, furniture, and appliances through these newspapers. You can even find houses. These newspapers are located near bus stops or subway stations, and you can take them for free. These newspapers are called <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u>.
g_text_translate.fr:
En Corée, il existe des journaux plus petits que les journaux ordinaires tels que "Flea Market", "Crossroads" et "Street Tree". Les entreprises à la recherche de personnes pour travailler font de la publicité ici, afin que les personnes à la recherche d'un emploi puissent utiliser ces journaux. Et les gens achètent et vendent des voitures, des meubles et des appareils électroménagers d'occasion par l'intermédiaire de ces journaux. Vous pouvez même trouver des maisons. Ces journaux sont situés près des arrêts de bus ou des stations de métro, et vous pouvez les prendre gratuitement. Ces journaux s'appellent <u><g>㉠</g><Document d'information sur la vie></u>.

#######

In Korea, there are newspapers that are smaller than ordinary newspapers such as 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' and 'Street Tree'. Companies looking for people to work advertise here, so people looking for jobs can use these newspapers. And people buy and sell used cars, furniture, and appliances through these newspapers. You can even find houses. These newspapers are located near bus stops or subway stations, and you can take them for free. These newspapers are called <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u>.
g_text_translate.es:
En Corea, hay periódicos que son más pequeños que los periódicos ordinarios, como 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' y 'Street Tree'. Las empresas que buscan personas para trabajar se anuncian aquí, por lo que las personas que buscan trabajo pueden usar estos periódicos. Y la gente compra y vende autos usados, muebles y electrodomésticos a través de estos periódicos. Incluso puedes encontrar casas. Estos periódicos se encuentran cerca de las paradas de autobús o estaciones de metro, y puedes llevarlos gratis. Estos periódicos se llaman <u><g>㉠</g><Documento informativo vivo></u>.

g_text_translate.id:
Di Korea, ada koran yang ukurannya lebih kecil dari koran biasa seperti 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' dan 'Street Tree'. Perusahaan yang mencari orang untuk bekerja beriklan di sini, sehingga orang yang mencari pekerjaan dapat menggunakan koran ini. Dan orang membeli dan menjual mobil bekas, furnitur, dan peralatan melalui surat kabar ini. Anda bahkan dapat menemukan rumah. Surat kabar ini terletak di dekat halte bus atau stasiun kereta bawah tanah, dan Anda dapat mengambilnya secara gratis. Surat kabar ini disebut <u><g>㉠</g><Kertas Informasi Hidup></u>.

g_text_translate.th:
ในเกาหลีมีหนังสือพิมพ์ที่มีขนาดเล็กกว่าหนังสือพิมพ์ทั่วไป เช่น 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' และ 'Street Tree' บริษัทต่างๆ ที่กำลังมองหาคนมาทำงานลงโฆษณาที่นี่ ดังนั้นผู้ที่กำลังมองหางานจึงสามารถใช้หนังสือพิมพ์เหล่านี้ได้ และผู้คนซื้อและขายรถยนต์มือสอง เฟอร์นิเจอร์ และเครื่องใช้ไฟฟ้าผ่านหนังสือพิมพ์เหล่านี้ คุณสามารถหาบ้านได้ หนังสือพิมพ์เหล่านี้ตั้งอยู่ใกล้กับป้ายรถเมล์หรือสถานีรถไฟใต้ดิน และคุณสามารถนำไปอ่านได้ฟรี หนังสือพิมพ์เหล่านี้เรียกว่า <u><g>㉠</g><เอกสารข้อมูลการดำรงชีวิต></u>.

g_text_translate.hi:
कोरिया में, ऐसे समाचार पत्र हैं जो सामान्य समाचार पत्रों से छोटे हैं जैसे 'पिस्सू मार्केट', 'क्रॉसरोड्स' और 'स्ट्रीट ट्री'। काम के लिए लोगों की तलाश करने वाली कंपनियां यहां विज्ञापन देती हैं, ताकि नौकरी की तलाश कर रहे लोग इन समाचार पत्रों का उपयोग कर सकें। और लोग इन समाचार पत्रों के माध्यम से पुरानी कारें, फर्नीचर और उपकरण खरीदते और बेचते हैं। आपको घर भी मिल सकते हैं. ये समाचार पत्र बस स्टॉप या सबवे स्टेशनों के पास स्थित हैं, और आप इन्हें निःशुल्क ले सकते हैं। इन समाचार पत्रों को <u> कहा जाता है<g>㉠</g><लिविंग इंफॉर्मेशन पेपर></u>।

g_text_translate.ne: कोरियामा ‘फ्ली मार्केट’, ‘क्रोसरोड्स’ र ‘स्ट्रीट ट्री’ जस्ता सामान्य अखबारहरू भन्दा सानो अखबारहरू छन्। काम गर्न खोज्ने कम्पनीहरूले यहाँ विज्ञापन गर्छन्, त्यसैले काम खोज्नेहरूले यी समाचारपत्रहरू प्रयोग गर्न सक्छन्। र मानिसहरूले यी समाचारपत्रहरू मार्फत प्रयोग गरिएका कारहरू, फर्निचर र उपकरणहरू किन्छन् र बेच्छन्। तपाईं घरहरू पनि पाउन सक्नुहुन्छ। यी समाचारपत्रहरू बस स्टपहरू वा सबवे स्टेशनहरू नजिकै अवस्थित छन्, र तपाईंले तिनीहरूलाई निःशुल्क लिन सक्नुहुन्छ। यी समाचारपत्रहरूलाई भनिन्छ <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u>।
g_text_translate.km: នៅប្រទេសកូរ៉េ មានកាសែតតូចជាងកាសែតធម្មតា ដូចជា 'ផ្សារចែវ' 'ផ្លូវបំបែក' និង 'ដើមឈើតាមដងផ្លូវ' ។ ក្រុមហ៊ុនដែលកំពុងស្វែងរកមនុស្សធ្វើការផ្សាយពាណិជ្ជកម្មនៅទីនេះ ដូច្នេះអ្នកដែលស្វែងរកការងារអាចប្រើប្រាស់កាសែតទាំងនេះបាន។ ហើយមនុស្សទិញ និងលក់រថយន្តជជុះ គ្រឿងសង្ហារិម និងឧបករណ៍ប្រើប្រាស់តាមរយៈកាសែតទាំងនេះ។ អ្នកក៏អាចរកផ្ទះបានដែរ។ កាសែតទាំងនេះមានទីតាំងនៅជិតចំណតឡានក្រុង ឬស្ថានីយ៍រថភ្លើងក្រោមដី ហើយអ្នកអាចយកវាដោយឥតគិតថ្លៃ។ កាសែតទាំងនេះត្រូវបានគេហៅថា <u><g>㉠</g>< Living Information Paper></u>។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියාවේ ‘ෆ්ලී මාකට්’, ‘ක්‍රොස්‍රෝඩ්ස්’, ‘ස්ට්‍රීට් ට්‍රී’ වැනි සාමාන්‍ය පුවත්පත්වලට වඩා කුඩා පුවත්පත් තිබේ. වැඩ කිරීමට මිනිසුන් සොයන සමාගම් මෙහි දැන්වීම් පළ කරන බැවින් රැකියා සොයන අයට මෙම පුවත්පත් භාවිතා කළ හැකිය. මිනිසුන් පාවිච්චි කරන ලද මෝටර් රථ, ගෘහ භාණ්ඩ සහ උපකරණ මිලදී ගැනීම සහ විකිණීම මෙම පුවත්පත් හරහා සිදු කරයි. ගෙවල් පවා හොයාගන්න පුළුවන්. මෙම පුවත්පත් බස් නැවතුම් පොළවල් හෝ උමං දුම්රිය ස්ථාන අසල පිහිටා ඇති අතර ඔබට ඒවා නොමිලේ රැගෙන යා හැකිය. මෙම පුවත්පත් <u><g>㉠</g><ජීවන ​​තොරතුරු පත්‍රිකාව></u> ලෙස හැඳින්වේ.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়াতে, 'ফ্লি মার্কেট', 'ক্রসরোড' এবং 'স্ট্রিট ট্রি'-এর মতো সাধারণ সংবাদপত্রের চেয়ে ছোট সংবাদপত্র রয়েছে। কাজ করার জন্য লোক খুঁজছে এমন কোম্পানি এখানে বিজ্ঞাপন দেয়, তাই যারা চাকরি খুঁজছেন তারা এই সংবাদপত্র ব্যবহার করতে পারেন। এবং লোকেরা এই সংবাদপত্রের মাধ্যমে ব্যবহৃত গাড়ি, আসবাবপত্র এবং যন্ত্রপাতি ক্রয় এবং বিক্রয় করে। আপনি এমনকি ঘর খুঁজে পেতে পারেন. এই সংবাদপত্রগুলি বাস স্টপ বা পাতাল রেল স্টেশনগুলির কাছে অবস্থিত এবং আপনি সেগুলি বিনামূল্যে নিতে পারেন৷ এই সংবাদপত্রগুলোকে বলা হয় <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u>।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားတွင် 'Flea Market'၊ 'Crossroads' နှင့် 'Street Tree' ကဲ့သို့သော သာမန်သတင်းစာများထက် သေးငယ်သော သတင်းစာများ ရှိပါသည်။ အလုပ်ရှာတဲ့ ကုမ္ပဏီတွေက ဒီမှာ ကြော်ငြာပေးတဲ့အတွက် အလုပ်ရှာတဲ့လူတွေက ဒီသတင်းစာတွေကို သုံးနိုင်တယ်။ ပြီးတော့ လူတွေက ကားဟောင်းတွေ၊ ပရိဘောဂတွေနဲ့ အသုံးအဆောင်ပစ္စည်းတွေကို အဲဒီသတင်းစာတွေကတဆင့် ၀ယ်ရောင်းကြတယ်။ အိမ်တွေတောင် ရှာလို့ရတယ်။ ဤသတင်းစာများသည် ဘတ်စ်ကားမှတ်တိုင်များ သို့မဟုတ် မြေအောက်ရထားဘူတာများအနီးတွင် တည်ရှိပြီး ၎င်းတို့ကို အခမဲ့ ယူဆောင်သွားနိုင်ပါသည်။ ဤသတင်းစာများကို <u><g>㉠</g><Living Information Paper></u> ဟုခေါ်သည်။

content.q_text: 밑줄 친 ㉠에 대한 설명으로 맞지 않는 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 이것을 통해 일을 구할 수 있습니다.

explain.vi:
Chọn giải thích không đúng về phần gạch chân ㉠.

1. Bán ở bến xe bus.
2. Kích thước nhỏ hơn báo thông thường.
3. Có thể tìm việc thông qua báo này.
4. Có Chợ đồ cũ', 'Giao lộ', 'Rặng cây bên đường',...
--------------------
무료: Miễn phí
가져가다: Cầm lấy, mang đi

Những tờ báo này có thể cầm đi miễn phí nên đáp án đúng là 1.
explain.en:
Choose the one that does not fit as an explanation for the underlined <g>㉠</g>.

1. They are sold near the bus stop.
2. They are smaller than regular newspapers. 
3. You can get a job through these newspapers.
4. There are 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads', and 'Street Tree'.
--------------------
<g>무료:</g> Free
<g>가져가다:</g> To take, to bring

These newspapers can be taken for free, so the correct answer is 1.
explain.it:
Scegli quello che non si adatta come spiegazione per il sottolineato<g>㉠</g>.

1. Sono venduti vicino alla fermata dell'autobus.
2. Sono più piccoli dei normali giornali.
3. Puoi ottenere un lavoro tramite questi giornali.
4. Ci sono "Flea Market", "Crossroads" e "Street Tree".
--------------------
<g>무료:</g>Libero
<g>가져가다:</g>Prendere, portare

Questi giornali possono essere presi gratuitamente, quindi la risposta corretta è 1.

4. 'पिस्सू बाजार', 'चौराहा' और 'स्ट्रीट ट्री' हैं।
------------------------
<g>무료:</g>मुक्त
<g>가져가다:</g>ले जाना, लाना

ये अखबार मुफ़्त में लिए जा सकते हैं, इसलिए सही उत्तर 1 है।

#######

Choose the one that does not fit as an explanation for the underlined <g>㉠</g>.

1. They are sold near the bus stop.
2. They are smaller than regular newspapers. 
3. You can get a job through these newspapers.
4. There are 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads', and 'Street Tree'.
--------------------
<g>무료:</g> Free
<g>가져가다:</g> To take, to bring

These newspapers can be taken for free, so the correct answer is 1.

explain.km:
ជ្រើសរើសមួយដែលមិនសមនឹងការពន្យល់សម្រាប់ <g>㉠</g> ដែលបានគូសបញ្ជាក់។

1. គេលក់នៅជិតចំណតឡានក្រុង។
2. ពួកគេមានទំហំតូចជាងកាសែតធម្មតា។
3. អ្នកអាចទទួលបានការងារតាមរយៈកាសែតទាំងនេះ។
4. មាន 'ផ្សារចែវ' 'ផ្លូវបំបែក' និង 'ដើមឈើតាមដងផ្លូវ' ។
--------------------
<g>무료៖</g> ឥតគិតថ្លៃ
<g>가져가다:</g> ដើម្បីយក នាំ

កាសែត​ទាំងនេះ​អាច​យក​បាន​ដោយ​ឥតគិតថ្លៃ ដូច្នេះ​ចម្លើយ​ត្រឹមត្រូវ​គឺ ១.
explain.si:
යටින් ඉරි ඇඳ ඇති <g>㉠</g> සඳහා පැහැදිලි කිරීමක් ලෙස නොගැලපෙන එකක් තෝරන්න.

1. ඒවා බස් නැවතුම අසල විකුණනු ලැබේ.
2. ඒවා සාමාන්‍ය පුවත්පත්වලට වඩා කුඩා ය.
3. මෙම පුවත්පත් හරහා ඔබට රැකියාවක් ලබා ගත හැක.
4. 'Flea Market', 'Crossroads' සහ 'Street Tree' ඇත.
----------------------
<g>무료:</g> නොමිලේ
<g>가져가다:</g> ගැනීමට, ගෙන ඒමට

මෙම පුවත්පත් නොමිලේ ලබා ගත හැකි බැවින් නිවැරදි පිළිතුර 1 වේ.
explain.bn:
আন্ডারলাইন করা <g>㉠</g>-এর ব্যাখ্যা হিসেবে উপযুক্ত নয় এমন একটি বেছে নিন।

3. আপনি এই সংবাদপত্রের মাধ্যমে চাকরি পেতে পারেন।
4. এখানে 'ফ্লি মার্কেট', 'ক্রসরোড' এবং 'স্ট্রিট ট্রি' রয়েছে।
------------------
<g>무료:</g> বিনামূল্যে
<g>가져가다:</g> নিতে, আনতে

explain.my:
မျဉ်းသားထားသော <g>㉠</g> အတွက် ရှင်းလင်းချက်အဖြစ် အဆင်မပြေသည့်အရာကို ရွေးပါ။

1. ကားမှတ်တိုင်အနီးတွင် ရောင်းသည်။
၂။ ၎င်းတို့သည် ပုံမှန်သတင်းစာများထက် သေးငယ်သည်။
3. ဤသတင်းစာများမှတဆင့်သင်အလုပ်ရနိုင်သည်။
4. 'ဈေးသည်'၊ 'လမ်းဆုံ' နှင့် 'လမ်းသစ်' ရှိသည်။
--------------------
<g>무료:</g> အခမဲ့
<g>가져가다:</g> ယူရန်၊ ယူလာရန်

#######

Who doesn't need these newspapers?

3. प्रयोग गरिएको फर्निचर बेच्न चाहने व्यक्ति

explain.km:
តើអ្នកណាមិនត្រូវការកាសែតទាំងនេះ?

1. មនុស្សម្នាក់ស្វែងរកផ្ទះ
2. អ្នកដែលចង់ទិញឡានជជុះ
3. អ្នកដែលចង់លក់គ្រឿងសង្ហារឹមជជុះ
4. ជាមនុស្សចង់មើលកម្មវិធីទូរទស្សន៍
--------------------

explain.si:
කාටද මේ පත්තර අවශ්‍ය නැත්තේ?

1. නිවසක් සොයන පුද්ගලයෙක්
2. පාවිච්චි කරන ලද මෝටර් රථයක් මිලදී ගැනීමට කැමති පුද්ගලයෙක්
3. පාවිච්චි කරන ලද ගෘහ භාණ්ඩ විකිණීමට කැමති අයෙක්
4. රූපවාහිනී වැඩසටහන් නැරඹීමට කැමති අයෙකි
----------------------
<g>가전제품:</g> ගෘහ උපකරණ
<g>중고:</g> භාවිතා කළ ලිපිය

<g>V/A + 기 때문에:</g> නිසා ... එසේ.
explain.bn:
কার এই সংবাদপত্রের প্রয়োজন নেই?

1. একজন ব্যক্তি একটি বাড়ি খুঁজছেন
2. একজন ব্যক্তি যিনি একটি ব্যবহৃত গাড়ি কিনতে চান৷
3. একজন ব্যক্তি যিনি ব্যবহৃত আসবাবপত্র বিক্রি করতে চান
4. একজন ব্যক্তি যিনি টেলিভিশন অনুষ্ঠান দেখতে চান
------------------
<g>가전제품:</g> বাড়ির যন্ত্রপাতি
<g>중고:</g> ব্যবহৃত নিবন্ধ

<g>V/A + 기 때문에:</g> কারণ ... তাই।

general.g_text: 길을 가다가 보면 교통 표지판이나 안전표지판을 자주 볼 수 있습니다. 그런데 이 표지판들에 사용되는 색깔에는 다 뜻이 있습니다. 산업안전보건법에 따르면 빨간색은 금지나 경고를 나타내고, 파란색은 지시를 나타내는데 사용됩니다. 그래서 '들어오지 마십시오'와 같은 금지 표시는 주로 빨간색으로 되어 있고, '자전거 길입니다'와 같은 지시 표시는 주로 파란색으로 되어 있습니다. 노란색으로 된 표지도 있는데, 이 것은 주로 경고나 주의를 나타냅니다. 그리고 녹색은 비상구나 병원이 있는 곳 등을 안내해 주는 표지에 사용됩니다. 그밖에 흰색과 검은색도 쓰이는데, 이 색들은 글자를 쓰는데 사용되거나 다른 표지들의 보조색으로 사용됩니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Trên đường đi, bạn sẽ thường xuyên nhìn thấy các biển báo giao thông và biển báo an toàn. Tuy nhiên, tất cả màu sắc được sử dụng trên các biển báo này đều có ý nghĩa. Theo Đạo luật An toàn và Sức khỏe Nghề nghiệp, màu đỏ được sử dụng để chỉ các điều bị cấm hoặc cảnh báo và màu xanh lam được sử dụng để chỉ dẫn. Vì vậy mà những biển báo như 'Không được phép vào trong' chủ yếu có màu đỏ và những biển chỉ dẫn như 'Đường dành cho xe đạp' có màu xanh lam. Cũng có những biển báo màu vàng chủ yếu dùng để đưa ra cảnh báo hoặc chú ý. Và các biển báo màu xanh lá thường dùng để chỉ dẫn cho những nơi như lối thoát hiểm hoặc bệnh viện, Ngoài ra màu trắng và đen cũng được sử dụng để viết chữ (lên các biển đó) hoặc được dùng làm màu bổ sung cho các biển báo khác.
g_text_translate.en: You can often see traffic signs or safety signs on the road. However, the colors used on these signs all have meanings. According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.it:
Spesso puoi vedere segnali stradali o segnali di sicurezza sulla strada. Tuttavia, i colori usati su questi segni hanno tutti un significato. Secondo l'Occupational Safety and Health Act, il rosso viene utilizzato per indicare divieti o avvertenze e il blu viene utilizzato per indicare le istruzioni. Questo è il motivo per cui i segnali di divieto come "Vietato entrare" sono solitamente in rosso, mentre i segnali didattici come "Questa è una pista ciclabile" sono principalmente in blu. Ci sono anche cartelli gialli, che di solito indicano avvertimenti o precauzioni. E il verde è usato per i segnali che ti guidano verso le uscite di emergenza e gli ospedali. Inoltre, vengono utilizzati anche il bianco e il nero, questi colori vengono utilizzati per scrivere lettere o come colori assortiti per altri segni.

#######

You can often see traffic signs or safety signs on the road. However, the colors used on these signs all have meanings. According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.fr:
Vous pouvez souvent voir des panneaux de signalisation ou des panneaux de sécurité sur la route. Cependant, les couleurs utilisées sur ces panneaux ont toutes une signification. Selon la Loi sur la sécurité et la santé au travail, le rouge est utilisé pour indiquer les interdictions ou les avertissements, et le bleu est utilisé pour indiquer les instructions. C'est pourquoi les panneaux d'interdiction tels que "Ne pas entrer" sont généralement en rouge et les panneaux d'instruction tels que "Ceci est une piste cyclable" sont principalement en bleu. Il existe également des panneaux jaunes, qui indiquent généralement des avertissements ou des mises en garde. Et le vert est utilisé pour les panneaux qui vous guident vers les issues de secours et les hôpitaux. De plus, le blanc et le noir sont également utilisés, ces couleurs sont utilisées pour écrire des lettres ou comme couleurs assorties pour d'autres signes.

#######

In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.es:
A menudo puede ver señales de tráfico o señales de seguridad en la carretera. Sin embargo, todos los colores usados ​​en estos letreros tienen significados. De acuerdo con la Ley de Seguridad y Salud Ocupacional, el rojo se usa para indicar prohibiciones o advertencias, y el azul se usa para indicar instrucciones. Por eso, las señales de prohibición como 'No entrar' suelen ser de color rojo, y las señales de instrucción como 'Esto es un carril bici' son principalmente de color azul. También hay carteles amarillos, que suelen indicar advertencias o precauciones. Y el verde se usa para las señales que lo guían hacia las salidas de emergencia y los hospitales. Además, también se utilizan el blanco y el negro, estos colores se utilizan para escribir letras o como colores surtidos para otros signos.
