g_text_translate.id:
Anda sering dapat melihat rambu lalu lintas atau rambu keselamatan di jalan. Namun, warna yang digunakan pada tanda ini semuanya memiliki arti. Menurut Undang-Undang Keselamatan dan Kesehatan Kerja, merah digunakan untuk menunjukkan larangan atau peringatan, dan biru digunakan untuk menunjukkan instruksi. Itu sebabnya rambu larangan seperti 'Jangan masuk' biasanya berwarna merah, dan rambu petunjuk seperti 'Ini jalur sepeda' kebanyakan berwarna biru. Ada juga tanda kuning, yang biasanya menunjukkan peringatan atau peringatan. Dan hijau digunakan untuk tanda yang memandu Anda ke pintu keluar darurat dan rumah sakit. Selain itu, warna putih dan hitam juga digunakan, warna ini digunakan untuk menulis huruf atau sebagai warna assort untuk tanda lainnya.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
道路には交通標識や安全標識をよく見かけます。しかし、これらの標識に使われている色にはすべて意味があります。労働安全衛生法による禁止や警告は赤色、指示は青色で表されています。そのため、「進入禁止」などの禁止標識は通常赤色で、「ここは自転車道です」などの指示標識は主に青色です。通常、警告または注意を示す黄色の標識もあります。また、緑は非常口や病院を案内する標識に使われています。また、白や黒も使われ、これらの色は文字を書いたり、他のサインのアソートカラーとして使用されます。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Auf der Straße sieht man oft Verkehrszeichen oder Sicherheitszeichen. Die auf diesen Schildern verwendeten Farben haben jedoch alle eine Bedeutung. Laut Arbeitsschutzgesetz kennzeichnet Rot Verbote oder Warnungen und Blau Anweisungen. Deshalb sind Verbotsschilder wie „Nicht betreten“ meist rot und Hinweisschilder wie „Das ist ein Radweg“ meist blau. Es gibt auch gelbe Schilder, die normalerweise Warnungen oder Vorsichtsmaßnahmen anzeigen. Und Grün wird für Schilder verwendet, die Sie zu Notausgängen und Krankenhäusern führen. Darüber hinaus werden auch Weiß und Schwarz verwendet, diese Farben werden zum Schreiben von Buchstaben oder als Mischfarben für andere Zeichen verwendet.

#######

That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
您经常可以在路上看到交通标志或安全标志。但是，这些标志上使用的颜色都具有含义。根据《职业安全卫生法》，红色用于表示禁止或警告，蓝色用于表示指示。这就是为什么“请勿进入”等禁止标志通常为红色，而“这是自行车道”等指示标志主要为蓝色。还有黄色标志，通常表示警告或注意事项。绿色用于引导您前往紧急出口和医院的标志。此外，还使用白色和黑色，这些颜色用于书写字母或作为其他标志的颜色。

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
На дороге часто можно увидеть дорожные знаки или знаки безопасности. Однако все цвета, используемые на этих знаках, имеют значения. Согласно Закону об охране труда, красный цвет используется для обозначения запретов или предупреждений, а синий – для обозначения инструкций. Вот почему запрещающие знаки, такие как «Не входить», обычно имеют красный цвет, а инструктивные знаки, такие как «Это велосипедная дорожка», — преимущественно синий цвет. Есть также желтые знаки, которые обычно обозначают предупреждения или предостережения. А зеленый цвет используется для знаков, которые ведут к аварийным выходам и больницам. Кроме того, используются также белый и черный цвета, эти цвета используются для написания букв или как цвета-ассорти для других знаков.

In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.th:
คุณมักจะเห็นป้ายจราจรหรือป้ายความปลอดภัยบนท้องถนน อย่างไรก็ตามสีที่ใช้บนป้ายเหล่านี้ล้วนมีความหมาย ตามกฎหมายความปลอดภัยและอาชีวอนามัย สีแดงใช้เพื่อระบุข้อห้ามหรือคำเตือน และสีน้ำเงินใช้เพื่อระบุคำแนะนำ นั่นคือเหตุผลว่าทำไมป้ายห้าม เช่น 'ห้ามเข้า' มักจะเป็นสีแดง และป้ายแนะนำ เช่น 'นี่คือทางจักรยาน' ส่วนใหญ่เป็นสีน้ำเงิน นอกจากนี้ยังมีป้ายสีเหลืองซึ่งมักจะบ่งบอกถึงคำเตือนหรือข้อควรระวัง และสีเขียวใช้สำหรับป้ายที่นำทางคุณไปยังทางออกฉุกเฉินและโรงพยาบาล นอกจากนี้ยังใช้สีขาวและสีดำ สีเหล่านี้ใช้สำหรับการเขียนตัวอักษรหรือเป็นสีคละสีสำหรับป้ายอื่นๆ

#######

You can often see traffic signs or safety signs on the road. However, the colors used on these signs all have meanings. According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.pt:
Muitas vezes você pode ver sinais de trânsito ou de segurança na estrada. No entanto, todas as cores usadas nesses sinais têm significados. De acordo com a Lei de Segurança e Saúde Ocupacional, o vermelho é usado para indicar proibições ou advertências e o azul é usado para indicar instruções. É por isso que os sinais de proibição como “Não entre” são geralmente vermelhos e os sinais de instrução como “Isto é uma ciclovia” são principalmente azuis. Existem também sinais amarelos, que geralmente indicam avisos ou cuidados. E o verde é usado para sinalizações que orientam para saídas de emergência e hospitais. Além disso, também são utilizados o branco e o preto, cores utilizadas para escrever letras ou como cores sortidas para outros sinais.

#######

According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.hi:
आप अक्सर सड़क पर यातायात संकेत या सुरक्षा संकेत देख सकते हैं। हालाँकि, इन संकेतों पर इस्तेमाल किए गए सभी रंगों के अपने अर्थ होते हैं। व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा और स्वास्थ्य अधिनियम के अनुसार, लाल का उपयोग निषेध या चेतावनियों को इंगित करने के लिए किया जाता है, और नीले का उपयोग निर्देशों को इंगित करने के लिए किया जाता है। इसीलिए 'प्रवेश न करें' जैसे निषेध संकेत आमतौर पर लाल रंग में होते हैं, और 'यह एक साइकिल पथ है' जैसे निर्देशात्मक संकेत मुख्य रूप से नीले रंग में होते हैं। इसमें पीले संकेत भी होते हैं, जो आमतौर पर चेतावनी या चेतावनी का संकेत देते हैं। और हरे रंग का उपयोग उन संकेतों के लिए किया जाता है जो आपको आपातकालीन निकास और अस्पतालों तक ले जाते हैं। इसके अलावा, सफेद और काले रंग का भी उपयोग किया जाता है, इन रंगों का उपयोग पत्र लिखने या अन्य संकेतों के लिए मिश्रित रंगों के रूप में किया जाता है।

#######

You can often see traffic signs or safety signs on the road. However, the colors used on these signs all have meanings. According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.tr:
Yolda sıklıkla trafik işaretlerini veya güvenlik işaretlerini görebilirsiniz. Ancak bu tabelalarda kullanılan renklerin hepsinin bir anlamı vardır. İş Sağlığı ve Güvenliği Kanunu'na göre kırmızı, yasakları veya uyarıları belirtmek için, mavi ise talimatları belirtmek için kullanılır. Bu nedenle 'Girmeyin' gibi yasak işaretleri genellikle kırmızı renkte, 'Burası bir bisiklet yolu' gibi talimat levhaları çoğunlukla mavi renktedir. Ayrıca genellikle uyarı veya ikazları belirten sarı işaretler de vardır. Yeşil ise sizi acil çıkışlara ve hastanelere yönlendiren işaretler için kullanılır. Ayrıca beyaz ve siyah da kullanılmakta olup, bu renkler harf yazımında veya diğer tabelalara renk çeşitlendirmek amacıyla kullanılmaktadır.

#######

You can often see traffic signs or safety signs on the road. However, the colors used on these signs all have meanings. According to the Occupational Safety and Health Act, red is used to indicate prohibitions or warnings, and blue is used to indicate instructions. That is why prohibition signs such as 'Do not enter' are usually in red, and instructional signs such as 'This is a bicycle path' are mainly in blue. There are also yellow signs, which usually indicate warnings or cautions. And green is used for signs that guide you to emergency exits and hospitals. In addition, white and black are also used, these colors are used for writing letters or as assort colors for other signs.
g_text_translate.ne: तपाईले प्रायः सडकमा ट्राफिक चिन्ह वा सुरक्षा संकेतहरू देख्न सक्नुहुन्छ। यद्यपि, यी चिन्हहरूमा प्रयोग गरिएका रंगहरू सबैको अर्थ छ। पेशागत सुरक्षा र स्वास्थ्य ऐन अनुसार रातो रङ निषेध वा चेतावनी संकेत गर्न प्रयोग गरिन्छ भने नीलोलाई निर्देशन संकेत गर्न प्रयोग गरिन्छ। यसैले 'प्रवेश नगर्नुहोस्' जस्ता निषेध चिन्हहरू सामान्यतया रातो रङमा हुन्छन्, र 'यो साइकल मार्ग हो' जस्ता निर्देशनात्मक संकेतहरू मुख्यतया नीलोमा हुन्छन्। त्यहाँ पहेंलो चिन्हहरू पनि छन्, जसले सामान्यतया चेतावनी वा सावधानीहरू संकेत गर्दछ। र हरियो संकेतहरूका लागि प्रयोग गरिन्छ जसले तपाईंलाई आपतकालीन निकास र अस्पतालहरूमा मार्गदर्शन गर्दछ। थप रूपमा, सेतो र कालो पनि प्रयोग गरिन्छ, यी रंगहरू अक्षरहरू लेख्न वा अन्य चिन्हहरूको लागि मिश्रित रंगहरूको रूपमा प्रयोग गरिन्छ।
g_text_translate.km: ជាញឹកញាប់អ្នកអាចឃើញផ្លាកសញ្ញាចរាចរណ៍ ឬផ្លាកសញ្ញាសុវត្ថិភាពនៅលើផ្លូវ។ ទោះជាយ៉ាងណាក៏ដោយ ពណ៌ដែលប្រើនៅលើសញ្ញាទាំងនេះសុទ្ធតែមានអត្ថន័យ។ យោងតាមច្បាប់ស្តីពីសុវត្ថិភាពការងារ និងសុខភាព ពណ៌ក្រហមត្រូវបានប្រើដើម្បីបង្ហាញពីការហាមឃាត់ ឬការព្រមាន ហើយពណ៌ខៀវត្រូវបានប្រើដើម្បីបង្ហាញពីការណែនាំ។ នោះហើយជាមូលហេតុដែលផ្លាកសញ្ញាហាមឃាត់ដូចជា 'ហាមចូល' ជាធម្មតាមានពណ៌ក្រហម ហើយសញ្ញាណែនាំដូចជា 'នេះជាផ្លូវកង់' ភាគច្រើនជាពណ៌ខៀវ។ វាក៏មានសញ្ញាពណ៌លឿងផងដែរ ដែលជាធម្មតាបង្ហាញពីការព្រមាន ឬការប្រុងប្រយ័ត្ន។ ហើយពណ៌បៃតងត្រូវបានប្រើសម្រាប់សញ្ញាដែលណែនាំអ្នកទៅកាន់ច្រកចេញសង្គ្រោះបន្ទាន់ និងមន្ទីរពេទ្យ។ លើសពីនេះទៀត ពណ៌ស និងខ្មៅក៏ត្រូវបានគេប្រើផងដែរ ពណ៌ទាំងនេះត្រូវបានប្រើសម្រាប់ការសរសេរអក្សរ ឬជាពណ៌ចម្រុះសម្រាប់សញ្ញាផ្សេងទៀត។
g_text_translate.si: ඔබට බොහෝ විට මාර්ග සංඥා හෝ ආරක්ෂිත සලකුණු මාර්ගයේ දැකිය හැකිය. කෙසේ වෙතත්, මෙම සලකුණු මත භාවිතා කරන වර්ණ සියල්ලම අර්ථයන් ඇත. වෘත්තීය සුරක්ෂිතතා සහ සෞඛ්‍ය පනතට අනුව, තහනම් කිරීම් හෝ අනතුරු ඇඟවීම් දැක්වීමට රතු පැහැය ද, උපදෙස් දැක්වීමට නිල් පැහැය ද යොදා ගනී. ‘ඇතුළත් නොවන්න’ වැනි තහනම් පුවරු සාමාන්‍යයෙන් රතු පැහැයෙන් ද ‘මේ පාපැදි මාර්ගයකි’ වැනි උපදේශන පුවරු ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් නිල් පැහැයෙන් ද ඇත්තේ එබැවිනි. සාමාන්යයෙන් අනතුරු ඇඟවීම් හෝ අනතුරු ඇඟවීම් පෙන්නුම් කරන කහ පැහැති සලකුණු ද ඇත. හදිසි පිටවීම් සහ රෝහල් වෙත ඔබව යොමු කරන සංඥා සඳහා කොළ භාවිතා වේ. ඊට අමතරව, සුදු සහ කළු ද භාවිතා වේ, මෙම වර්ණ අකුරු ලිවීමට හෝ වෙනත් සලකුණු සඳහා වර්ණ ලෙස භාවිතා කරයි.
g_text_translate.bn: আপনি প্রায়ই রাস্তায় ট্রাফিক সাইন বা নিরাপত্তা চিহ্ন দেখতে পারেন। যাইহোক, এই চিহ্নগুলিতে ব্যবহৃত রঙগুলির সমস্ত অর্থ রয়েছে। পেশাগত নিরাপত্তা ও স্বাস্থ্য আইন অনুযায়ী, লাল রঙ নিষেধাজ্ঞা বা সতর্কতা নির্দেশ করতে ব্যবহৃত হয় এবং নীল নির্দেশাবলী নির্দেশ করতে ব্যবহৃত হয়। এই কারণেই 'প্রবেশ করবেন না'-এর মতো নিষেধাজ্ঞার চিহ্নগুলি সাধারণত লাল রঙের হয় এবং 'এটি একটি সাইকেল পথ'-এর মতো নির্দেশমূলক চিহ্নগুলি প্রধানত নীল রঙে থাকে। এছাড়াও হলুদ চিহ্ন রয়েছে, যা সাধারণত সতর্কতা বা সতর্কতা নির্দেশ করে। এবং সবুজ চিহ্নগুলির জন্য ব্যবহার করা হয় যা আপনাকে জরুরী প্রস্থান এবং হাসপাতালে নির্দেশ করে। এছাড়াও, সাদা এবং কালোও ব্যবহার করা হয়, এই রঙগুলি অক্ষর লেখার জন্য বা অন্যান্য চিহ্নগুলির জন্য বিভিন্ন রঙ হিসাবে ব্যবহৃত হয়।
g_text_translate.my: လမ်းပေါ်တွင် ယာဉ်ကြောပိတ်ဆို့မှု ဆိုင်းဘုတ်များ သို့မဟုတ် ဘေးကင်းရေး ဆိုင်းဘုတ်များကို မကြာခဏ မြင်တွေ့နိုင်သည်။ သို့သော် ဤလက္ခဏာများပေါ်တွင် သုံးသောအရောင်များသည် အဓိပ္ပါယ်ရှိသည်။ လုပ်ငန်းခွင်ဘေးကင်းရေးနှင့် ကျန်းမာရေးဥပဒေအရ တားမြစ်ချက်များ သို့မဟုတ် သတိပေးချက်များကို ညွှန်ပြရန် အနီရောင်ကို အသုံးပြုပြီး အပြာရောင်ကို ညွှန်ကြားချက်များကို ညွှန်ပြရန် အသုံးပြုသည်။ ထို့ကြောင့် 'မဝင်ရ' ကဲ့သို့သော တားမြစ်ဆိုင်းဘုတ်များသည် အများအားဖြင့် အနီရောင်ဖြစ်ပြီး 'ဤသည်မှာ စက်ဘီးလမ်းကြောင်းဖြစ်သည်' ကဲ့သို့သော လမ်းညွှန်ဆိုင်းဘုတ်များသည် အဓိကအားဖြင့် အပြာရောင်ဖြစ်သည်။ သတိပေးချက်များ သို့မဟုတ် သတိပေးချက်များကို ညွှန်ပြလေ့ရှိသော အဝါရောင်လက္ခဏာများလည်း ရှိပါသည်။ အရေးပေါ်ထွက်ပေါက်များနှင့် ဆေးရုံများသို့ လမ်းညွှန်သည့် ဆိုင်းဘုတ်များအတွက် အစိမ်းရောင်ကို အသုံးပြုသည်။ ထို့အပြင် အဖြူနှင့် အနက်ကိုလည်း အသုံးပြုသည်၊ ဤအရောင်များကို စာလုံးရေးရန် သို့မဟုတ် အခြား အမှတ်အသားများအတွက် အရောင်မျိုးစုံအဖြစ် အသုံးပြုသည်။

general.g_text: 한국말에는 우리 몸과 관계된 표현이 많습니다. 아무 음식이나 잘 먹지 않고 자기가 좋아하는 것만 먹는 사람은 '입이 짧다'라고 말합니다. 그리고 들은 말을 다른 사람한테 잘 옮기지 않는 사람은 '입이 무겁다'라고 합니다. 입이 가벼운 사람은 그 반대의 뜻입니다. 그러니까 입이 ㉠_________ 사람한테는 비밀을 말하지 않는 것이 좋습니다. 또 어떤 음식이 맛이 있을 때 '음식이 입에 맞다'라고 말합니다. 눈이나 발을 이용한 표현도 있습니다. '눈이 높다'라고 하면 바라는 것이 너무 많은 사람을 말합니다. 그리고 '발이 넓다'라고 하면 여기저기 아는 사람이 많다는 뜻입니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Trong tiếng Hàn có rất nhiều biểu hiện liên quan đến cơ thể chúng ta. Những người không hay ăn bất cứ món gì mà chỉ ăn những món mình thích thì gọi là 'Miệng ngắn - Kén ăn'. Và những người không kể chuyện mình nghe được từ người khác cho người khác thì được gọi là 'Miệng nặng - Kín tiếng'. Người miệng nhẹ thì mang ý nghĩa trái ngược. Vì vậy tốt hơn là không nên nói bí mật với những người miệng ㉠_________. Ngoài ra, khi thấy món ăn nào đó ngon, người ta sẽ nói là 'Món ăn vừa miệng (Món ăn hợp khẩu vị). Có cả biểu hiện bằng mắt hoặc chân. 'Mắt cao' chỉ người có quá nhiều mong ước. Và khi nói 'Bàn chân rộng' thì có nghĩa là người đó quen biết rất nhiều người ở nơi đây nơi đó.
g_text_translate.en: There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.it:
Ci sono molte espressioni legate al nostro corpo in coreano. Si dice che le persone che non mangiano bene qualsiasi cibo e mangiano solo ciò che vogliono abbiano una "bocca corta". E colui che non trasmette agli altri ciò che ha sentito viene chiamato "bocca pesante". Una persona dalla bocca leggera significa il contrario. Quindi è meglio non raccontare segreti a chi è ㉠ chiacchierone. Inoltre, quando un cibo ha un buon sapore, diciamo "il cibo si adatta alla bocca". Ci sono anche espressioni che usano occhi o piedi. Se dici "gli occhi sono alti", intendi una persona che vuole troppo. E quando dici "Ho i piedi larghi", significa che hai un sacco di persone che conosci dappertutto.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.fr:
Il existe de nombreuses expressions liées à notre corps en coréen. On dit que les personnes qui ne mangent pas bien et ne mangent que ce qu'elles aiment ont la «bouche courte». Et celui qui ne transmet pas ce qu'il a entendu aux autres est appelé « bouche lourde ». Une personne à la bouche légère signifie le contraire. Il vaut donc mieux ne pas dire de secrets aux gens qui ont la bouche légère. De plus, lorsque certains aliments ont bon goût, nous disons « la nourriture tient dans la bouche ». Il existe également des expressions utilisant les yeux ou les pieds. Si vous dites "les yeux sont levés", vous voulez dire une personne qui en veut trop. Et quand vous dites "j'ai les pieds larges", cela signifie que vous connaissez beaucoup de gens un peu partout.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.es:
Hay muchas expresiones relacionadas con nuestro cuerpo en coreano. Se dice que las personas que no comen bien ningún alimento y solo comen lo que les gusta tienen "boca corta". Y al que no transmite lo que escuchó a los demás se le llama 'boca pesada'. Una persona de boca ligera significa lo contrario. Así que es mejor no contar secretos a las personas que son ㉠ de boca ligera. Además, cuando alguna comida sabe bien, decimos 'la comida cabe en la boca'. También hay expresiones que usan ojos o pies. Si dices 'ojos altos', te refieres a una persona que quiere demasiado. Y cuando dices 'tengo los pies anchos', significa que tienes mucha gente que conoces por todas partes.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.id:
Ada banyak ekspresi yang berhubungan dengan tubuh kita dalam bahasa Korea. Orang yang tidak makan makanan dengan baik dan hanya makan apa yang mereka sukai dikatakan memiliki 'mulut pendek'. Dan orang yang tidak menyampaikan apa yang didengarnya kepada orang lain disebut 'mulut berat'. Orang yang bermulut ringan berarti sebaliknya. Jadi lebih baik tidak membocorkan rahasia kepada orang yang ㉠ bermulut ringan. Juga, ketika beberapa makanan terasa enak, kita mengatakan 'makanannya pas di mulut'. Ada juga ekspresi yang menggunakan mata atau kaki. Jika Anda mengatakan 'mata tinggi', maksud Anda adalah orang yang terlalu banyak menginginkan. Dan ketika Anda mengatakan 'Saya memiliki kaki yang lebar', itu berarti Anda memiliki banyak orang yang Anda kenal di mana-mana.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.ja:
韓国語には体に関する表現がたくさんあります。食べ物が苦手で、好きなものしか食べない人は「口が短い」と言われています。そして、聞いたことを他人に伝えない人を「ヘヴィマウス」と呼びます。口が軽い人はその逆です。だから㉠口が軽い人には内緒は言わないほうがいい。また、食べ物がおいしいときは「口に合う」と言います。目や足を使った表現もあります。 「目が高い」と言えば、欲張りすぎる人。そして、「私は広い足を持っています」と言うとき、それはあなたが知っている人がいたるところにたくさんいることを意味します.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.de:
Auf Koreanisch gibt es viele Ausdrücke, die sich auf unseren Körper beziehen. Menschen, die nichts gut essen und nur das essen, was ihnen schmeckt, haben einen „kurzen Mund“. Und derjenige, der das, was er gehört hat, nicht weitergibt, wird als „Schwermaul“ bezeichnet. Eine Person mit leichtem Mund meint das Gegenteil. Also ist es besser, Leuten, die ㉠ leichtsinnig sind, keine Geheimnisse zu erzählen. Auch wenn etwas gut schmeckt, sagen wir „das Essen passt in den Mund“. Es gibt auch Ausdrücke mit Augen oder Füßen. Wenn Sie sagen, dass die Augen hoch sind, meinen Sie eine Person, die zu viel will. Und wenn du sagst „Ich habe breite Füße“, bedeutet das, dass du viele Leute hast, die du überall kennst.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
韩语中有很多与我们的身体有关的表达方式。什么食物都吃不好，只吃自己喜欢吃的人被称为“嘴巴短”。而不将所闻所言传于人者，谓之“重口”。轻言细语的人意思恰恰相反。所以最好不要把秘密告诉㉠ 轻嘴的人。此外，当某些食物味道不错时，我们会说“食物适合入口”。也有使用眼睛或脚的表情。如果你说“眼高手低”，你的意思是一个想要太多的人。当你说“我的脚很宽”时，这意味着你到处都认识很多人。

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
韓語中有很多與我們的身體有關的表達方式。什麼食物都吃不好，只吃自己喜歡吃的人被稱為“嘴巴短”。而不將所聞所言傳於人者，謂之“重口”。輕言細語的人意思恰恰相反。所以最好不要把秘密告訴㉠ 輕嘴的人。此外，當某些食物味道不錯時，我們會說“食物適合入口”。也有使用眼睛或腳的表情。如果你說“眼高手低”，你的意思是一個想要太多的人。當你說“我的腳很寬”時，這意味著你到處都認識很多人。

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.ru:
В корейском языке есть много выражений, связанных с нашим телом. О людях, которые плохо едят и едят только то, что им нравится, говорят, что у них «короткий рот». А того, кто не передает другим услышанное, называют «тяжелоговорящим». Легкомысленный человек означает обратное. Так что лучше не рассказывать секреты людям ㉠ легкоречивым. Кроме того, когда какая-то еда вкусная, мы говорим: «Еда помещается во рту». Есть также выражения с использованием глаз или ног. Если вы говорите «глаза высокие», вы имеете в виду человека, который хочет слишком многого. И когда вы говорите: «У меня широкие ноги», это означает, что у вас повсюду много знакомых.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.th:
มีสำนวนมากมายที่เกี่ยวข้องกับร่างกายของเราในภาษาเกาหลี คนที่กินอาหารไม่เก่งและกินเฉพาะสิ่งที่ชอบเท่านั้นที่กล่าวกันว่ามี 'ปากสั้น' และคนที่ไม่เล่าสิ่งที่ได้ยินให้คนอื่นฟังเรียกว่า 'ปากแข็ง' คนปากเบา แปลว่าตรงกันข้าม ดังนั้นอย่าบอกความลับแก่คนที่พูดเบาจะดีกว่า นอกจากนี้เมื่ออาหารบางอย่างมีรสชาติดี เราก็จะพูดว่า 'อาหารนั้นเข้าปาก' นอกจากนี้ยังมีสำนวนการใช้ตาหรือเท้าด้วย ถ้าพูดว่า 'ตาสูง' หมายถึงคนที่ต้องการมากเกินไป และเมื่อคุณพูดว่า 'ฉันมีเท้ากว้าง' นั่นหมายความว่าคุณมีผู้คนมากมายที่คุณรู้จักอยู่ทั่วทุกแห่ง

And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.pt:
Existem muitas expressões relacionadas ao nosso corpo em coreano. Diz-se que as pessoas que não comem bem e só comem o que gostam têm “boca curta”. E aquele que não transmite aos outros o que ouviu é chamado de ‘boca pesada’. Uma pessoa de boca leve significa o oposto. Então é melhor não contar segredos para pessoas que têm a boca leve. Além disso, quando alguma comida tem um gosto bom, dizemos “a comida cabe na boca”. Também existem expressões que usam olhos ou pés. Se você diz 'olhos estão altos', você quer dizer uma pessoa que quer demais. E quando você diz 'Eu tenho pés largos', significa que você tem muitas pessoas que conhece por todo o lugar.

#######

Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.hi:
कोरियाई भाषा में हमारे शरीर से जुड़े कई भाव मौजूद हैं। जो लोग कोई भी खाना अच्छे से नहीं खाते और केवल वही खाते हैं जो उन्हें पसंद है, उन्हें 'छोटा मुंह' कहा जाता है। और जो सुनी हुई बात दूसरों को नहीं बताता, उसे 'भारी मुँह' कहा जाता है। हल्के-फुल्के व्यक्ति का मतलब विपरीत होता है। इसलिए बेहतर है कि ऐसे लोगों को रहस्य न बताएं जो हल्के-फुल्के हों। इसके अलावा, जब कुछ भोजन का स्वाद अच्छा होता है, तो हम कहते हैं 'खाना मुँह में फिट बैठता है'। आँखों या पैरों का उपयोग करके भी अभिव्यक्तियाँ होती हैं। यदि आप कहते हैं 'आँखें ऊंची हैं', तो आपका मतलब एक ऐसा व्यक्ति है जो बहुत अधिक चाहता है। और जब आप कहते हैं 'मेरे पैर चौड़े हैं', तो इसका मतलब है कि आपके पास हर जगह बहुत सारे लोग हैं जिन्हें आप जानते हैं।

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.tr:
Korece'de vücudumuzla ilgili birçok ifade vardır. Hiçbir yemeği iyi yemeyen ve sadece sevdiği şeyleri yiyen kişilerin 'ağızlarının kısa' olduğu söylenir. Duyduğunu başkalarına aktarmayana da 'ağız ağızlı' denir. Hafif ağızlı bir kişi tam tersi anlamına gelir. Bu yüzden hafif ağızlı insanlara sırlarını söylememek daha iyidir. Ayrıca bazı yiyeceklerin tadı güzel olunca 'yemek ağza sığar' deriz. Gözlerin veya ayakların kullanıldığı ifadeler de vardır. 'Gözler yukarıda' diyorsan, çok fazla şey isteyen bir insanı kastediyorsun. Ve 'Ayaklarım geniş' dediğinizde, her yerde tanıdığınız birçok insan var demektir.

#######

There are many expressions related to our body in Korean. People who don't eat any food well and only eat what they like are said to have a 'short mouth'. And the one who doesn't convey what he heard to others is called 'heavy mouth'. A light-mouthed person means the opposite. So it's better not to tell secrets to people who are ㉠ light-mouthed. Also, when some food tastes good, we say 'the food fits in the mouth'. There are also expressions using eyes or feet. If you say 'eyes are high', you mean a person who wants too much. And when you say 'I have wide feet', it means that you have a lot of people you know all over the place.
g_text_translate.ne: कोरियालीमा हाम्रो शरीरसँग सम्बन्धित धेरै अभिव्यक्तिहरू छन्। कुनै पनि खानेकुरा राम्ररी नखाने र आफूलाई मन पर्ने खानेकुरा मात्र खाने मानिसलाई ‘छोटो मुख’ भनिन्छ। र जसले सुनेको कुरा अरूलाई नपुगाउने उसलाई 'भारी मुख' भनिन्छ। हल्का मुख भएको व्यक्तिको अर्थ उल्टो हुन्छ। त्यसैले हलुका मुख भएका मानिसहरूलाई गोप्य कुरा नबताउनु राम्रो हुन्छ। साथै, जब कुनै खानाको स्वाद राम्रो हुन्छ, हामी भन्छौं 'खाना मुखमा फिट हुन्छ'। त्यहाँ आँखा वा खुट्टा प्रयोग गरेर अभिव्यक्तिहरू पनि छन्। यदि तपाईंले 'आँखा उच्च छन्' भन्नुभयो भने, तपाईंले धेरै चाहने व्यक्तिलाई भन्नुहुन्छ। र जब तपाइँ 'मेरो खुट्टा फराकिलो छ' भन्नुहुन्छ, यसको मतलब यो हो कि तपाइँ सबै ठाउँमा चिनेका धेरै व्यक्तिहरू छन्।
g_text_translate.km: មានកន្សោមជាច្រើនដែលទាក់ទងនឹងរាងកាយរបស់យើងជាភាសាកូរ៉េ។ អ្នក​ដែល​ញ៉ាំ​អាហារ​មិន​បាន​ល្អ ហើយ​ញ៉ាំ​តែ​អ្វី​ដែល​ខ្លួន​ចូលចិត្ត​ត្រូវ​បាន​គេ​និយាយ​ថា​មាន​មាត់​ខ្លី។ ហើយ​អ្នក​ណា​ដែល​មិន​បញ្ចេញ​នូវ​អ្វី​ដែល​ខ្លួន​បាន​ឮ​ដល់​អ្នក​ដទៃ​នោះ​គេ​ហៅ​ថា​មាត់​ធ្ងន់។ មនុស្ស​មាត់​ស្រាល​មាន​ន័យ​ផ្ទុយ។ ដូច្នេះ យក​ល្អ​កុំ​ប្រាប់​អាថ៌កំបាំង​ដល់​មនុស្ស​ដែល​ស្លូតបូត។ ម្យ៉ាងទៀត នៅពេលដែលអាហារខ្លះមានរសជាតិឆ្ងាញ់ យើងនិយាយថា 'អាហារសមនឹងមាត់' ។ វាក៏មានកន្សោមដោយប្រើភ្នែកឬជើងផងដែរ។ ប្រសិនបើអ្នកនិយាយថា "ភ្នែកខ្ពស់" អ្នកមានន័យថាមនុស្សដែលចង់បានច្រើនពេក។ ហើយនៅពេលដែលអ្នកនិយាយថា "ខ្ញុំមានជើងធំទូលាយ" វាមានន័យថាអ្នកមានមនុស្សជាច្រើនដែលអ្នកស្គាល់គ្រប់ទីកន្លែង។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියානු භාෂාවෙන් අපගේ ශරීරය හා සම්බන්ධ බොහෝ යෙදුම් තිබේ. කිසිම කෑමක් හරියට නොකා තමන් කැමති දේ පමණක් කන මිනිසුන්ට ‘කොට කටක්’ ඇත. තවද තමා ඇසූ දෙය අන් අයට නොපෙන්වන තැනැත්තාට 'බර කට' යැයි කියනු ලැබේ. සැහැල්ලු මුඛයක් ඇති පුද්ගලයා යනු ප්රතිවිරුද්ධයයි. ඒ නිසා ㉠ සැහැල්ලුවෙන් ඉන්න අයට රහස් නොකියා ඉන්න එක හොඳයි. ඒ වගේම සමහර කෑමවල රස ඇති වුණාම අපි කියනවා ‘කෑම කටට ගැලපෙනවා’ කියලා. ඇස් හෝ පාද භාවිතා කරන ප්‍රකාශන ද ඇත. ‘ඇස් උස්’ කිව්වොත් ඕනෑවට වඩා ඕනෑ කරන කෙනෙක්. ඒවගේම ‘මට පළල් පාද තියෙනවා’ කිව්වම ඒ කියන්නේ ඔයා දන්න කියන මිනිස්සු ගොඩක් තැන්වල ඉන්නවා.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়ান ভাষায় আমাদের শরীরের সাথে সম্পর্কিত অনেক অভিব্যক্তি রয়েছে। যে সমস্ত লোক ভালভাবে কোন খাবার খান না এবং শুধুমাত্র যা পছন্দ করেন তা খান তাদের 'খাটো মুখ' বলা হয়। আর যে শুনেছে তা অন্যের কাছে পৌঁছে দেয় না তাকে বলা হয় 'ভারী মুখ'। হালকা মুখের ব্যক্তি মানে বিপরীত। তাই হালকা মুখের লোকদের গোপন কথা না বলাই ভালো। এছাড়াও, যখন কিছু খাবারের স্বাদ ভাল হয়, তখন আমরা বলি 'খাবার মুখে মানায়'। এছাড়াও চোখ বা পা ব্যবহার করে অভিব্যক্তি আছে। আপনি যদি বলেন 'চোখ উঁচু', তাহলে আপনি এমন একজন ব্যক্তিকে বোঝাবেন যিনি খুব বেশি চান। এবং যখন আপনি বলেন 'আমার চওড়া পা আছে', এর মানে হল যে সমস্ত জায়গায় আপনার পরিচিত অনেক লোক রয়েছে।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားဘာသာစကားတွင် ကျွန်ုပ်တို့၏ခန္ဓာကိုယ်နှင့် ပတ်သက်သည့်အသုံးအနှုန်းများစွာရှိသည်။ အစာကောင်းကောင်းမစားဘဲ သူတို့ကြိုက်တာကိုပဲ စားတဲ့သူတွေက ပါးစပ်တိုတယ်လို့ ပြောကြတယ်။ သူတပါးကြားသမျှကို မပြောပြတတ်သူကို 'ပါးစပ်ကြီး' ဟုခေါ်သည်။ ပါးစပ် ပေါ့ပါးသူ ဆိုသည်မှာ ဆန့်ကျင်ဘက် ဖြစ်သည်။ ဒါကြောင့် ㉠ပါးစပ် ပေါ့ပါးတဲ့သူတွေကို လျှို့ဝှက်ချက်တွေ မပြောတာက ပိုကောင်းပါတယ်။ တစ်ချို့အစားအစာတွေက အရသာကောင်းတဲ့အခါ 'အစာက ပါးစပ်ထဲမှာ အံဝင်တယ်' လို့ပြောကြတယ်။ မျက်လုံး သို့မဟုတ် ခြေထောက်ကို အသုံးပြုသည့် အသုံးအနှုန်းများလည်း ရှိသည်။ 'မျက်လုံးက မြင့်တယ်' လို့ပြောရင် အရမ်းလိုချင်တဲ့လူကို ဆိုလိုတာပါ။ 'ကျွန်ုပ်မှာ ခြေထောက်ကျယ်တယ်' လို့ ပြောရင် နေရာအနှံ့မှာ သင်သိတဲ့လူတွေ အများကြီးရှိတယ်ဆိုတာပါပဲ။

content.q_text: 빈칸 ㉠에 들어갈 말로 알맞은 것을 고르십시오.

explain.vi:
Điền cụm từ thích hợp vào ㉠.

1. Ngắn
2. Cao
3. Nhẹ
4. Nặng
--------------------
입이 가볍다: Miệng nhẹ, lẻo mép, bép xép

A + (으)ㄴ + N: Định ngữ của tính từ.

Người miệng nặng là người kín tiếng, người miệng nhẹ thì ngược lại nên đáp án đúng là 2.
explain.en:
Choose the appropriate word to fill in the blank <g>㉠</g>.

1. Short
2. Tall
3. Light
4. Heavy
--------------------
<g>입이 가볍다:</g> To have a light mouth, to be big-mouthed

<g>A + (으)ㄴ + N:</g> Noun modifier (for adjectives).

A person with a heavy mouth is a quiet person, a person with a light mouth is the opposite, so the correct answer is 2.

<g>뜻:</g> अर्थ

<g>N + (이)나:</g> वा।
<g>V/A + (으)면:</g> यदि।

पाठको सामग्री शरीरका अंगहरू (मुख, हात, खुट्टा) सँग सम्बन्धित अभिव्यक्तिहरूको उदाहरण र व्याख्या हो, त्यसैले सही उत्तर 1 हो।
explain.km:
តើអត្ថបទនេះពន្យល់អ្វី?

1. កន្សោមភាសាកូរ៉េទាក់ទងនឹងរាងកាយ
2. លក្ខណៈរបស់ជនជាតិកូរ៉េដែលបង្ហាញជាភាសាកូរ៉េ
3. អាហារកូរ៉េដែលសាកសមនឹងរសជាតិរបស់ជនបរទេស
៤.សុជីវធម៌​កូរ៉េ​ត្រូវ​ធ្វើ​តាម​ចំពោះ​មុខ​មនុស្ស​ធំ
--------------------
<g>표현៖</g> កន្សោម
<g>뜻:</g> អត្ថន័យ

<g>N + (이)나:</g> ឬ។
<g>V/A+ (으)면:</g> ប្រសិនបើ។

ខ្លឹមសារនៃអត្ថបទគឺជាឧទាហរណ៍ និងការពន្យល់អំពីកន្សោមដែលទាក់ទងនឹងផ្នែករាងកាយ (មាត់ ដៃ ជើង) ដូច្នេះចម្លើយត្រឹមត្រូវគឺ ១.
explain.si:
මෙම පාඨය පැහැදිලි කරන්නේ කුමක්ද?

1. ශරීරයට සම්බන්ධ කොරියානු ප්‍රකාශන
2. කොරියානු භාෂාවෙන් ප්‍රකාශිත කොරියානුවන්ගේ ලක්ෂණ
3. විදේශිකයන්ගේ රසයට ගැලපෙන කොරියානු ආහාර
4. වැඩිහිටියන් ඉදිරියේ අනුගමනය කළ යුතු කොරියානු ආචාර විධි
----------------------
<g>표현:</g> ප්‍රකාශනය
<g>뜻:</g> අර්ථය

<g>N + (이)나:</g> හෝ.
<g>V/A + (으)면:</g> නම්.

පෙළෙහි අන්තර්ගතය ශරීර කොටස් (මුඛය, අත්, පාද) සම්බන්ධ ප්‍රකාශනවල උදාහරණය සහ පැහැදිලි කිරීම වේ, එබැවින් නිවැරදි පිළිතුර 1 වේ.
explain.bn:
এই টেক্সট ব্যাখ্যা কি?

1. শরীরের সাথে সম্পর্কিত কোরিয়ান অভিব্যক্তি
2. কোরিয়ান ভাষায় প্রকাশ করা কোরিয়ানদের বৈশিষ্ট্য
3. কোরিয়ান খাবার যা বিদেশীদের স্বাদের জন্য উপযুক্ত
4. প্রাপ্তবয়স্কদের সামনে অনুসরণ করার জন্য কোরিয়ান শিষ্টাচার
------------------
<g>표현:</g> অভিব্যক্তি
<g>뜻:</g> অর্থ

<g>N + (이)나:</g> অথবা।
<g>V/A + (으)면:</g> যদি।

পাঠ্যের বিষয়বস্তু হল শরীরের অঙ্গ (মুখ, হাত, পা) সম্পর্কিত অভিব্যক্তিগুলির উদাহরণ এবং ব্যাখ্যা, তাই সঠিক উত্তর হল 1।

general.g_text: 서울과 부산 같은 큰 도시에서는 버스나 지하철을 탈 때 교통카드를 사용합니다. 버스나 지하철을 타고 내릴 때 단말기에 교통카드를 대면 자동으로 요금이 카드에서 빠져나갑니다. 교통카드는 지하철역이나 버스 정류장, 편의점 등에서 살 수 있으며 요금을 다 쓴 후에는 다시 충전해서 사용할 수 있습니다. 보통 교통카드는 신용카드나 전화카드와 같이 넓은 사각형으로 생겼지만, 요즘은 사용하기 편하고 보기에도 예쁜 여러 가지 모양의 교통카드가 나오고 있습니다. 교통카드를 사용하면 현금으로 낼 때보다 더 싸고 편리하기 때문에 대부분 이것을 사용합니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tại các thành phố lớn như Seoul và Busan, thẻ giao thông được sử dụng khi đi xe buýt hoặc đi tàu điện ngầm. Khi lên và xuống xe buýt hoặc tàu điện ngầm, tiền vé sẽ tự động được trừ vào thẻ khi chạm thẻ giao thông vào máy quẹt thẻ. Thẻ giao thông có thể được mua tại nhiều nơi như các ga tàu điện ngầm, bến xe bus và các cửa hàng tiện lợi, đồng thời có thể nạp tiền và sử dụng lại sau khi hết tiền trong thẻ. Thông thường, thẻ giao thông có hình tứ giác rộng như thẻ tín dụng hoặc thẻ điện thoại, nhưng ngày nay, thẻ giao thông với nhiều hình dạng thuận tiện cho việc sử dụng và trông đẹp mắt đang được ra mắt. Hầu hết mọi người sử dụng thẻ giao thông vì chi phí rẻ hơn và thuận tiện hơn so với thanh toán bằng tiền mặt.
g_text_translate.en: In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.it:
Nelle grandi città come Seul e Busan, le persone usano le tessere di trasporto quando prendono autobus o metropolitane. Quando sali o scendi dall'autobus o dalla metropolitana, metti la tua carta di trasporto sul lettore di carte e la tariffa viene automaticamente detratta dalla carta. Le carte di trasporto possono essere acquistate presso le stazioni della metropolitana, le fermate degli autobus, i minimarket, ecc. e possono essere ricaricate una volta esaurita la tariffa. Di solito, le carte di trasporto hanno la forma di quadrati larghi come carte di credito o schede telefoniche, ma oggigiorno le carte di trasporto sono disponibili in varie forme facili da usare e belle da vedere. La maggior parte delle persone usa le carte di trasporto perché sono più economiche e convenienti rispetto al pagamento in contanti.

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.fr:
Dans les grandes villes comme Séoul et Busan, les gens utilisent des cartes de transport lorsqu'ils prennent le bus ou le métro. Lorsque vous montez ou descendez du bus ou du métro, placez votre carte de transport sur le lecteur de carte et le tarif est automatiquement déduit de la carte. Les cartes de transport peuvent être achetées dans les stations de métro, les arrêts de bus, les dépanneurs, etc., et peuvent être rechargées une fois le tarif épuisé. Habituellement, les cartes de transport ont la forme de larges carrés comme les cartes de crédit ou les cartes téléphoniques, mais de nos jours, les cartes de transport se présentent sous différentes formes, faciles à utiliser et belles à regarder. La plupart des gens utilisent des cartes de transport car elles sont moins chères et plus pratiques que de payer en espèces.

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.es:
En ciudades grandes como Seúl y Busan, las personas usan tarjetas de transporte cuando toman autobuses o subterráneos. Cuando suba o baje del autobús o del metro, coloque su tarjeta de transporte en el lector de tarjetas y la tarifa se deducirá automáticamente de la tarjeta. Las tarjetas de transporte se pueden comprar en estaciones de metro, paradas de autobús, tiendas de conveniencia, etc., y se pueden recargar después de que se agote la tarifa. Por lo general, las tarjetas de transporte tienen la forma de cuadrados anchos como las tarjetas de crédito o las tarjetas telefónicas, pero en estos días las tarjetas de transporte vienen en varias formas que son fáciles de usar y hermosas a la vista. La mayoría de la gente usa tarjetas de transporte porque son más baratas y convenientes que pagar en efectivo.

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.id:
Di kota-kota besar seperti Seoul dan Busan, orang menggunakan kartu transportasi saat naik bus atau kereta bawah tanah. Saat Anda naik atau turun dari bus atau kereta bawah tanah, letakkan kartu transportasi Anda di pembaca kartu, dan ongkos akan dipotong secara otomatis dari kartu. Kartu transportasi dapat dibeli di stasiun kereta bawah tanah, halte bus, minimarket, dll., dan dapat diisi ulang setelah tarif habis. Biasanya kartu transportasi berbentuk kotak lebar seperti kartu kredit atau kartu telepon, namun belakangan ini kartu transportasi hadir dalam berbagai bentuk yang mudah digunakan dan indah dipandang. Kebanyakan orang menggunakan kartu transportasi karena lebih murah dan nyaman daripada membayar dengan uang tunai.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
ソウルや釜山のような大都市では、バスや地下鉄に乗るときに交通カードを使用します。バスや地下鉄の乗り降りの際に交通カードをカードリーダーにかざすと、自動的に運賃が引き落とされます。交通カードは、地下鉄の駅やバス停、コンビニエンスストアなどで購入でき、運賃を使い切った後にチャージすることができます。通常、交通系カードはクレジットカードやテレホンカードなどのように幅の広い正方形の形をしていますが、最近は使いやすく、見た目も美しい交通系カードがさまざまな形で登場しています。現金で支払うよりも安くて便利なため、ほとんどの人が交通カードを使用しています。

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.de:
In Großstädten wie Seoul und Busan verwenden die Menschen Transportkarten, wenn sie Busse oder U-Bahnen nehmen. Wenn Sie in den Bus oder die U-Bahn ein- oder aussteigen, legen Sie Ihre Transportkarte auf das Kartenlesegerät, und der Fahrpreis wird automatisch von der Karte abgezogen. Transportkarten können an U-Bahn-Stationen, Bushaltestellen, Lebensmittelgeschäften usw. gekauft und nach Verbrauch des Fahrpreises wieder aufgeladen werden. Normalerweise haben Fahrkarten die Form von breiten Quadraten wie Kreditkarten oder Telefonkarten, aber heutzutage gibt es Fahrkarten in verschiedenen Formen, die einfach zu verwenden und schön anzusehen sind. Die meisten Leute benutzen Transportkarten, weil sie billiger und bequemer sind als mit Bargeld zu bezahlen.

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在首尔和釜山等大城市，人们在乘坐公共汽车或地铁时使用交通卡。上下公交、地铁时，将交通卡放在读卡器上，车费自动从卡中扣除。交通卡可以在地铁站、公交站、便利店等处购买，车费用完后可以充值。通常，交通卡的形状像信用卡或电话卡一样的宽方形，但现在交通卡的形状多种多样，使用方便且外观漂亮。大多数人使用交通卡是因为它们比现金支付更便宜、更方便。

g_text_translate.ru:
В крупных городах, таких как Сеул и Пусан, люди пользуются транспортными картами, когда едут на автобусе или метро. Когда вы садитесь в автобус или метро или выходите из автобуса или метро, ​​поднесите транспортную карту к устройству считывания карт, и стоимость проезда автоматически спишется с карты. Транспортные карты можно приобрести на станциях метро, ​​автобусных остановках, в магазинах и т. д., а пополнить их можно после того, как будет израсходована плата за проезд. Обычно транспортные карты имеют форму широких квадратов, как кредитные карты или телефонные карты, но в наши дни транспортные карты бывают разных форм, которые просты в использовании и красивы на вид. Большинство людей используют транспортные карты, потому что они дешевле и удобнее, чем оплата наличными.

#######

Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. g_text_translate.th:
ในเมืองใหญ่ เช่น โซลและปูซาน ผู้คนใช้บัตรโดยสารเมื่อโดยสารรถประจำทางหรือรถไฟใต้ดิน เมื่อคุณขึ้นหรือลงรถบัสหรือรถไฟใต้ดิน ให้วางบัตรโดยสารของคุณบนเครื่องอ่านบัตร จากนั้นค่าโดยสารจะถูกหักออกจากบัตรโดยอัตโนมัติ บัตรโดยสารสามารถซื้อได้ที่สถานีรถไฟใต้ดิน ป้ายรถเมล์ ร้านสะดวกซื้อ ฯลฯ และสามารถเติมเงินได้หลังจากใช้ค่าโดยสารหมดแล้ว ปกติแล้วบัตรขนส่งจะมีรูปทรงสี่เหลี่ยมกว้างๆ เช่น บัตรเครดิตหรือบัตรโทรศัพท์ แต่ปัจจุบัน บัตรขนส่งมีรูปทรงต่างๆ ให้เลือกใช้ง่ายและดูสวยงาม คนส่วนใหญ่ใช้บัตรโดยสารเนื่องจากมีราคาถูกและสะดวกกว่าการชำระด้วยเงินสด

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.pt:
Em grandes cidades como Seul e Busan, as pessoas usam cartões de transporte quando pegam ônibus ou metrô. Ao entrar ou sair do ônibus ou metrô, coloque seu cartão de transporte no leitor de cartão e a tarifa será descontada automaticamente do cartão. Os cartões de transporte podem ser adquiridos em estações de metrô, pontos de ônibus, lojas de conveniência, etc., e podem ser recarregados após o esgotamento da tarifa. Normalmente, os cartões de transporte têm o formato de quadrados largos, como cartões de crédito ou cartões telefônicos, mas hoje em dia os cartões de transporte vêm em vários formatos que são fáceis de usar e bonitos de se ver. A maioria das pessoas usa cartões de transporte porque são mais baratos e convenientes do que pagar em dinheiro.

#######

Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.hi:
सियोल और बुसान जैसे बड़े शहरों में, लोग बसें या सबवे लेते समय परिवहन कार्ड का उपयोग करते हैं। जब आप बस या सबवे पर चढ़ते या उतरते हैं, तो अपना परिवहन कार्ड कार्ड रीडर पर रखें, और किराया स्वचालित रूप से कार्ड से काट लिया जाता है। परिवहन कार्ड सबवे स्टेशनों, बस स्टॉप, सुविधा स्टोर आदि पर खरीदे जा सकते हैं, और किराया समाप्त होने के बाद रिचार्ज किया जा सकता है। आमतौर पर, परिवहन कार्ड क्रेडिट कार्ड या फोन कार्ड जैसे चौड़े वर्गों के आकार के होते हैं, लेकिन आजकल परिवहन कार्ड विभिन्न आकारों में आते हैं जो उपयोग में आसान और देखने में सुंदर होते हैं। अधिकांश लोग परिवहन कार्ड का उपयोग करते हैं क्योंकि वे नकद से भुगतान करने की तुलना में सस्ते और अधिक सुविधाजनक होते हैं।

#######

In large cities such as Seoul and Busan, people use transportation cards when they take buses or subways. When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.tr:
Seul ve Busan gibi büyük şehirlerde insanlar otobüs veya metroya binerken ulaşım kartlarını kullanıyor. Otobüs veya metroya bindiğinizde veya indiğinizde ulaşım kartınızı kart okuyucuya takın, ücret otomatik olarak karttan düşülsün. Ulaşım kartları metro istasyonları, otobüs durakları, marketler vb. yerlerden satın alınabilir ve ücret bittikten sonra yeniden yüklenebilir. Genellikle ulaşım kartları, kredi kartı veya telefon kartı gibi geniş kare şeklindedir ancak günümüzde ulaşım kartları, kullanımı kolay ve görünümü güzel olan çeşitli şekillerde sunulmaktadır. Çoğu kişi ulaşım kartlarını nakit ödemeye göre daha ucuz ve daha uygun olduğu için kullanıyor.

#######

When you get on or off the bus or subway, put your transportation card on the card reader, and the fare is automatically deducted from the card. Transportation cards can be purchased at subway stations, bus stops, convenience stores, etc., and can be recharged after the fare is used up. Usually, transportation cards are shaped like wide squares like credit cards or phone cards, but these days transportation cards come in various shapes that are easy to use and beautiful to look at. Most people use transportation cards because they are cheaper and more convenient than paying with cash.
g_text_translate.ne: सियोल र बुसान जस्ता ठूला शहरहरूमा, मानिसहरूले बस वा सबवे चढ्दा यातायात कार्डहरू प्रयोग गर्छन्। जब तपाईं बस वा सबवेमा चढ्नुहुन्छ वा बाहिर जानुहुन्छ, आफ्नो यातायात कार्ड कार्ड रिडरमा राख्नुहोस्, र भाडा स्वतः कार्डबाट कटौती हुन्छ। यातायात कार्डहरू सबवे स्टेशनहरू, बस स्टपहरू, सुविधा स्टोरहरू, आदिमा खरिद गर्न सकिन्छ, र भाडा प्रयोग गरिसकेपछि रिचार्ज गर्न सकिन्छ। सामान्यतया, ट्राफिक कार्डहरू क्रेडिट कार्ड वा फोन कार्डहरू जस्तै चौडा वर्गहरू जस्तै आकारका हुन्छन्, तर आजकल यातायात कार्डहरू विभिन्न आकारहरूमा आउँछन् जुन प्रयोग गर्न सजिलो छ र हेर्नको लागि सुन्दर छ। धेरै मानिसहरूले यातायात कार्डहरू प्रयोग गर्छन् किनभने तिनीहरू सस्तो र नगदको साथ भुक्तानी भन्दा बढी सुविधाजनक छन्।
g_text_translate.km: នៅតាមទីក្រុងធំៗដូចជា សេអ៊ូល និងប៊ូសាន មនុស្សប្រើប្រាស់ប័ណ្ណដឹកជញ្ជូននៅពេលពួកគេជិះឡានក្រុង ឬរថភ្លើងក្រោមដី។ នៅពេលអ្នកឡើង ឬចុះពីឡានក្រុង ឬរថភ្លើងក្រោមដី សូមដាក់កាតដឹកជញ្ជូនរបស់អ្នកនៅលើឧបករណ៍អានកាត ហើយតម្លៃសំបុត្រត្រូវបានកាត់ចេញពីកាតដោយស្វ័យប្រវត្តិ។ ប័ណ្ណដឹកជញ្ជូនអាចទិញបាននៅស្ថានីយ៍រថភ្លើងក្រោមដី ចំណតឡានក្រុង ហាងលក់ទំនិញផ្សេងៗ។ល។ ហើយអាចបញ្ចូលទឹកប្រាក់ឡើងវិញបានបន្ទាប់ពីថ្លៃសំបុត្រប្រើប្រាស់អស់ហើយ។ ជាធម្មតា ប័ណ្ណដឹកជញ្ជូនមានទម្រង់ជាការ៉េធំទូលាយ ដូចជាកាតឥណទាន ឬកាតទូរសព្ទ ប៉ុន្តែសព្វថ្ងៃនេះ ប័ណ្ណដឹកជញ្ជូនមានរូបរាងផ្សេងៗគ្នា ដែលងាយស្រួលប្រើ និងស្រស់ស្អាតក្នុងការមើល។ មនុស្សភាគច្រើនប្រើប្រាស់ប័ណ្ណដឹកជញ្ជូន ព្រោះវាមានតម្លៃថោក និងងាយស្រួលជាងការបង់ប្រាក់ជាសាច់ប្រាក់។
g_text_translate.si: සෝල් සහ බුසාන් වැනි විශාල නගරවල මිනිසුන් බස් රථ හෝ උමං මාර්ගවල ගමන් කරන විට ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත් භාවිතා කරයි. ඔබ බසයට හෝ උමං මාර්ගයට ගොඩ වන විට හෝ බැස යන විට, ඔබේ ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත කාඩ්පත් කියවනය මත තබන්න, එවිට ගාස්තුව ස්වයංක්‍රීයව කාඩ්පතෙන් අඩු වේ. ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත් උමං දුම්රිය ස්ථාන, බස් නැවතුම්පළ, පහසුව සඳහා වෙළඳසැල් ආදියෙන් මිලදී ගත හැකි අතර ගාස්තුව අවසන් වූ පසු නැවත ආරෝපණය කළ හැකිය. සාමාන්‍යයෙන් ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත් ක්‍රෙඩිට් කාඩ්පත් හෝ දුරකථන කාඩ්පත් වැනි පුළුල් චතුරස්‍රවල හැඩයෙන් යුක්ත වන නමුත් වර්තමානයේ ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත් භාවිතා කිරීමට පහසු සහ බැලීමට අලංකාර ලෙස විවිධ හැඩයන්ගෙන් යුක්ත වේ. බොහෝ අය ප්‍රවාහන කාඩ්පත් භාවිතා කරන්නේ ඒවා මුදල් වලින් ගෙවීමට වඩා ලාභදායී සහ පහසු බැවිනි.
g_text_translate.bn: সিউল এবং বুসানের মতো বড় শহরগুলিতে, লোকেরা বাস বা সাবওয়েতে যাওয়ার সময় পরিবহন কার্ড ব্যবহার করে। আপনি যখন বাস বা পাতাল রেলে উঠবেন বা নামবেন, তখন আপনার পরিবহন কার্ড কার্ড রিডারে রাখুন এবং ভাড়া স্বয়ংক্রিয়ভাবে কার্ড থেকে কেটে নেওয়া হবে। ট্রান্সপোর্টেশন কার্ডগুলি সাবওয়ে স্টেশন, বাস স্টপ, কনভিনিয়েন্স স্টোর ইত্যাদিতে কেনা যেতে পারে এবং ভাড়া শেষ হওয়ার পরে রিচার্জ করা যেতে পারে। সাধারণত, পরিবহন কার্ডগুলি ক্রেডিট কার্ড বা ফোন কার্ডের মতো চওড়া স্কোয়ারের মতো আকৃতির হয়, কিন্তু আজকাল পরিবহন কার্ডগুলি বিভিন্ন আকারে আসে যা ব্যবহার করা সহজ এবং দেখতে সুন্দর। বেশিরভাগ লোক পরিবহন কার্ড ব্যবহার করে কারণ তারা নগদ অর্থ প্রদানের চেয়ে সস্তা এবং আরও সুবিধাজনক।
g_text_translate.my: ဆိုးလ်နှင့် ဘူဆန်ကဲ့သို့သော မြို့ကြီးများတွင် လူများသည် ဘတ်စ်ကား သို့မဟုတ် မြေအောက်ရထားများကို စီးသည့်အခါ သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်များကို အသုံးပြုကြသည်။ ဘတ်စ်ကား သို့မဟုတ် မြေအောက်ရထားပေါ် သို့မဟုတ် ဆင်းသည့်အခါတွင် သင်၏ သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်ကို ကတ်ဖတ်စက်တွင် ထားကာ ယာဉ်စီးခကို ကတ်မှ အလိုအလျောက် နုတ်ယူမည်ဖြစ်သည်။ သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်များကို မြေအောက်ရထားဘူတာများ၊ ဘတ်စ်ကားမှတ်တိုင်များ၊ ကုန်စုံစတိုးဆိုင်များ စသည်တို့တွင် ဝယ်ယူနိုင်ပြီး ယာဉ်စီးခကုန်ပြီးနောက် ပြန်လည်အားသွင်းနိုင်ပါသည်။ အများအားဖြင့်၊ သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်များသည် ခရက်ဒစ်ကတ်များ သို့မဟုတ် ဖုန်းကတ်များကဲ့သို့ ကျယ်ပြန့်သောစတုရန်းပုံသဏ္ဍာန်ဖြစ်သော်လည်း ယနေ့ခေတ် သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်များသည် ကြည့်ရှုရလွယ်ကူပြီး လှပသောပုံစံအမျိုးမျိုးဖြင့် ရောက်ရှိလာပါသည်။ လူအများစုသည် သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးကတ်များကို ငွေသားဖြင့်ပေးဆောင်ခြင်းထက် စျေးသက်သာပြီး ပိုမိုအဆင်ပြေသောကြောင့် အသုံးပြုကြသည်။

content.q_text: 이 글을 읽고 알 수 없는 것은 무엇입니까?

explain.vi:
Điều không thể biết được từ đoạn văn này là gì?

1. Giá của thẻ giao thông
2. Hình dạng của thẻ giao thông
3. Cách sử dụng của thẻ giao thông
4. Ưu điểm của việc sử dụng thẻ giao thông
--------------------
단말기: Thiết bị đầu cuối, máy đọc/quẹt thẻ
장점: Ưu điểm, điểm tốt
빠져나가다: Thoát ra khỏi

general.g_text: 저는 어제 퇴근할 때 지하철을 탔습니다. 비가 와서 그런지 지하철 안은 복잡했습니다. 그런데 내릴 때 가방을 지하철 선반 위에 두고 우산만 들고 내렸습니다. 집에 도착해서 지하철 분실물 센터에 전화를 걸었습니다. “여보세요? 분실물 센터지요? 1호선 지하철을 탔는데요. 가방을 두고 내렸어요. 가방의 색깔은 갈색이고, 크기는 30cm, 50cm쯤 됩니다. 그런 가방이 있어요?”분실물 센터 직원은“아직 그런 가방은 들어오지 않았는데요. 들어오면 연락 드리겠습니다. 성함과 전화번호를 알려 주시겠어요?”라고 했습니다. 저는 이름과 전화번호를 알려 주고 전화를 끊었습니다. 제 가방을 꼭 찾을 수 있었으면 좋겠습니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Hôm qua khi tan làm tôi đi tàu điện ngầm. Có lẽ vì trời mưa nên tàu điện ngầm rất đông đúc. Rồi khi xuống tàu, tôi để lại túi trên kệ tàu điện ngầm và chỉ mang chiếc ô xuống. Về đến nhà, tôi gọi đến trung tâm đồ thất lạc trên tàu điện ngầm. "A lô? Trung tâm đồ thất lạc đúng không ạ? Tôi đã đi tàu điện ngầm tuyến 1. Tôi đã để quên túi xách của mình. Túi có màu nâu và kích thước khoảng 30cm, 50cm. Có chiếc túi nào như vậy không ạ?" Nhân viên trung tâm đồ thất lạc nói: "Vẫn chưa có chiếc túi nào như thế ạ. Nếu có chúng tôi sẽ liên lạc với bạn. Bạn có thể cho tôi biết tên và số điện thoại của bạn được không?" Tôi cho biết tên và số điện thoại rồi cúp máy. Tôi hy vọng có thể tìm thấy túi của mình.
g_text_translate.en: I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.it:
Ho preso la metropolitana quando sono uscito dal lavoro ieri. Forse a causa della pioggia, la metropolitana era complicata. Ma quando sono sceso, ho lasciato la borsa sullo scaffale della metropolitana e sono sceso con solo l'ombrello. Ho chiamato la metropolitana oggetti smarriti quando sono tornato a casa. "Pronto? È questo il centro oggetti smarriti? Ho preso la metropolitana sulla linea 1. Ho lasciato la mia borsa. Il colore della borsa è marrone e la dimensione è di circa 30 cm e 50 cm. Esiste una borsa così?" Il personale del centro oggetti smarriti ha detto: "Non abbiamo ancora ricevuto una borsa del genere. Ti chiamo quando arriva. Posso avere il tuo nome e numero di telefono?" Ho dato il mio nome e numero di telefono e ho riattaccato. Spero di trovare la mia borsa.

#######

I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.fr:
J'ai pris le métro en sortant du travail hier. Peut-être à cause de la pluie, le métro était compliqué. Mais quand je suis descendu, j'ai laissé mon sac sur l'étagère du métro et je suis descendu avec seulement le parapluie. J'ai appelé le métro perdu et trouvé quand je suis rentré. "Bonjour? Est-ce le centre des objets trouvés? J'ai pris le métro sur la ligne 1. J'ai laissé mon sac. La couleur du sac est marron et la taille est d'environ 30 cm et 50 cm. Existe-t-il un sac comme ça?" Le personnel du centre des objets trouvés a dit : « Nous n'avons pas encore reçu un tel sac. Je vous appellerai quand il arrivera. Puis-je avoir votre nom et votre numéro de téléphone ? J'ai donné mon nom et mon numéro de téléphone et j'ai raccroché. J'espère que je pourrai trouver mon sac.

#######

g_text_translate.es:
Tomé el metro cuando salí del trabajo ayer. Quizás por la lluvia, el metro se complicó. Pero cuando me bajé, dejé mi bolso en el estante del metro y me bajé solo con el paraguas. Llamé al metro perdido y encontrado cuando llegué a casa. "¿Hola? ¿Este es el centro de objetos perdidos? Tomé el metro en la Línea 1. Dejé mi bolso. El color del bolso es marrón y el tamaño es de unos 30 cm y 50 cm. ¿Hay un bolso así?" El personal del centro de objetos perdidos dijo: "Todavía no hemos recibido una bolsa así. Te llamaré cuando llegue. ¿Me puedes dar tu nombre y número de teléfono?". Di mi nombre y número de teléfono y colgué. Espero poder encontrar mi bolso.

g_text_translate.id:
Saya naik kereta bawah tanah ketika saya pulang kerja kemarin. Mungkin karena hujan, kereta bawah tanah menjadi rumit. Tetapi ketika saya turun, saya meninggalkan tas saya di rak kereta bawah tanah dan turun hanya dengan membawa payung. Saya menelepon kereta bawah tanah yang hilang dan ditemukan ketika saya sampai di rumah. "Halo? Apakah ini pusat yang hilang dan ditemukan? Saya naik kereta bawah tanah di Jalur 1. Saya meninggalkan tas saya. Warna tasnya coklat, dan ukurannya sekitar 30cm dan 50cm. Apakah ada tas seperti itu?" Staf Lost and Found Center berkata, "Kami belum menerima tas seperti itu. Saya akan menelepon Anda ketika sudah masuk. Bolehkah saya mengetahui nama dan nomor telepon Anda?" Saya memberikan nama dan nomor telepon saya dan menutup telepon. Saya harap saya dapat menemukan tas saya.

g_text_translate.ja:
昨日仕事が終わったとき、私は地下鉄に乗った。雨のせいか、地下鉄が混んでいました。でも、降りるときは地下鉄の棚にカバンを置いて、傘だけ持って降りました。帰宅後、地下鉄の落とし物に電話。 「こんにちは。ここが遺失物センターですか。地下鉄1号線で乗りました。カバンを置いてきました。カバンの色は茶色で、大きさは30cmと50cmくらいです。そのようなカバンはありますか？」遺失物センターの職員は「そのようなバッグはまだ届いていません。届きましたらお電話します。お名前と電話番号を教えていただけますか」とのことでした。名前と電話番号を告げて電話を切った。バッグが見つかることを願っています。

g_text_translate.de:
Ich bin gestern nach der Arbeit mit der U-Bahn gefahren. Vielleicht wegen des Regens war die U-Bahn kompliziert. Aber als ich ausstieg, ließ ich meine Tasche im U-Bahn-Regal und stieg nur mit dem Regenschirm aus. Ich rief die U-Bahn Lost and Found an, als ich nach Hause kam. „Hallo? Ist das das Fundbüro? Ich bin mit der U-Bahn Linie 1 gefahren. Ich habe meine Tasche dort gelassen. Die Farbe der Tasche ist braun und die Größe beträgt etwa 30 cm und 50 cm. Gibt es so eine Tasche?“ Die Mitarbeiter des Fundbüros sagten: „Wir haben noch keine solche Tasche erhalten. Ich rufe Sie an, wenn sie eintrifft. Darf ich Ihren Namen und Ihre Telefonnummer haben?“ Ich gab meinen Namen und meine Telefonnummer an und legte auf. Ich hoffe, ich finde meine Tasche.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我昨天下班时坐地铁。可能是下雨的缘故，地铁比较复杂。但是下车的时候，我把包落在了地铁架子上，只带了把雨伞就下车了。我打电话给地铁失而复得，回到家。 “你好？请问这里是失物招领处吗？我坐一号线的地铁，我把包落下了。包的颜色是棕色的，尺寸大概是30cm和50cm左右，有没有那种包？”失物招领中心的工作人员说：“我们还没有收到这样的包，等它来了我会打电话给你，请问你的姓名和电话号码？”我给了我的名字和电话号码然后挂断了电话。我希望我能找到我的包。

#######

g_text_translate.zh-TW:

g_text_translate.ru:
Я поехал на метро, ​​когда вчера пришел с работы. Возможно, из-за дождя движение в метро было затруднено. Но когда я вышел, я оставил свою сумку на полке в метро и отделался только зонтиком. Я позвонил в метро бюро находок, когда вернулся домой. "Здравствуйте? Это центр бюро находок? Я поехал на метро по 1 линии. Сумку оставил. Цвет сумки коричневый, размер примерно 30см и 50см. Есть такая сумка?" Сотрудники центра бюро находок сказали: «Такую сумку мы еще не получали. Я позвоню вам, когда она придет. Могу я узнать ваше имя и номер телефона?» Я назвал свое имя и номер телефона и повесил трубку. Надеюсь, я смогу найти свою сумку.

I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.th:
ฉันนั่งรถไฟใต้ดินเมื่อฉันเลิกงานเมื่อวานนี้ อาจเป็นเพราะฝนตก ทำให้รถไฟใต้ดินมีความซับซ้อน แต่เมื่อฉันลงจากรถ ฉันทิ้งกระเป๋าไว้บนชั้นวางรถไฟใต้ดินและลงโดยมีเพียงร่มเท่านั้น ฉันโทรหารถไฟใต้ดินที่หายไปและพบว่าเมื่อฉันกลับถึงบ้าน “สวัสดี? ที่นี่คือศูนย์ของหายหรือเปล่า ฉันนั่งรถไฟใต้ดินสาย 1 ทิ้งกระเป๋าไว้ กระเป๋าเป็นสีน้ำตาล ขนาดประมาณ 30 ซม. และ 50 ซม. มีกระเป๋าแบบนั้นไหม” เจ้าหน้าที่ศูนย์รับแจ้งของหายกล่าวว่า “เรายังไม่ได้รับกระเป๋าดังกล่าว เมื่อของมาถึงแล้วจะโทรไปขอชื่อและเบอร์โทรศัพท์ครับ” ฉันแจ้งชื่อและหมายเลขโทรศัพท์แล้ววางสาย ฉันหวังว่าฉันจะหากระเป๋าของฉันเจอ

#######

I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.pt:
Ontem peguei o metrô quando saí do trabalho. Talvez por causa da chuva o metrô tenha ficado complicado. Mas quando desci, deixei minha bolsa na prateleira do metrô e saí só com o guarda-chuva. Liguei para o metrô de achados e perdidos quando cheguei em casa. "Olá? Este é o centro de achados e perdidos? Peguei o metrô na Linha 1. Deixei minha bolsa. A cor da bolsa é marrom e o tamanho é cerca de 30cm e 50cm. Existe uma bolsa assim?" A equipe do centro de achados e perdidos disse: "Ainda não recebemos essa sacola. Ligo para você quando ela chegar. Posso saber seu nome e número de telefone?" Dei meu nome e telefone e desliguei. Espero encontrar minha bolsa.

#######

I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.hi:
कल जब मैं काम से निकला तो मैंने मेट्रो पकड़ी। शायद बारिश की वजह से मेट्रो में दिक्कत हो रही थी। लेकिन जब मैं उतरा, तो मैंने अपना बैग सबवे शेल्फ पर छोड़ दिया और केवल छाता लेकर उतर गया। मैंने सबवे को खोया हुआ कहा और जब मैं घर पहुंचा तो पाया। "हैलो? क्या यह खोया और पाया केंद्र है? मैंने लाइन 1 पर मेट्रो ली। मैंने अपना बैग छोड़ दिया। बैग का रंग भूरा है, और आकार लगभग 30 सेमी और 50 सेमी है। क्या ऐसा कोई बैग है?" खोया-पाया केंद्र के कर्मचारियों ने कहा, "हमें अभी तक ऐसा कोई बैग नहीं मिला है। जब वह आएगा तो मैं आपको फोन करूंगा। क्या मैं आपका नाम और फोन नंबर ले सकता हूं?" मैंने अपना नाम और फोन नंबर दिया और फोन रख दिया। मुझे आशा है कि मुझे अपना बैग मिल जाएगा।

#######

I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.tr:
Dün işten çıkınca metroya bindim. Belki yağmurdan dolayı metro karmaşıktı. Ama indiğimde çantamı metro rafına bıraktım ve elimde sadece şemsiyeyle indim. Eve geldiğimde kaybolup bulduğum metroyu aradım. "Alo? Burası kayıp eşya merkezi mi? 1. Hattan metroya bindim. Çantamı bıraktım. Çantanın rengi kahverengi, boyutu 30cm ve 50cm civarında. Böyle bir çanta var mı?" Kayıp eşya merkezi personeli, "Bize böyle bir çanta henüz ulaşmadı. Geldiğinde sizi arayacağım. Adınızı ve telefon numaranızı alabilir miyim?" Adımı ve telefon numaramı verip kapattım. Umarım çantamı bulabilirim.

#######

I took the subway when I got off work yesterday. Maybe because of the rain, the subway was complicated. But when I got off, I left my bag on the subway shelf and got off with only the umbrella. I called the subway lost and found when I got home. "Hello? Is this the lost and found center? I took the subway on Line 1. I left my bag. The color of the bag is brown, and the size is about 30cm and 50cm. Is there a bag like that?" The lost and found center staff said," We haven't received such a bag yet. I'll call you when it comes in. May I have your name and phone number?" I gave my name and phone number and hung up. I hope I can find my bag.
g_text_translate.ne: हिजो कामबाट छुट्दा मैले सबवे लिएँ। सायद वर्षाको कारण, सबवे जटिल थियो। तर जब म ओर्लें, मैले मेरो झोला सबवे शेल्फमा छोडें र छाता मात्र लिएर ओर्लें। मैले सबवे हराएको फोन गरें र घर पुग्दा भेटें। "नमस्कार? के यो हराएको र भेटिएको केन्द्र हो? मैले लाइन 1 मा सबवे लिएँ। मैले मेरो झोला छोडें। झोलाको रंग खैरो छ, र साइज लगभग 30 सेमी र 50 सेन्टीमिटर छ। यस्तो झोला छ?" हराएका र फेला परेका केन्द्रका कर्मचारीले भने, "अहिलेसम्म हामीले त्यस्तो झोला पाएका छैनौं। आएपछि फोन गर्छु। तपाईंको नाम र फोन नम्बर पाउन सक्छु?" मैले नाम र फोन नम्बर दिएँ र काटिदिएँ । मलाई आशा छ कि म मेरो झोला फेला पार्न सक्छु।
g_text_translate.km: ខ្ញុំបានជិះរថភ្លើងក្រោមដីពេលខ្ញុំចេញពីធ្វើការកាលពីម្សិលមិញ។ ប្រហែលជាដោយសារភ្លៀង រថភ្លើងក្រោមដីមានភាពស្មុគស្មាញ។ ប៉ុន្តែ​ពេល​ខ្ញុំ​ចុះ​មក ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ទុក​កាបូប​នៅ​លើ​ធ្នើរ​រថភ្លើង​ក្រោម​ដី ហើយ​ចុះ​ដោយ​មាន​តែ​ឆ័ត្រ។ ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ទូរស័ព្ទ​ទៅ​រថភ្លើង​ក្រោម​ដី​ដែល​បាន​បាត់ ហើយ​រក​ឃើញ​ពេល​ខ្ញុំ​មក​ដល់​ផ្ទះ។ "ជំរាបសួរ? នេះ​ជា​មជ្ឈមណ្ឌល​ដែល​បាត់​ហើយ​រក​ឃើញ? ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ជិះ​រថភ្លើង​ក្រោម​ដី​នៅ​ខ្សែ​ទី 1។ ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ទុក​កាបូប​របស់​ខ្ញុំ។ ពណ៌​នៃ​កាបូប​គឺ​ពណ៌​ត្នោត ហើយ​ទំហំ​ប្រហែល 30cm និង 50cm តើ​មាន​កាបូប​បែប​នោះ​ទេ?" បុគ្គលិក​មជ្ឈមណ្ឌល​ដែល​បាត់​និង​រក​ឃើញ​បាន​និយាយ​ថា "យើង​មិន​ទាន់​បាន​ទទួល​កាបូប​បែប​នេះ​នៅ​ឡើយ​ទេ ខ្ញុំ​នឹង​ទូរស័ព្ទ​ទៅ​អ្នក​ពេល​វា​ចូល​មក។ តើ​ខ្ញុំ​អាច​ដាក់​ឈ្មោះ និង​លេខ​ទូរសព្ទ​របស់​អ្នក​បាន​ទេ?" ខ្ញុំ​បាន​ដាក់​ឈ្មោះ​និង​លេខ​ទូរស័ព្ទ​របស់​ខ្ញុំ​ហើយ​បាន​ព្យួរ។ ខ្ញុំសង្ឃឹមថាខ្ញុំអាចរកឃើញកាបូបរបស់ខ្ញុំ។
g_text_translate.si: මම ඊයේ වැඩ ඇරිලා එනකොට උමං මාර්ගය ගත්තා. සමහර විට වර්ෂාව නිසා උමං මාර්ගය සංකීර්ණ විය. ඒත් බහිනකොට මම බෑග් එක උමං දුම්රිය රාක්කයේ තියලා කුඩේ විතරක් අරන් බැස්සා. මම උමං මාර්ගය නැති වූ අතර මම ගෙදර එන විට සොයා ගත්තා. "හෙලෝ? මේකද නැතිවෙලා හොයාගත්ත මධ්‍යස්ථානය? මම උමං මාර්ගය 1 ලයින් එකේ ගත්තා. මම මගේ බෑග් එක දාලා ගියා. බෑග් එකේ පාට දුඹුරු, සයිස් එක 30cm සහ 50cm වගේ. එහෙම බෑග් එකක් තියෙනවද?" නැතිවූ සහ සොයාගත් මධ්‍යස්ථාන කාර්ය මණ්ඩලය පැවසුවේ "අපට තවමත් එවැනි බෑගයක් ලැබී නැත. එය පැමිණි පසු මම ඔබට කතා කරන්නම්. මට ඔබේ නම සහ දුරකථන අංකය ලබා දෙන්න පුළුවන්ද?" මම නමයි දුරකථන අංකයයි දීලා ඇමතුම විසන්ධි කළා. මට මගේ බෑගය සොයා ගත හැකි යැයි මම බලාපොරොත්තු වෙමි.
g_text_translate.bn: আমি গতকাল কাজ বন্ধ করে পাতাল রেল নিলাম। হয়তো বৃষ্টির কারণে পাতাল রেল জটিল ছিল। কিন্তু যখন আমি নামলাম, তখন আমি আমার ব্যাগটি পাতাল রেলের শেলফে রেখেছিলাম এবং কেবল ছাতা নিয়েই নামলাম। আমি পাতাল রেল কল হারিয়ে এবং যখন আমি বাড়িতে ফিরে খুঁজে পেয়েছি. "হ্যালো? এটি কি হারিয়ে যাওয়া এবং খুঁজে পাওয়া কেন্দ্র? আমি লাইন 1 এ সাবওয়ে নিয়েছিলাম। আমি আমার ব্যাগ রেখে এসেছি। ব্যাগের রঙ বাদামী, এবং আকার প্রায় 30 সেমি এবং 50 সেমি। এরকম একটি ব্যাগ আছে?" হারিয়ে যাওয়া ও খুঁজে পাওয়া কেন্দ্রের কর্মীরা বলেন, "এ ধরনের ব্যাগ আমরা এখনো পাইনি। যখন আসবে তখন আমি আপনাকে কল করব। আমি কি আপনার নাম ও ফোন নম্বর পেতে পারি?" আমি আমার নাম ও ফোন নাম্বার দিয়ে ফোন কেটে দিলাম। আমি আশা করি আমি আমার ব্যাগ খুঁজে পেতে পারেন.
g_text_translate.my: မနေ့က အလုပ်ဆင်းချိန်မှာ မြေအောက်ရထားစီးခဲ့တယ်။ မိုးရွာတာကြောင့် မြေအောက်ရထားက ရှုပ်ထွေးပါတယ်။ ဒါပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော် ဆင်းပြီးချိန်မှာတော့ ကျွန်တော့်အိတ်ကို မြေအောက်ရထားစင်ပေါ် ထားခဲ့ပြီး ထီးတစ်လက်နဲ့ ဆင်းခဲ့ပါတယ်။ မြေအောက်ရထား ပျောက်သွားတာကို ဖုန်းဆက်ပြီး အိမ်ပြန်ရောက်တဲ့အခါ တွေ့တယ်။ "ဟဲလို၊ ဒါက ပျောက်ပြီး ရှာတွေ့တဲ့ စင်တာလား။ မြေအောက်ရထားလိုင်း 1 မှာ ငါစီးခဲ့တယ်။ အိတ်ကို ထားခဲ့လိုက်တယ်။ အိတ်ရဲ့အရောင်က အညိုရောင်ဖြစ်ပြီး အရွယ်အစားက 30cm နဲ့ 50cm လောက်ရှိတယ်။ အဲလိုအိတ်ရှိလား" ပျောက်ဆုံးသွားတဲ့ စင်တာက ဝန်ထမ်းက "အဲဒီလို အိတ်ကို မရသေးပါဘူး၊ ဝင်လာရင် ဖုန်းဆက်လိုက်မယ်။ မင်းနာမည်နဲ့ ဖုန်းနံပါတ် ရမလား" နာမည်နဲ့ ဖုန်းနံပါတ်ကို ပေးပြီး ဖုန်းချလိုက်တယ်။ ငါ့အိတ်ကို ရှာတွေ့နိုင်မယ်လို့ မျှော်လင့်ပါတယ်။

general.g_text: 이것은 한국의 ㉠________입니다. 이것은 한국을 나타냅니다. 이것은 한국의 얼굴과 같습니다. 각 나라마다 자기 나라의 ㉠________이/가 있습니다. 나라마다 ㉠________의 모양과 색이 다릅니다. 한국의 것은 가운데 원이 빨간색과 파란색으로 되어 있고, 바탕은 하얀색, 사방에 있는 4괘는 까만색으로 되어 있습니다. 여러분 나라의 ㉠________은/는 어떤 모양과 색으로 되어 있습니까?

g_text_translate.vi: Đây là ㉠________ của Hàn Quốc. Thứ này đại diện cho Hàn Quốc. Nó giống như bộ mặt của Hàn Quốc. Mỗi quốc gia sẽ có ㉠________ của quốc gia đó. g_text_translate.ne: यो कोरियाको <g>㉠</g> राष्ट्रिय झण्डा हो। यसले कोरियाको प्रतिनिधित्व गर्छ। यो कोरियाको अनुहार जस्तै हो। प्रत्येक देशको आफ्नै <g>㉠</g> राष्ट्रिय झण्डा हुन्छ। विभिन्न देशहरूमा विभिन्न <g>㉠</g> राष्ट्रिय झण्डाको आकार र रंगहरू छन्। कोरियालीको बीचमा रातो र नीलो वृत्त, सेतो पृष्ठभूमि र चारैतिर कालो ४ ट्रिग्राम हुन्छ। तपाईंको देशको <g>㉠</g> राष्ट्रिय झण्डा कस्तो आकार र रंग हो?
g_text_translate.km: នេះជាទង់ជាតិរបស់ប្រទេសកូរ៉េ <g>㉠</g>។ នេះតំណាងឱ្យប្រទេសកូរ៉េ។ មុខដូចកូរ៉េ ប្រទេសនីមួយៗមានទង់ជាតិ<g>㉠</g>របស់ខ្លួន។ ប្រទេសផ្សេងៗគ្នាមានរូបរាង និងពណ៌ទង់ជាតិ <g>㉠</g>ខុសៗគ្នា។ កូរ៉េ​មាន​រង្វង់​ពណ៌​ក្រហម និង​ខៀវ​នៅ​កណ្តាល ផ្ទៃ​ខាង​ក្រោយ​ពណ៌​ស និង​ត្រីកោណ​ពណ៌​ខ្មៅ ៤ នៅ​គ្រប់​ជ្រុង​ទាំងអស់។ តើទង់ជាតិ<g>㉠</g>របស់ប្រទេសរបស់អ្នកមានរូបរាង និងពណ៌អ្វី?
g_text_translate.si: මෙය කොරියාවේ <g>㉠</g>ජාතික ධජයයි. මෙය කොරියාව නියෝජනය කරයි. මේක හරියට කොරියාවේ මුහුණ වගේ. සෑම රටකටම තමන්ගේම <g>㉠</g>ජාතික ධජයක් ඇත. විවිධ රටවලට විවිධ <g>㉠</g>ජාතික කොඩි හැඩයන් සහ වර්ණ ඇත. කොරියානු එකේ මැද රතු සහ නිල් කවයක්, සුදු පසුබිමක් සහ කළු ත්‍රිග්‍රෑම් 4ක් සෑම පැත්තකින්ම ඇත. ඔබේ රටේ <g>㉠</g>ජාතික ධජයේ හැඩය සහ වර්ණය කුමක්ද?
g_text_translate.bn: এটি কোরিয়ার <g>㉠</g> জাতীয় পতাকা। এটি কোরিয়ার প্রতিনিধিত্ব করে। এ যেন কোরিয়ার চেহারা। প্রতিটি দেশের নিজস্ব <g>㉠</g> জাতীয় পতাকা রয়েছে। বিভিন্ন দেশের বিভিন্ন <g>㉠</g>জাতীয় পতাকার আকৃতি এবং রঙ রয়েছে। কোরিয়ানটির মাঝখানে একটি লাল এবং নীল বৃত্ত, একটি সাদা পটভূমি এবং চারদিকে কালো 4 ট্রিগ্রাম রয়েছে। আপনার দেশের <g>㉠</g> জাতীয় পতাকার আকৃতি এবং রঙ কি?
g_text_translate.my: ဤသည်မှာ <g>㉠</g>ကိုရီးယားနိုင်ငံ၏ အလံဖြစ်သည်။ ဒါက ကိုရီးယားကို ကိုယ်စားပြုတယ်။ ဒါက ကိုရီးယားမျက်နှာနဲ့တူတယ်။ နိုင်ငံတိုင်းတွင် ၎င်း၏<g>㉠</g>နိုင်ငံတော်အလံရှိသည်။ နိုင်ငံအသီးသီးတွင် မတူညီသော <g>㉠</g>နိုင်ငံတော်အလံပုံစံများနှင့် အရောင်များရှိသည်။ အလယ်တွင် အနီရောင်နှင့် အပြာရောင် စက်ဝိုင်းတစ်ခု၊ အဖြူနောက်ခံနှင့် အနက်ရောင် 4 trigrams ရှိသည်။ သင့်နိုင်ငံ၏ <g>㉠</g>နိုင်ငံအလံသည် မည်သည့်ပုံသဏ္ဍာန်နှင့်အရောင်ရှိသနည်း။

general.g_text: 저는 우즈베키스탄에서 온 누리끼입니다. 우리 나라의 수도 타슈켄트에는 지하철이 있습니다. 파란색, 빨간색, 초록색의 3개 노선이 있습니다. 우리 나라 사람들은 버스를 주로 타지만, 지하철을 타는 사람도 많습니다. 지하철을 탈 때에는 플라스틱으로 만든 동전 모양의 표를 사용합니다. 지하철 요금은 20 ‘숨’(SUM: 우즈베키스탄의 화폐 단위)입니다. 한국의 수도 서울에도 지하철이 있습니다. 서울 사람들은 버스보다 지하철을 더 많이타는 것 같습니다. 서울에는 현재 9개의 노선이 있습니다. 지하철을 타면 어디든지 빠르게 갈 수 있기 때문에 서울의 지하철은 매우 ㉠____________. 지하철을 탈 때에는 일회용 교통카드나 일반 교통카드를 이용합니다. 지하철 요금은 900원이지만 멀리갈 때는 더 비쌉니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi là Nuriki đến từ Uzbekistan. Tashkent, thủ đô của nước chúng tôi, có hệ thống tàu điện ngầm. Có 3 tuyến: xanh dương, đỏ và xanh lá cây. Người dân ở nước chúng ta chủ yếu sử dụng xe buýt, nhưng cũng có rất nhiều người đi tàu điện ngầm. Khi đi tàu điện ngầm, người ta sử dụng vé hình đồng xu làm bằng nhựa. Giá vé tàu điện ngầm là 20 'Sum' (SUM: đơn vị tiền tệ của Uzbekistan). Ở thủ đô Seoul của Hàn Quốc, cũng có hệ thống tàu điện ngầm. Người dân Seoul dường như đi tàu điện ngầm nhiều hơn xe buýt. Seoul hiện có 9 đường tàu. Tàu điện ngầm ở Seoul rất ㉠ ____________ vì bạn có thể đến bất cứ đâu nhanh chóng bằng cách đi tàu điện ngầm. Khi đi tàu điện ngầm, hãy sử dụng thẻ giao thông bình thường hoặc thẻ dùng một lần. Giá vé tàu điện ngầm là 900 won, nhưng sẽ đắt hơn nếu bạn đi xa.
g_text_translate.en: I am Nuriki from Uzbekistan. There is a subway system in Tashkent, the capital of our country. There are 3 routes: blue, red and green. People in my country mostly take the bus, but there are also many people who take the subway. When riding the subway, we use a coin-shaped ticket made of plastic. The subway fare is 20 ‘sum’ (SUM: monetary unit of Uzbekistan). Seoul, the capital of Korea, also has a subway system. It seems that Seoul people take the subway more than the bus. Seoul currently has 9 lines. The subway in Seoul is very <g>㉠</g>convenient because you can go anywhere quickly by taking the subway. When taking the subway, people use a disposable transportation card or a general transportation card. The subway fare is 900 won, but the farther you go, the more expensive it is.
g_text_translate.it:
Sono Nuriki dall'Uzbekistan. C'è un sistema di metropolitana a Tashkent, la capitale del nostro paese. Ci sono 3 percorsi: blu, rosso e verde. Le persone nel mio paese prendono principalmente l'autobus, ma ci sono anche molte persone che prendono la metropolitana. Quando andiamo in metropolitana, usiamo un biglietto di plastica a forma di moneta. La tariffa della metropolitana è di 20 "somma" (SOMMA: unità monetaria dell'Uzbekistan). Seoul, la capitale della Corea, ha anche un sistema di metropolitana. Sembra che la gente di Seoul prenda la metropolitana più che l'autobus. Seoul ha attualmente 9 linee. La metropolitana di Seoul è molto<g>㉠</g>comodo perché puoi andare ovunque velocemente prendendo la metropolitana. Quando prendono la metropolitana, le persone usano una carta di trasporto usa e getta o una carta di trasporto generale. La tariffa della metropolitana è di 900 won, ma più vai lontano, più è costoso.

#######

People in my country mostly take the bus, but there are also many people who take the subway. When riding the subway, we use a coin-shaped ticket made of plastic. The subway fare is 20 ‘sum’ (SUM: monetary unit of Uzbekistan). Seoul, the capital of Korea, also has a subway system. It seems that Seoul people take the subway more than the bus. Seoul currently has 9 lines. The subway in Seoul is very <g>㉠</g>convenient because you can go anywhere quickly by taking the subway. When taking the subway, people use a disposable transportation card or a general transportation card. The subway fare is 900 won, but the farther you go, the more expensive it is.
g_text_translate.fr:
Je suis Nuriki d'Ouzbékistan. Il y a un système de métro à Tachkent, la capitale de notre pays. Il y a 3 parcours : bleu, rouge et vert. Dans mon pays, les gens prennent surtout le bus, mais il y a aussi beaucoup de gens qui prennent le métro. Lorsque nous prenons le métro, nous utilisons un ticket en plastique en forme de pièce de monnaie. Le tarif du métro est de 20 "sum" (SUM : unité monétaire de l'Ouzbékistan). Séoul, la capitale de la Corée, dispose également d'un système de métro. Il semble que les Séouliens prennent plus le métro que le bus. Séoul compte actuellement 9 lignes. Le métro de Séoul est très<g>㉠</g>pratique car on peut aller n'importe où rapidement en prenant le métro. Lorsqu'ils prennent le métro, les gens utilisent une carte de transport jetable ou une carte de transport général. Le ticket de métro est de 900 wons, mais plus vous allez loin, plus c'est cher.
