韓服は韓国の伝統衣装です。女性の韓服は「チマチョゴリ」、男性の韓服は「バジチョゴリ」です。上に着るものがチョゴリ、下に着るものがスカートとパンツ。ハンボクはゆったりしていて体に張り付かないので快適です。また、季節によって生地が異なるため、夏の韓服は涼しく、冬の韓服は暖かいです。昔は白い韓服を着ていましたが、最近はカラフルで可愛らしい韓服を着ています。しかし、最近では韓服を着ることはあまりありません。元日や秋夕などの祝日、婚礼の日、祝祭日にのみ着用します。

g_text_translate.th:
ฮันบกเป็นเสื้อผ้าเกาหลีแบบดั้งเดิม ฮันบกของผู้หญิงคือ 'chima-jeogori' และฮันบกของผู้ชายคือ 'baji-jeogori' จอโกรีคือชุดที่คุณใส่ท่อนบน ส่วนกระโปรงและกางเกงคือชุดที่คุณใส่ท่อนล่าง ชุดฮันบกสวมใส่สบายเพราะหลวมและไม่ยึดติดกับลำตัว นอกจากนี้ เนื่องจากมีการใช้ผ้าที่แตกต่างกันไปขึ้นอยู่กับฤดูกาล ฮันบกในฤดูร้อนจึงเย็นสบาย และฮันบกในฤดูหนาวจึงอบอุ่น ในอดีตผู้คนมักจะสวมชุดฮันบกสีขาว แต่ทุกวันนี้ผู้คนจะสวมชุดฮันบกที่มีสีสันและสวยงาม แต่ปัจจุบันนี้ผู้คนมักไม่สวมชุดฮันบก จะสวมใส่เฉพาะในวันหยุด เช่น วันปีใหม่และวันชูซ็อก วันแต่งงาน และวันฉลองเท่านั้น

O Hanbok é confortável de trabalhar porque é solto e não gruda no corpo. Além disso, como são usados ​​tecidos diferentes dependendo da estação, o hanbok de verão é fresco e o hanbok de inverno é quente. No passado, as pessoas geralmente usavam hanbok branco, mas hoje em dia as pessoas usam hanbok colorido e bonito. Mas hoje em dia as pessoas não costumam usar hanbok. É usado apenas em feriados como Dia de Ano Novo e Chuseok, dia de casamento e dia de festa.

#######

Hanbok is a traditional Korean clothing. Women's hanbok is 'chima-jeogori' and men's hanbok is 'baji-jeogori'. Jeogori is what you wear on top, and skirt and pants are what you wear on the bottom. Hanbok is comfortable to work in because it is loose and does not stick to the body. Also, because different fabrics are used depending on the season, the summer hanbok is cool, and the winter hanbok is warm. In the past, people usually wore white hanbok, but these days, people wear colorful and pretty hanbok. But nowadays, people don't usually wear hanbok. It is only worn on holidays such as New Year's Day and Chuseok, wedding day, and feast day.
g_text_translate.hi:
हनबोक एक पारंपरिक कोरियाई परिधान है। महिलाओं का हनबोक 'चीमा-जोगोरी' है और पुरुषों का हनबोक 'बाजी-जोगोरी' है। जियोगोरी वह है जो आप ऊपर पहनते हैं, और स्कर्ट और पैंट वह है जो आप नीचे पहनते हैं। हनबॉक में काम करना आरामदायक है क्योंकि यह ढीला है और शरीर से चिपकता नहीं है। इसके अलावा, क्योंकि मौसम के आधार पर अलग-अलग कपड़ों का उपयोग किया जाता है, ग्रीष्मकालीन हैनबोक ठंडा होता है, और सर्दियों का हैनबोक गर्म होता है। पहले, लोग आमतौर पर सफेद हैनबोक पहनते थे, लेकिन आजकल, लोग रंगीन और सुंदर हैनबोक पहनते हैं। लेकिन आजकल, लोग आमतौर पर हैनबोक नहीं पहनते हैं। इसे केवल नए साल के दिन और चुसेओक, शादी के दिन और दावत के दिन जैसी छुट्टियों पर ही पहना जाता है।

#######

Hanbok is a traditional Korean clothing. Women's hanbok is 'chima-jeogori' and men's hanbok is 'baji-jeogori'. Jeogori is what you wear on top, and skirt and pants are what you wear on the bottom. Hanbok is comfortable to work in because it is loose and does not stick to the body. Also, because different fabrics are used depending on the season, the summer hanbok is cool, and the winter hanbok is warm. In the past, people usually wore white hanbok, but these days, people wear colorful and pretty hanbok. But nowadays, people don't usually wear hanbok. It is only worn on holidays such as New Year's Day and Chuseok, wedding day, and feast day.
g_text_translate.tr:
Hanbok geleneksel bir Kore giysisidir. Kadınların hanbok'u 'chima-jeogori', erkeklerin hanbok'u ise 'baji-jeogori'dir. Jeogori üstte giydiğiniz şeydir, etek ve pantolon ise altta giydiğiniz şeydir. Hanbok'un çalışması rahattır çünkü gevşektir ve vücuda yapışmaz. Ayrıca mevsime göre farklı kumaşlar kullanıldığı için yazlık hanbok serin, kışlık hanbok ise sıcaktır. Geçmişte insanlar genellikle beyaz hanbok giyerlerdi ama günümüzde insanlar rengarenk ve hoş hanbok giyiyorlar. Ancak günümüzde insanlar genellikle hanbok giymiyor. Yalnızca Yeni Yıl ve Chuseok, düğün günü ve bayram günü gibi tatillerde giyilir.

#######

Hanbok is a traditional Korean clothing. Women's hanbok is 'chima-jeogori' and men's hanbok is 'baji-jeogori'. Jeogori is what you wear on top, and skirt and pants are what you wear on the bottom. Hanbok is comfortable to work in because it is loose and does not stick to the body. Also, because different fabrics are used depending on the season, the summer hanbok is cool, and the winter hanbok is warm. In the past, people usually wore white hanbok, but these days, people wear colorful and pretty hanbok. But nowadays, people don't usually wear hanbok. It is only worn on holidays such as New Year's Day and Chuseok, wedding day, and feast day.
g_text_translate.ne: हानबोक एक परम्परागत कोरियाली कपडा हो। महिलाको हानबोक ‘चिमा–जोगोरी’ र पुरुषको हानबोक ‘बाजी–जोगोरी’ हो । जियोगोरी भनेको तपाईले माथि लगाउनु हुन्छ, र स्कर्ट र प्यान्ट तपाईले तल लगाउनु हुन्छ। हानबोक काम गर्न सहज छ किनभने यो ढीलो छ र शरीरमा टाँसिदैन। साथै, मौसम अनुसार फरक-फरक कपडाहरू प्रयोग हुने भएकाले, गर्मी हान्बोक चिसो हुन्छ, र जाडो हान्बोक न्यानो हुन्छ। विगतमा, मानिसहरूले सामान्यतया सेतो हानबोक लगाएका थिए, तर आजकल, मानिसहरूले रंगीन र सुन्दर हानबोक लगाउँछन्। तर आजकल, मानिसहरू सामान्यतया हानबोक लगाउँदैनन्। यो नयाँ वर्षको दिन र चुसेक, विवाहको दिन, र भोजको दिन जस्ता बिदाहरूमा मात्र लगाइन्छ।
g_text_translate.km: Hanbok គឺជាសំលៀកបំពាក់ប្រពៃណីរបស់កូរ៉េ។ Hanbok របស់ស្ត្រីគឺ 'chima-jeogori' ហើយ hanbok បុរសគឺ 'baji-jeogori' ។ Jeogori គឺជាអ្វីដែលអ្នកពាក់នៅលើកំពូល ហើយសំពត់ និងខោគឺជាអ្វីដែលអ្នកពាក់នៅខាងក្រោម។ Hanbok ងាយស្រួល​ក្នុង​ការ​ធ្វើ​ការ​ព្រោះ​វា​រលុង មិន​ជាប់​នឹង​ខ្លួន។ ម្យ៉ាងទៀត ដោយសារក្រណាត់ផ្សេងៗគ្នាត្រូវបានប្រើប្រាស់អាស្រ័យលើរដូវកាល ហាន់បុករដូវក្តៅគឺត្រជាក់ ហើយហាន់បុករដូវរងាមានភាពកក់ក្តៅ។ កាល​ពី​មុន មនុស្ស​តែង​ស្លៀក​ហាន់​បុក​ពណ៌​ស ប៉ុន្តែ​សម័យ​នេះ​គេ​ស្លៀក​ហាន់​បុក​ចម្រុះ​ពណ៌​ស្អាត។ ប៉ុន្តែ​សព្វ​ថ្ងៃ​នេះ មនុស្ស​ម្នា​មិន​សូវ​ស្លៀក​ហាន់​បុក​ទេ។ វា​ត្រូវ​បាន​ពាក់​តែ​ក្នុង​ថ្ងៃ​ឈប់​សម្រាក​ដូច​ជា​ថ្ងៃ​ចូល​ឆ្នាំ និង Chuseok ថ្ងៃ​មង្គលការ និង​ថ្ងៃ​បុណ្យ។
g_text_translate.si: හැන්බොක් යනු සාම්ප්‍රදායික කොරියානු ඇඳුමකි. කාන්තා හැන්බොක් යනු 'චිමා-ජෙගෝරි' වන අතර පිරිමි හැන්බොක් 'බජි-ජෙගෝරි' වේ. Jeogori යනු ඔබ ඉහළින් අඳින අතර, ඔබ පහළින් අඳින්නේ සාය සහ කලිසම් ය. හැන්බොක් ලිහිල් හා ශරීරයට නොගැලපෙන නිසා වැඩ කිරීමට පහසු වේ. එසේම, සමය අනුව විවිධ රෙදි වර්ග භාවිතා කරන නිසා, ගිම්හාන hanbok සිසිල් වන අතර, ශීත hanbok උණුසුම් වේ. ඉස්සර මිනිස්සු සාමාන්‍යයෙන් සුදු හැන්බොක් ඇඳගෙන හිටියට අද මිනිස්සු අඳින්නේ පාට පාට ලස්සන හැන්බොක්. නමුත් වර්තමානයේ මිනිසුන් සාමාන්‍යයෙන් හැන්බොක් අඳින්නේ නැත. එය පැළඳ සිටින්නේ අලුත් අවුරුදු දිනය සහ චුසොක්, විවාහ දිනය සහ මංගල්‍ය දිනය වැනි නිවාඩු දිනවල පමණි.
g_text_translate.bn: হ্যানবোক একটি ঐতিহ্যবাহী কোরিয়ান পোশাক। মহিলাদের হানবোক হল 'চিমা-জিওগরি' এবং পুরুষদের হ্যানবক হল 'বাজি-জেগোরি'। জিওগোরি হল আপনি উপরে যা পরেন এবং স্কার্ট এবং প্যান্ট হল আপনি নীচে যা পরেন। হ্যানবোক কাজ করতে আরামদায়ক কারণ এটি ঢিলেঢালা এবং শরীরের সাথে লেগে থাকে না। এছাড়াও, যেহেতু ঋতুর উপর নির্ভর করে বিভিন্ন কাপড় ব্যবহার করা হয়, গ্রীষ্মের হ্যানবোক শীতল এবং শীতকালীন হ্যানবোক উষ্ণ। অতীতে, লোকেরা সাধারণত সাদা হ্যানবোক পরত, কিন্তু আজকাল, লোকেরা রঙিন এবং সুন্দর হ্যানবোক পরে। কিন্তু আজকাল, লোকেরা সাধারণত হ্যানবোক পরে না। এটি শুধুমাত্র ছুটির দিনে যেমন নববর্ষের দিন এবং চুসেওক, বিবাহের দিন এবং ভোজের দিন পরিধান করা হয়।
g_text_translate.my: Hanbok သည် ကိုရီးယားရိုးရာအဝတ်အစားတစ်ခုဖြစ်သည်။ အမျိုးသမီး ဟန်ဘောက 'chima-jeogori' ဖြစ်ပြီး အမျိုးသား ဟန်ဘောက 'baji-jeogori' ဖြစ်သည်။ Jeogori သည် သင် အပေါ်ထပ်တွင် ၀တ်ဆင်ထားပြီး စကတ် နှင့် ဘောင်းဘီ သည် အောက်ခြေတွင် သင်ဝတ်ဆင်သည့် အရာဖြစ်သည်။ Hanbok သည် ပေါ့ပါးပြီး ခန္ဓာကိုယ်နှင့် မကပ်နိုင်သောကြောင့် အလုပ်လုပ်ရန် အဆင်ပြေသည်။ ထို့အပြင် ရာသီပေါ် မူတည်၍ အထည်အမျိုးမျိုးကို အသုံးပြုထားသောကြောင့် နွေရာသီ ဟန်ဘောက်သည် အေးမြပြီး ဆောင်းရာသီဟန်ဘောက နွေးထွေးသည်။ အရင်တုန်းက လူတွေက အဖြူရောင် ဟန်ဘောက်ကို ၀တ်လေ့ရှိပေမယ့် အခုခေတ်မှာတော့ ရောင်စုံနဲ့ လှပတဲ့ ဟန်ဘောက်ကို ၀တ်ကြတယ်။ ဒါပေမယ့် အခုခေတ်မှာတော့ လူတွေက ဟန်ဘောက်ကို မ၀တ်ကြဘူး။ နှစ်သစ်ကူးနေ့ နှင့် Chuseok ၊ မင်္ဂလာနေ့ နှင့် ပွဲနေ့ ကဲ့သို့သော အားလပ်ရက်များတွင်သာ ဝတ်ဆင်ကြပါသည်။

Hanbok'un tanımına uyanı seçin.

1. Sadece kadınlar jeogori giyer.
2. Tüm hanbok renkleri aynıdır.
3. Mevsime bağlı olarak kumaş farklıdır.
4. Etkinlikleri yapmak rahat olduğu için insanlar bu günlerde hala onu çok fazla kullanıyor.
--------------------
<g>옷감:</g>Kumaş
<g>계절:</g>Mevsim

<g>N + 에 따라:</g>Göre, bağlı olarak.

#######

Choose the one that fits the description of Hanbok.

1. Only women wear jeogori.
2. All hanbok colors are the same.
3. Depending on the season, the fabric is different.
4. People still wear it a lot these days because it's comfortable to do activities.
--------------------
<g>옷감:</g> Fabric
<g>계절:</g> Season

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> According to, depending on.
explain.ne:
हानबोकको विवरणसँग मिल्ने एउटा छान्नुहोस्।

१. महिलाहरु मात्र जोगोरी लगाउँछन्।
2. सबै हानबोक रंगहरू समान छन्।
3. मौसम अनुसार, कपडा फरक छ।
4. मानिसहरू अझै पनि यसलाई धेरै दिन लगाउँछन् किनभने यो गतिविधिहरू गर्न सहज छ।
----------------------------------
<g>옷감:</g> कपडा
<g>계절:</g> सिजन

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> अनुसार, निर्भर गर्दछ।
explain.km:
ជ្រើសរើសមួយណាដែលសមនឹងការពិពណ៌នារបស់ Hanbok ។

1. មានតែស្ត្រីទេដែលពាក់ jeogori ។
2. ពណ៌ hanbok ទាំងអស់គឺដូចគ្នា។
3. អាស្រ័យលើរដូវកាលក្រណាត់គឺខុសគ្នា។
4. សព្វថ្ងៃនេះមនុស្សនៅតែពាក់វាច្រើនព្រោះវាមានផាសុកភាពក្នុងការធ្វើសកម្មភាព។
--------------------
<g>옷감:</g> ក្រណាត់
<g>계절:</g> រដូវ

<g>N +에 따라:</g> យោងទៅតាម អាស្រ័យលើ។
explain.si:
Hanbok හි විස්තරයට ගැලපෙන එකක් තෝරන්න.

1. jeogori අඳින්නේ කාන්තාවන් පමණි.
2. සියලුම හැන්බොක් වර්ණ සමාන වේ.
3. සමය අනුව, රෙදි වෙනස් වේ.
4. ක්‍රියාකාරකම් කිරීමට පහසු නිසා මිනිසුන් මේ දිනවල බොහෝ විට එය පැළඳ සිටී.
----------------------
<g>옷감:</g> රෙදි
<g>계절:</g> වාරය

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> අනුව, මත පදනම්ව.
explain.bn:
হ্যানবোকের বর্ণনার সাথে মানানসই একটি বেছে নিন।

1. শুধুমাত্র মহিলারা জেগোরি পরেন।
2. সব হ্যানবোক রং একই.
3. ঋতু উপর নির্ভর করে, ফ্যাব্রিক বিভিন্ন হয়.
4. লোকেরা এখনও এটি অনেক বেশি পরিধান করে কারণ এটি কার্যকলাপ করতে আরামদায়ক।
------------------
<g>옷감:</g> ফ্যাব্রিক
<g>계절:</g> সিজন

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> অনুযায়ী, নির্ভর করে।
explain.my:
Hanbok ၏ဖော်ပြချက်နှင့်ကိုက်ညီမည့်တစ်ခုကိုရွေးချယ်ပါ။

1. အမျိုးသမီးများသာ jeogori ဝတ်ဆင်ပါ။
2. Hanbok အရောင်အားလုံး အတူတူပါပဲ။
3. ရာသီပေါ် မူတည်ပြီး အထည်က ကွဲပြားပါတယ်။
4. လှုပ်ရှားမှုတွေလုပ်ရတာ သက်တောင့်သက်သာရှိတဲ့အတွက် အခုခေတ်လူတွေက ဝတ်နေတုန်းပါပဲ။
--------------------
<g>옷감:</g> အထည်
<g>계절:</g> ရာသီ

<g>N +에 따라:</g> အရ၊ အပေါ်မူတည်သည်။

general.g_text: 다음은 한국에서 다른 지역으로 전화를 걸 때 누르는 지역 번호입니다. 서울은 02, 인천은 032, 경기도는 031, 강원도는 033 등입니다. 같은 지역 안에 있는 사람에게 전화를 걸 때는 지역 번호를 누르지 않습니다. 외국에서 한국에 전화를 걸 때는 국가번호 82를 먼저 누르고, 그 다음에 지역 번호를 누르는데, 이때 지역 번호 앞에 있는 ‘0’을 빼고 눌러야 합니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Sau đây là mã vùng dùng để bấm khi gọi điện đến một khu vực khác ở Hàn Quốc. 02 cho Seoul, 032 cho Incheon, 031 cho Gyeonggi-do, 033 cho Gangwon-do, v.v. Khi gọi cho ai đó trong cùng khu vực thì không bấm mã vùng. Khi gọi đến Hàn Quốc từ nước ngoài, trước tiên hãy bấm mã quốc gia 82, sau đó là mã vùng, lúc này phải bỏ số '0' phía trước mã vùng.
g_text_translate.en: The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.it:
Quello che segue è il prefisso locale che premi quando chiami un'altra area in Corea. Seoul è 02, Incheon è 032, Gyeonggi-do è 031, Gangwon-do è 033 e così via. Quando chiami qualcuno all'interno della stessa area, non comporre il prefisso. Quando chiami la Corea dall'estero, componi prima il prefisso internazionale 82, quindi il prefisso locale.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.fr:
Voici l'indicatif régional sur lequel vous appuyez lorsque vous appelez une autre région en Corée. Séoul est 02, Incheon est 032, Gyeonggi-do est 031, Gangwon-do est 033, et ainsi de suite. Lorsque vous appelez quelqu'un dans la même zone, ne composez pas l'indicatif régional. Lorsque vous appelez la Corée depuis l'étranger, composez d'abord l'indicatif du pays 82, puis l'indicatif régional.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.es:
El siguiente es el código de área que presiona cuando llama a otra área en Corea. Seúl es 02, Incheon es 032, Gyeonggi-do es 031, Gangwon-do es 033, y así sucesivamente. Cuando llame a alguien dentro de la misma área, no marque el código de área. Cuando llame a Corea desde el extranjero, marque primero el código de país 82 y luego el código de área.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.id:
Berikut adalah kode area yang Anda tekan saat menghubungi area lain di Korea. Seoul 02, Incheon 032, Gyeonggi-do 031, Gangwon-do 033, dan seterusnya. Saat menelepon seseorang dalam area yang sama, jangan menekan kode area. Saat menelepon Korea dari luar negeri, tekan kode negara 82 terlebih dahulu, lalu kode area.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.ja:
以下は、韓国の他の地域に電話をかけるときに押す市外局番です。ソウルは02、仁川は032、京畿道は031、江原道は033など。同じエリア内の人に電話するときは、市外局番をダイヤルしないでください。海外から韓国に電話する場合は、国番号82、市外局番の順にダイヤルしてください。

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.de:
Das Folgende ist die Vorwahl, die Sie drücken, wenn Sie in ein anderes Gebiet in Korea anrufen. Seoul ist 02, Incheon ist 032, Gyeonggi-do ist 031, Gangwon-do ist 033 und so weiter. Wenn Sie jemanden innerhalb desselben Bereichs anrufen, wählen Sie nicht die Vorwahl. Wenn Sie Korea aus dem Ausland anrufen, wählen Sie zuerst die Landesvorwahl 82 und dann die Ortsvorwahl.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
以下是拨打韩国其他地区时要按的区号。首尔是02，仁川是032，京畿道是031，江原道是033等等。给同一地区的人打电话时，不要拨区号。从国外拨打韩国时，先拨国家代码82，再拨区号。

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
以下是撥打韓國其他地區時要按的區號。首爾是02，仁川是032，京畿道是031，江原道是033等等。給同一地區的人打電話時，不要撥區號。從國外撥打韓國時，先撥國家代碼82，再撥區號。

#######

When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.ru:
Ниже приведен код города, который вы нажимаете, когда звоните в другой регион Кореи. Сеул — 02, Инчхон — 032, Кёнгидо — 031, Канвондо — 033 и так далее. Когда вы звоните кому-то из своего региона, не набирайте код города. При звонке в Корею из-за границы сначала наберите код страны 82, затем код города.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.th:
ต่อไปนี้เป็นรหัสพื้นที่ที่คุณกดเมื่อโทรไปยังพื้นที่อื่นในเกาหลี โซลคือ 02 อินชอนคือ 032 คยองกีโดคือ 031 คังวอนโดคือ 033 และอื่นๆ เมื่อโทรหาคนในพื้นที่เดียวกันอย่ากดรหัสพื้นที่ เมื่อโทรเกาหลีจากต่างประเทศให้กดรหัสประเทศ 82 ก่อน จากนั้นจึงกดรหัสพื้นที่

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.pt:
A seguir está o código de área que você pressiona quando liga para outra área na Coreia. Seul tem 02, Incheon tem 032, Gyeonggi-do tem 031, Gangwon-do tem 033 e assim por diante. Ao ligar para alguém na mesma área, não disque o código de área. Ao ligar para a Coreia do exterior, disque primeiro o código do país 82 e depois o código de área.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.hi:
निम्नलिखित वह क्षेत्र कोड है जिसे आप कोरिया में किसी अन्य क्षेत्र में कॉल करते समय दबाते हैं। सियोल 02 है, इंचियोन 032 है, ग्योंगगी-डो 031 है, गैंगवॉन-डो 033 है, इत्यादि। एक ही क्षेत्र में किसी को कॉल करते समय, क्षेत्र कोड डायल न करें। विदेश से कोरिया कॉल करते समय पहले देश कोड 82 डायल करें, फिर क्षेत्र कोड।

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kore'de başka bir bölgeyi aradığınızda basacağınız alan kodu aşağıdadır. Seul 02, Incheon 032, Gyeonggi-do 031, Gangwon-do 033 vb. Aynı bölgede bulunan birini ararken alan kodunu çevirmeyin. Yurt dışından Kore'yi ararken önce ülke kodu 82'yi, ardından alan kodunu çevirin.

#######

The following is the area code that you press when you call another area in Korea. Seoul is 02, Incheon is 032, Gyeonggi-do is 031, Gangwon-do is 033, and so on. When calling someone within the same area, do not dial the area code. When calling Korea from abroad, dial the country code 82 first, then the area code.
g_text_translate.ne: तपाईंले कोरियाको अर्को क्षेत्रमा कल गर्दा तपाईंले थिच्नुहुने क्षेत्रीय कोड निम्न छ। सियोल ०२ हो, इन्चियोन ०३२ हो, ग्योन्गी-डो ०३१ हो, ग्याङ्वोन-डो ०३३ हो, र यस्तै अन्य। एउटै क्षेत्र भित्र कसैलाई कल गर्दा, क्षेत्र कोड डायल नगर्नुहोस्। विदेशबाट कोरियामा कल गर्दा, पहिले देशको कोड 82 डायल गर्नुहोस्, त्यसपछि क्षेत्रीय कोड।
g_text_translate.km: ខាង​ក្រោម​នេះ​គឺ​ជា​លេខ​កូដ​តំបន់​ដែល​អ្នក​ចុច​នៅ​ពេល​អ្នក​ហៅ​ទៅ​តំបន់​ផ្សេង​ក្នុង​ប្រទេស​កូរ៉េ។ ទីក្រុងសេអ៊ូលគឺ 02, Incheon គឺ 032, Gyeonggi-do គឺ 031, Gangwon-do គឺ 033 ហើយដូច្នេះនៅលើ។ ពេល​ហៅ​ទៅ​នរណា​ម្នាក់​ក្នុង​តំបន់​ដូច​គ្នា កុំ​ចុច​លេខ​កូដ​តំបន់។ ពេល​ហៅ​កូរ៉េ​មក​ពី​បរទេស ត្រូវ​ចុច​លេខ​កូដ​ប្រទេស 82 ជា​មុន​សិន បន្ទាប់​មក​លេខ​កូដ​តំបន់។
g_text_translate.si: පහත දැක්වෙන්නේ ඔබ කොරියාවේ වෙනත් ප්‍රදේශයකට කතා කරන විට ඔබ එබූ ප්‍රදේශ කේතයයි. සෝල් 02, ඉන්චියොන් 032, ග්යොංගි-ඩෝ 031, ගංවොන්-ඩෝ 033, යනාදී වශයෙන්. එකම ප්‍රදේශය තුළ සිටින කෙනෙකුට ඇමතීමේ දී ප්‍රදේශයේ කේතය අමතන්න එපා. විදේශයක සිට කොරියාවට ඇමතීමේ දී පළමුව රටේ කේතය 82 අමතන්න, පසුව ප්‍රදේශ කේතය අමතන්න.
g_text_translate.bn: আপনি কোরিয়ার অন্য এলাকায় কল করার সময় যে এলাকা কোডটি চাপবেন তা নিচে দেওয়া হল। সিউল 02, ইঞ্চিওন 032, জিওংগি-ডো 031, গ্যাংওন-ডো 033 এবং আরও অনেক কিছু। একই এলাকার মধ্যে কাউকে কল করার সময়, এলাকা কোড ডায়াল করবেন না। বিদেশ থেকে কোরিয়া কল করার সময়, প্রথমে দেশের কোড 82 ডায়াল করুন, তারপর এলাকা কোড।
g_text_translate.my: အောက်ဖော်ပြပါသည် ကိုရီးယားရှိ အခြားနေရာကို ဖုန်းခေါ်ဆိုသည့်အခါ သင်နှိပ်သည့် ဧရိယာကုဒ်ဖြစ်သည်။ ဆိုးလ်က 02၊ အင်ချွန်းက 032၊ Gyeonggi-do က 031၊ Gangwon-do က 033 စသဖြင့်ပေါ့။ တူညီသောဧရိယာအတွင်းမှတစ်စုံတစ်ဦးကိုခေါ်ဆိုသောအခါ၊ ဧရိယာကုဒ်ကိုမခေါ်ဆိုပါနှင့်။ နိုင်ငံခြားမှ ကိုရီးယားသို့ ဖုန်းခေါ်ဆိုသည့်အခါ နိုင်ငံ၏ကုဒ် 82 ကို ဦးစွာခေါ်ဆိုပြီးနောက် ဧရိယာကုဒ်ကို နှိပ်ပါ။

content.q_text: 외국에서 서울에 살고 있는 친구에게 전화를 하려고 합니다. 친구의 전화번호는 730-0201번입니다. 전화번호를 맞게 누른 것을 고르십시오.

explain.vi:
Tôi định gọi từ nước ngoài đến cho một người bạn sống ở Seoul. Số điện thoại của bạn ấy là 730-0201. Hãy chọn câu bấm số đúng.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
국가 번호: Mã quốc gia
지역 번호: Mã vùng

Khi gọi từ nước ngoài trước hết phải bấm mã quốc gia là 82, mã vùng Seoul là 02, bỏ số 0 phía trước là 2, sau đó đến số điện thoại của người bạn.
explain.en:
I am trying to call a friend living in Seoul from abroad. My friend's phone number is 730-0201. Choose the correct number I have to dial.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g> Country code
<g>지역 번호:</g> Area code

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.
explain.it:
Sto cercando di chiamare un amico che vive a Seoul dall'estero. Il numero di telefono del mio amico è 730-0201. Scegli il numero corretto che devo comporre.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g>Prefisso internazionale
<g>지역 번호:</g>Prefisso

Quando chiami dall'estero, devi prima comporre il prefisso internazionale 82, il prefisso di Seoul 02, rimuovere lo 0 davanti al 2 e quindi il numero di telefono dell'amico.

#######

I am trying to call a friend living in Seoul from abroad. My friend's phone number is 730-0201. Choose the correct number I have to dial.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g> Country code
<g>지역 번호:</g> Area code

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.
explain.fr:
J'essaie d'appeler un ami vivant à Séoul depuis l'étranger. Le numéro de téléphone de mon ami est le 730-0201. Choisissez le bon numéro que je dois composer.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g>Code postal
<g>지역 번호:</g>Indicatif régional

Lorsque vous appelez depuis l'étranger, vous devez d'abord composer l'indicatif du pays 82, l'indicatif régional de Séoul 02, supprimer le 0 devant le 2, puis le numéro de téléphone de l'ami.

#######

I am trying to call a friend living in Seoul from abroad. My friend's phone number is 730-0201. Choose the correct number I have to dial.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g> Country code
<g>지역 번호:</g> Area code

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.
explain.es:
Estoy tratando de llamar a un amigo que vive en Seúl desde el extranjero. El número de teléfono de mi amigo es 730-0201. Elija el número correcto que tengo que marcar.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g>Código de país
<g>지역 번호:</g>Codigo de AREA

Cuando llame desde el extranjero, primero debe marcar el código de país 82, el código de área de Seúl 02, quitar el 0 delante del 2 y luego el número de teléfono del amigo.

#######

I am trying to call a friend living in Seoul from abroad. My friend's phone number is 730-0201. Choose the correct number I have to dial.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g> Country code
<g>지역 번호:</g> Area code

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.
explain.id:
Saya mencoba menelepon seorang teman yang tinggal di Seoul dari luar negeri. Nomor telepon teman saya adalah 730-0201. Pilih nomor yang benar yang harus saya hubungi.

1. 2-82-730-0201
2. 82-2-730-0201
3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------

<g>지역 번호:</g>Kode area

Saat menelepon dari luar negeri, pertama-tama Anda harus menekan kode negara 82, kode area Seoul 02, hapus 0 di depan angka 2, lalu nomor telepon teman.

#######

I am trying to call a friend living in Seoul from abroad. My friend's phone number is 730-0201. Choose the correct number I have to dial.

1. 2-82-730-0201

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.
explain.ja:
海外からソウルに住んでいる友達に電話をかけようとしています。私の友人の電話番号は 730-0201 です。ダイヤルする必要がある正しい番号を選択してください。

3. 02-82-730-0201
4. 82-02-730-0201
--------------------
<g>국가 번호:</g>国番号
<g>지역 번호:</g>市外局番

海外から電話をかける場合は、まず国番号82、ソウルの市外局番02をダイヤルし、2の前の0を削除してから、友人の電話番号をダイヤルする必要があります.

When calling from abroad, you must first dial the country code 82, the Seoul area code 02, remove the 0 in front of the 2, and then the friend's phone number.

Wenn Sie aus dem Ausland anrufen, müssen Sie zuerst die Landesvorwahl 82, die Vorwahl von Seoul 02 wählen, die 0 vor der 2 entfernen und dann die Telefonnummer des Freundes.

general.g_text: 한국의 돈은‘원’으로 셉니다. 한국 돈에는 동전과 지폐가 있습니다. 동전에는 10원짜리, 50원짜리, 100원짜리, 500원짜리 네 가지 종류가 있습니다. 동전 중에서는 500원짜리의 크기가 제일 큽니다. 지폐는 종이로 만든 돈입니다. 1,000원짜리, 5,000원짜리, 10,000원짜리, 50,000원짜리 네 가지 종류가 있습니다. 지폐에는 사람의 얼굴이 그려져 있습니다. 천 원짜리에는 이황의 얼굴이, 오천 원짜리에는 이이의 얼굴이, 만 원짜리에는 한글을 만든 세종대왕의 얼굴이, 오만 원짜리에는 신사임당의 얼굴이 그려져 있습니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tiền Hàn Quốc được tính bằng "won". Tiền Hàn Quốc bao gồm tiền xu và tiền giấy. Có bốn loại tiền xu là: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won và 500 won. Trong số các loại tiền xu, mệnh giá 500 won có kích thước lớn nhất. Tiền giấy là tiền làm bằng giấy. Có 4 loại: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won và 50,000 won. Tờ tiền có một khuôn mặt người được vẽ trên đó. Trên tờ 1,000 won là khuôn mặt của Lee Hwang, 5,000 won là Lee Yi, 10,000 won là vua Sejong - người phát minh ra chữ Hangeul, 50,000 won là Shin Saimdang.
g_text_translate.en: Korean money is counted as ‘won’. Korean money has coins and banknotes. There are four types of coins: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, and 500 won. Among the coins, the 500 won coin is the largest. Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.
g_text_translate.it:
Il denaro coreano viene conteggiato come "vinto". Il denaro coreano ha monete e banconote. Esistono quattro tipi di monete: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won e 500 won. Tra le monete, la moneta da 500 won è la più grande. La banconota è denaro fatto di carta. Ci sono quattro denominazioni: 1.000 won, 5.000 won, 10.000 won e 50.000 won. Sulla banconota è disegnato un volto umano. La banconota da 1.000 vinte presenta il volto di Yi Hwang, la banconota da 5.000 vinte presenta il volto di Yi Yi, la banconota da 10.000 vinte presenta il volto di Re Sejong e la banconota da 50.000 vinte presenta il volto di Shin Shaim Dang.

#######

Korean money is counted as ‘won’. Korean money has coins and banknotes. There are four types of coins: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, and 500 won. Among the coins, the 500 won coin is the largest. Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.
g_text_translate.fr:
L'argent coréen est compté comme "gagné". L'argent coréen a des pièces et des billets de banque. Il existe quatre types de pièces : 10 won, 50 won, 100 won et 500 won. Parmi les pièces, la pièce de 500 wons est la plus grande. Le billet de banque est de l'argent en papier. Il existe quatre coupures : 1 000 won, 5 000 won, 10 000 won et 50 000 won. Le billet de banque a un visage humain dessiné dessus. Le billet de 1 000 wons présente le visage de Yi Hwang, le billet de 5 000 wons présente le visage de Yi Yi, le billet de 10 000 wons présente le visage du roi Sejong et le billet de 50 000 wons présente le visage de Shin Shaim Dang.

#######

Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.

Entre las monedas, la moneda de 500 wones es la más grande. El billete es dinero hecho de papel. Hay cuatro denominaciones: 1000 wones, 5000 wones, 10 000 wones y 50 000 wones. El billete tiene un rostro humano dibujado. El billete de 1000 wones presenta la cara de Yi Hwang, el billete de 5000 wones presenta la cara de Yi Yi, el billete de 10 000 wones presenta la cara del Rey Sejong y el billete de 50 000 wones presenta la cara de Shin Shaim Dang.

g_text_translate.id:
Uang Korea dihitung sebagai 'menang'. Uang Korea memiliki koin dan uang kertas. Ada empat jenis koin: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, dan 500 won. Di antara koin-koin tersebut, koin 500 won adalah yang terbesar. Uang kertas adalah uang yang terbuat dari kertas. Ada empat denominasi: 1.000 won, 5.000 won, 10.000 won, dan 50.000 won. Uang kertas itu memiliki wajah manusia yang tergambar di atasnya. Uang kertas 1.000 won menampilkan wajah Yi Hwang, uang kertas 5.000 won menampilkan wajah Yi Yi, uang kertas 10.000 won menampilkan wajah Raja Sejong, dan uang kertas 50.000 won menampilkan wajah Shin Shaim Dang.

韓国のお金は「ウォン」として数えます。韓国のお金には硬貨と紙幣があります。コインは10ウォン、50ウォン、100ウォン、500ウォンの4種類。硬貨の中では500ウォン硬貨が一番大きい。紙幣は紙でできたお金です。 1,000ウォン、5,000ウォン、10,000ウォン、50,000ウォンの4つの金種があります。紙幣には人の顔が描かれています。千ウォン札はイ・ファンの顔、五千ウォン札はイ・イの顔、一万ウォン札は世宗大王の顔、五万ウォン札はシン・シャイム・ダンの顔である。

Die 1.000-Won-Note zeigt das Gesicht von Yi Hwang, die 5.000-Won-Note zeigt das Gesicht von Yi Yi, die 10.000-Won-Note zeigt das Gesicht von König Sejong und die 50.000-Won-Note zeigt das Gesicht von Shin Shaim Dang.

g_text_translate.zh-TW:
韓國的錢被算作“韓元”。韓國貨幣有硬幣和紙幣。硬幣有10韓元、50韓元、100韓元、500韓元四種。在這些硬幣中，500韓元的硬幣最大。鈔票是紙做的錢。有四種面額：1,000 韓元、5,000 韓元、10,000 韓元和 50,000 韓元。鈔票上畫著一張人臉。 1000韓元是李黃的臉，5000韓元是李毅的臉，10000韓元是世宗的臉，50000韓元是申善任堂的臉。

#######

Korean money is counted as ‘won’. Korean money has coins and banknotes. There are four types of coins: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, and 500 won. Among the coins, the 500 won coin is the largest. Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.
g_text_translate.ru:
Корейские деньги считаются «выигранными». Корейские деньги имеют монеты и банкноты. Существует четыре типа монет: 10 вон, 50 вон, 100 вон и 500 вон. Среди монет монета номиналом 500 вон является самой крупной. Банкнота – это деньги, сделанные из бумаги. Существует четыре номинала: 1000 вон, 5000 вон, 10 000 вон и 50 000 вон. На банкноте изображено человеческое лицо. На банкноте в 1000 вон изображено лицо И Хвана, на банкноте в 5000 вон изображено лицо И И, на банкноте в 10 000 вон изображено лицо короля Седжона, а на банкноте в 50 000 вон изображено лицо Шин Шаим Данга.

เงินเกาหลีนับเป็น 'วอน' เงินเกาหลีมีทั้งเหรียญและธนบัตร เหรียญมีสี่ประเภท: 10 วอน, 50 วอน, 100 วอน และ 500 วอน ในบรรดาเหรียญเหล่านั้น เหรียญ 500 วอนเป็นเหรียญที่ใหญ่ที่สุด ธนบัตรคือเงินที่ทำจากกระดาษ มีสี่สกุลเงิน: 1,000 วอน, 5,000 วอน, 10,000 วอน และ 50,000 วอน ธนบัตรมีรูปหน้ามนุษย์อยู่ ธนบัตร 1,000 วอนมีใบหน้าของยี่ฮวาง ธนบัตร 5,000 วอนมีใบหน้าของยี่ยี่ ธนบัตร 10,000 วอนมีใบหน้าของกษัตริย์เซจง และธนบัตร 50,000 วอนมีใบหน้าชินไชม์ดัง

g_text_translate.pt:
O dinheiro coreano é contado como “ganho”. O dinheiro coreano contém moedas e notas. Existem quatro tipos de moedas: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won e 500 won. Entre as moedas, a moeda de 500 won é a maior. Nota é dinheiro feito de papel. Existem quatro denominações: 1.000 won, 5.000 won, 10.000 won e 50.000 won. A nota tem um rosto humano desenhado. A nota de 1.000 won apresenta o rosto de Yi Hwang, a nota de 5.000 won apresenta o rosto de Yi Yi, a nota de 10.000 won apresenta o rosto do Rei Sejong e a nota de 50.000 won apresenta o rosto de Shin Shaim Dang.

g_text_translate.hi:
कोरियाई धन को 'जीता' के रूप में गिना जाता है। कोरियाई मुद्रा में सिक्के और बैंकनोट होते हैं। सिक्के चार प्रकार के होते हैं: 10 जीते, 50 जीते, 100 जीते, और 500 जीते। सिक्कों में 500 का जीता हुआ सिक्का सबसे बड़ा है। बैंकनोट कागज से बनी मुद्रा है। चार मूल्यवर्ग हैं: 1,000 जीते, 5,000 जीते, 10,000 जीते, और 50,000 जीते। बैंकनोट पर एक मानवीय चेहरा बना हुआ है। 1,000 के नोट में यी ह्वांग का चेहरा, 5,000 के नोट में यी यी का चेहरा, 10,000 के नोट में किंग सेजोंग का चेहरा और 50,000 के नोट में शिन शैम डांग का चेहरा शामिल है।

#######

Korean money is counted as ‘won’. Korean money has coins and banknotes. There are four types of coins: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, and 500 won. Among the coins, the 500 won coin is the largest. Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kore parası 'kazanılmış' olarak sayılır. Kore parasının madeni paraları ve banknotları var. Dört tür jeton vardır: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won ve 500 won. Madeni paralar arasında 500 wonluk madeni para en büyüğüdür. Banknot kağıttan yapılmış paradır. Dört mezhep vardır: 1.000 won, 5.000 won, 10.000 won ve 50.000 won. Banknotun üzerinde insan yüzü çizilmiştir. 1.000 wonluk banknotta Yi Hwang'ın yüzü, 5.000 wonluk banknotta Yi Yi'nin yüzü, 10.000 wonluk banknotta Kral Sejong'un yüzü ve 50.000 wonluk banknotta Shin Shaim Dang'ın yüzü yer alıyor.

#######

Korean money is counted as ‘won’. Korean money has coins and banknotes. There are four types of coins: 10 won, 50 won, 100 won, and 500 won. Among the coins, the 500 won coin is the largest. Banknote is money made of paper. There are four denominations: 1,000 won, 5,000 won, 10,000 won, and 50,000 won. The banknote has a human face drawn on it. The 1,000-won note features Yi Hwang's face, the 5,000-won note features Yi Yi's face, the 10,000-won note features King Sejong's face, and the 50,000-won note features Shin Shaim Dang face.
g_text_translate.ne: कोरियाली पैसालाई 'जित' मानिन्छ। कोरियाली पैसामा सिक्का र बैंक नोटहरू छन्। त्यहाँ चार प्रकारका सिक्काहरू छन्: 10 वोन, 50 वोन, 100 वोन, र 500 वोन। सिक्काहरू मध्ये, 500 वोन सिक्का सबैभन्दा ठूलो हो। बैंक नोट कागजबाट बनेको पैसा हो। त्यहाँ चार सम्प्रदायहरू छन्: 1,000 वोन, 5,000 वोन, 10,000 वोन, र 50,000 वोन। नोटमा मानव अनुहार कोरिएको छ। १ हजार जितेको नोटमा यी ह्वाङको अनुहार, ५ हजार जितेको नोटमा यी यीको अनुहार, १० हजार जितेको नोटमा राजा सेजोङको अनुहार र ५० हजार जितेको नोटमा शिन शाइम दाङको अनुहार रहेको छ।
g_text_translate.km: លុយកូរ៉េត្រូវបានរាប់ជា 'វ៉ុន' ។ លុយកូរ៉េមានកាក់ និងក្រដាសប្រាក់។ កាក់មានបួនប្រភេទគឺ 10 វ៉ុន 50 វ៉ុន 100 វ៉ុន និង 500 វ៉ុន។ ក្នុងចំណោមកាក់ទាំងនោះ កាក់ 500 វ៉ុន គឺជាកាក់ធំបំផុត។ ក្រដាសប្រាក់គឺជាលុយដែលធ្វើពីក្រដាស។ មាន ៤ និកាយ៖ ១,០០០វ៉ុន, ៥,០០០វ៉ុន, ១០,០០០វ៉ុន និង ៥០,០០០វ៉ុន។ ក្រដាសប្រាក់មានមុខមនុស្សគូសនៅលើវា។ ក្រដាសប្រាក់ 1,000 វ៉ុន មានមុខរបស់ Yi Hwang, ក្រដាសប្រាក់ 5,000 វ៉ុនមានមុខរបស់ Yi Yi, ក្រដាសប្រាក់ 10,000 វ៉ុនមានមុខរបស់ស្តេច Sejong និងក្រដាសប្រាក់ 50,000 វ៉ុនមានមុខ Shin Shaim Dang។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියානු මුදල් ගණන් කරනු ලබන්නේ 'දිනූ' ලෙසය. කොරියානු මුදල්වල කාසි සහ මුදල් නෝට්ටු තිබේ. කාසි වර්ග හතරක් ඇත: වොන් 10, වොන් 50, වොන් 100 සහ වොන් 500. කාසි අතර, වොන් 500 කාසිය විශාලතම වේ. මුදල් නෝට්ටුව යනු කඩදාසි වලින් සාදන ලද මුදල් ය. නිකායන් හතරක් ඇත: වොන් 1,000, වොන් 5,000, වොන් 10,000 සහ වොන් 50,000. මුදල් නෝට්ටුවේ මිනිස් මුහුණක් ඇඳ ඇත. ජයග්‍රාහී 1000 නෝට්ටුවේ Yi Hwang ගේ මුහුණත්, 5000-Won නෝට්ටුවේ Yi Yi ගේ මුහුණත්, 10,000-Won නෝට්ටුවේ Sejong රජුගේ මුහුණත්, 50,000-Won නෝට්ටුවේ Shin Shaim Dang මුහුණත් ඇතුළත් වේ.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়ান অর্থকে 'জিত' হিসাবে গণনা করা হয়। কোরিয়ান টাকা কয়েন এবং নোট আছে. চার ধরনের কয়েন আছে: 10 win, 50 win, 100 win, এবং 500 win. কয়েনগুলির মধ্যে, 500 ওয়ান কয়েনটি সবচেয়ে বড়। ব্যাঙ্কনোট হল কাগজের তৈরি টাকা। চারটি মূল্যবোধ রয়েছে: 1,000 ওন, 5,000 ওন, 10,000 ওয়ান এবং 50,000 ওয়ান। নোটটিতে মানুষের মুখ আঁকা আছে। 1,000-জিতের নোটে Yi Hwang-এর মুখ, 5,000-জিতের নোটে Yi Yi-এর মুখ, 10,000-জিতের নোটে রাজা সেজং-এর মুখ, এবং 50,000-জিতের নোটে শিন শাইম ডাং-এর মুখ।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားငွေကို 'ဝမ်' အဖြစ် ရေတွက်သည်။ ကိုရီးယားငွေတွင် အကြွေစေ့များနှင့် ငွေစက္ကူများရှိသည်။ ဒင်္ဂါးအမျိုးအစား လေးမျိုးရှိပါတယ်- ဝမ် 10 ဝမ် 50 ဝမ် 100 ဝမ် နှင့် 500 ဝမ်။ အကြွေစေ့များထဲတွင် ဝမ် 500 အကြွေစေ့သည် အကြီးဆုံးဖြစ်သည်။ ငွေစက္ကူသည် စက္ကူဖြင့်ပြုလုပ်ထားသော ငွေဖြစ်သည်။ ဝမ် 1,000, 5,000, 10,000 W, နှင့် 50,000 ဂိုဏ်းခွဲ လေးခုရှိသည်။ ငွေစက္ကူတွင် လူမျက်နှာပါရှိသည်။ ဝမ် 1,000 မှတ်စုတွင် Yi Hwang ၏မျက်နှာ၊ ဝမ် 5,000 မှတ်စုတွင် Yi Yi ၏မျက်နှာ၊ ဝမ် 10,000 မှတ်စုတွင် King Sejong ၏မျက်နှာနှင့် ဝမ် 50,000 မှတ်စုတွင် Shin Shaim Dang မျက်နှာပါရှိသည်။

general.g_text: 이것은 KTX의 노선도입니다. KTX는 한국의 고속철도입니다. 고속철도는 한국뿐 아니라 일본, 프랑스, 독일 등에도 있습니다. 한국의 고속철도는 2004년에 개통되었습니다. KTX는 보통 열차보다 빠르고 안전하며 편리합니다. 보통 시속 200~300km로 달립니다. KTX를 타면 서울에서 부산까지 2시간 40분쯤 걸립니다. 요금은 고속버스보다 두 배쯤 비싸지만, 속도는 두 배쯤 빠릅니다. KTX 표를 인터넷이나 전화로 예매하면 할인도 받을 수 있습니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Đây là sơ đồ đường đi của KTX. KTX là đường sắt cao tốc của Hàn Quốc. Đường sắt cao tốc không chỉ có ở Hàn Quốc, mà còn ở Nhật Bản, Pháp và Đức. Đường sắt cao tốc của Hàn Quốc được khai trương vào năm 2004. KTX nhanh hơn, an toàn và thuận tiện hơn tàu thường. Nó thường chạy với tốc độ 200-300 km/h. Nếu đi bằng KTX thì mất khoảng 2 giờ 40 phút từ Seoul đến Busan. Giá vé đắt gấp đôi xe tốc hành nhưng tốc độ nhanh gấp đôi. Bạn cũng có thể được giảm giá nếu đặt vé KTX trên internet hoặc qua điện thoại.
g_text_translate.en: This is the route map of KTX. KTX is a high-speed railway in Korea. High-speed railways are not only in Korea, but also in Japan, France, and Germany. Korea's high-speed railway was opened in 2004. KTX is faster, safer, and more convenient than regular trains. It usually runs at 200 to 300 kilometers per hour. It takes about 2 hours and 40 minutes from Seoul to Busan by KTX. The fare is twice as expensive as the express bus, but it is twice as fast. You can also get a discount if you book a KTX ticket online or by phone.
g_text_translate.it:
Questa è la mappa del percorso di KTX. KTX è una ferrovia ad alta velocità in Corea. Le ferrovie ad alta velocità non sono solo in Corea, ma anche in Giappone, Francia e Germania. La ferrovia ad alta velocità della Corea è stata aperta nel 2004. KTX è più veloce, più sicuro e più conveniente dei treni normali. Di solito va da 200 a 300 chilometri all'ora. g_text_translate.ne: यो KTX को मार्ग नक्शा हो। KTX कोरिया मा एक उच्च गति रेलवे छ। हाई-स्पीड रेलहरू कोरियामा मात्र होइन, जापान, फ्रान्स र जर्मनीमा पनि छन्। कोरियाको हाई-स्पीड रेलवे 2004 मा खोलिएको थियो। KTX नियमित रेलहरू भन्दा छिटो, सुरक्षित र अधिक सुविधाजनक छ। यो सामान्यतया 200 देखि 300 किलोमिटर प्रति घण्टामा चल्छ। यो KTX द्वारा सियोल देखि बुसान सम्म लगभग 2 घण्टा र 40 मिनेट लाग्छ। भाडा एक्सप्रेस बस भन्दा दोब्बर महँगो छ, तर यो दोब्बर छिटो छ। तपाईंले KTX टिकट अनलाइन वा फोन मार्फत बुक गर्नुभयो भने पनि तपाईंले छुट प्राप्त गर्न सक्नुहुन्छ।
g_text_translate.km: នេះគឺជាផែនទីផ្លូវរបស់ KTX ។ KTX គឺជាផ្លូវរថភ្លើងល្បឿនលឿននៅក្នុងប្រទេសកូរ៉េ។ ផ្លូវរថភ្លើងល្បឿនលឿនមិនត្រឹមតែនៅក្នុងប្រទេសកូរ៉េប៉ុណ្ណោះទេ ប៉ុន្តែក៏មាននៅក្នុងប្រទេសជប៉ុន បារាំង និងអាល្លឺម៉ង់ផងដែរ។ ផ្លូវរថភ្លើងល្បឿនលឿនរបស់ប្រទេសកូរ៉េត្រូវបានបើកនៅឆ្នាំ 2004 ។ KTX គឺលឿនជាង សុវត្ថិភាព និងងាយស្រួលជាងរថភ្លើងធម្មតា។ ជាធម្មតាវារត់ក្នុងល្បឿនពី ២០០ ទៅ ៣០០ គីឡូម៉ែត្រក្នុងមួយម៉ោង។ វាត្រូវចំណាយពេលប្រហែល 2 ម៉ោង 40 នាទីពីទីក្រុងសេអ៊ូលទៅប៊ូសានដោយ KTX ។ ថ្លៃ​សំបុត្រ​គឺ​ថ្លៃ​ជាង​ឡាន​ក្រុង​លឿន​ជាង​២​ដង ប៉ុន្តែ​វា​លឿន​ជាង​២​ដង។ អ្នកក៏អាចទទួលបានការបញ្ចុះតម្លៃផងដែរ ប្រសិនបើអ្នកកក់សំបុត្រ KTX តាមអ៊ីនធឺណិត ឬតាមទូរស័ព្ទ។
g_text_translate.si: KTX හි මාර්ග සිතියම මෙයයි. KTX යනු කොරියාවේ අධිවේගී දුම්රිය මාර්ගයකි. අධිවේගී දුම්රිය කොරියාවේ පමණක් නොව ජපානයේ, ප්‍රංශයේ සහ ජර්මනියේ ද ඇත. කොරියාවේ අධිවේගී දුම්රිය මාර්ගය 2004 දී විවෘත කරන ලදී. KTX සාමාන්‍ය දුම්රිය වලට වඩා වේගවත්, ආරක්ෂිත සහ පහසු වේ. එය සාමාන්‍යයෙන් පැයට කිලෝමීටර් 200ත් 300ත් අතර වේගයකින් ගමන් කරයි. KTX මගින් සෝල් සිට බුසාන් දක්වා පැය 2 යි විනාඩි 40 ක් පමණ ගත වේ. ශීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථයේ ගාස්තුව මෙන් දෙගුණයක් මිල අධික නමුත් එය දෙගුණයක් වේගවත් වේ. ඔබ අන්තර්ජාලයෙන් හෝ දුරකථනයෙන් KTX ටිකට් පතක් වෙන්කරවා ගන්නේ නම් ඔබට වට්ටමක් ද ලබා ගත හැක.
g_text_translate.bn: এটি KTX এর রুট ম্যাপ। KTX কোরিয়ার একটি উচ্চ-গতির রেলপথ। উচ্চ-গতির রেলপথগুলি কেবল কোরিয়াতেই নয়, জাপান, ফ্রান্স এবং জার্মানিতেও রয়েছে। কোরিয়ার উচ্চ-গতির রেলপথ 2004 সালে খোলা হয়েছিল। KTX নিয়মিত ট্রেনের চেয়ে দ্রুত, নিরাপদ এবং আরও সুবিধাজনক। এটি সাধারণত 200 থেকে 300 কিলোমিটার প্রতি ঘন্টায় চলে। KTX দ্বারা সিউল থেকে বুসান যেতে প্রায় 2 ঘন্টা 40 মিনিট সময় লাগে। ভাড়া এক্সপ্রেস বাসের তুলনায় দ্বিগুণ ব্যয়বহুল, তবে এটি দ্বিগুণ দ্রুত। আপনি অনলাইনে বা ফোনের মাধ্যমে KTX টিকেট বুক করলেও আপনি ছাড় পেতে পারেন।
g_text_translate.my: ဤသည်မှာ KTX ၏ လမ်းကြောင်းမြေပုံဖြစ်သည်။ KTX သည် ကိုရီးယားတွင် မြန်နှုန်းမြင့်ရထားလမ်းတစ်ခုဖြစ်သည်။ အမြန်ရထားလမ်းများသည် ကိုရီးယားတွင်သာမက ဂျပန်၊ ပြင်သစ်နှင့် ဂျာမနီတို့တွင်လည်း ရှိသည်။ ကိုရီးယား၏ မြန်နှုန်းမြင့်ရထားလမ်းကို 2004 ခုနှစ်တွင် ဖွင့်လှစ်ခဲ့သည်။ KTX သည် ပုံမှန်ရထားများထက် ပိုမိုမြန်ဆန်၊ လုံခြုံပြီး ပိုမိုအဆင်ပြေပါသည်။ ပုံမှန်အားဖြင့် တစ်နာရီ ကီလိုမီတာ ၂၀၀ မှ ၃၀၀ နှုန်းဖြင့် မောင်းနှင်သည်။ Seoul မှ Busan သို့ KTX ဖြင့် 2 နာရီ 40 မိနစ်ခန့်ကြာပါသည်။ ယာဉ်စီးခက အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကားထက် နှစ်ဆ ပိုဈေးကြီးပေမယ့် နှစ်ဆ ပိုမြန်ပါတယ်။ KTX လက်မှတ်ကို အွန်လိုင်း သို့မဟုတ် ဖုန်းဖြင့် ကြိုတင်မှာယူပါက လျှော့စျေးကိုလည်း ရရှိနိုင်သည်။

2. ផ្លូវរថភ្លើងល្បឿនលឿនមានតែនៅក្នុងប្រទេសកូរ៉េប៉ុណ្ណោះ។
3. KTX រត់ពី 200 ទៅ 300 គីឡូម៉ែត្រក្នុងមួយម៉ោង។
4. អ្នកអាចទិញសំបុត្រ KTX ថោកតាមអ៊ីនធឺណិត។
--------------------
<g>N + 만៖</g> ប៉ុណ្ណោះ។
<g>N + 도:</g> ផងដែរ។
<g>N + 뿐(만) 아니라:</g> មិនត្រឹមតែប៉ុណ្ណោះទេ ប៉ុន្តែក៏មានផងដែរ។
explain.si:
මෙම පාඨයේ අන්තර්ගතයට වඩා වෙනස් වන්නේ කුමක්ද?

1. KTX යනු කොරියාවේ අධිවේගී දුම්රිය මාර්ගයයි.
2. අධිවේගී දුම්රිය ඇත්තේ කොරියාවේ පමණි.
3. KTX පැයට කිලෝමීටර් 200 සිට 300 දක්වා ධාවනය වේ.
4. ඔබට ලාභදායී KTX ටිකට්පත් අන්තර්ජාලය හරහා මිලදී ගත හැක.
----------------------
<g>N + 만:</g> පමණයි.
<g>N + 도:</g> ද.
<g>N + 뿐(만) 아니라:</g> පමණක් නොව, ද.
explain.bn:
এই লেখার বিষয়বস্তু থেকে ভিন্ন কি?

1. KTX হল কোরিয়ার উচ্চ-গতির রেলপথ।
2. উচ্চ-গতির রেলপথ শুধুমাত্র কোরিয়ায় উপলব্ধ।
3. কেটিএক্স প্রতি ঘন্টায় 200 থেকে 300 কিমি চলে।
4. আপনি অনলাইনে সস্তায় KTX টিকেট কিনতে পারেন।
------------------
<g>N + 만:</g> শুধুমাত্র।
<g>N + 도:</g> খুব।
<g>N + 뿐(만) 아니라:</g> শুধু নয়, এছাড়াও।
explain.my:
ဤစာတို၏ အကြောင်းအရာနှင့် မည်သို့ကွာခြားသနည်း။

1. KTX သည် ကိုရီးယားနိုင်ငံ၏ မြန်နှုန်းမြင့်ရထားလမ်းဖြစ်သည်။
2. အမြန်ရထားလမ်းသည် ကိုရီးယားတွင်သာ ရနိုင်သည်။
3. KTX သည် တစ်နာရီလျှင် ကီလိုမီတာ 200 မှ 300 အထိ မောင်းနှင်နိုင်သည်။
4. စျေးပေါသော KTX လက်မှတ်များကို အွန်လိုင်းတွင် ဝယ်ယူနိုင်ပါသည်။
--------------------
<g>N + 만:</g> သာလျှင်။
<g>N + 도:</g> လည်းပါသည်။
<g>N + 뿐(만) 아니라:</g> မသာ၊

general.g_text: 고속버스는 고속도로로 해서 전국의 도시를 다니는 버스입니다. 고속버스는 ㉠<u>기차가 다닐 수 없는곳까지 사람들을 데려다 주는</u> 아주 편리한 교통수단입니다. 고속버스를 이용해 서 전국의 모든 도시에 갈 수 있습니다. ㉡<u>기차보다 자주 다니고</u>, ㉢<u>값도 기차보다 쌉니다</u>. 하지만 ㉣<u>주말 이나 휴가 때, 명절 때에는 길이 막혀서 시간이 많이 걸립니다</u>.

g_text_translate.vi: Xe buýt tốc hành là xe buýt chạy qua các thành phố trên cả nước trên đường cao tốc. Xe buýt tốc hành là phương tiện di chuyển vô cùng tiện lợi ㉠<u>đưa mọi người đến những nơi mà xe lửa không đến được</u>. Bạn có thể đến tất cả các thành phố trong cả nước bằng xe buýt tốc hành. ㉡<u>nó chạy thường xuyên hơn tàu lửa</u>, ㉢<u>nó cũng rẻ hơn tàu lửa</u>. Nhưng ㉣<u>vào cuối tuần, kỳ nghỉ và ngày lễ thì rất mất thời gian vì bị tắc đường</u>.
g_text_translate.en: Express buses are buses that travel through cities across the country on expressways. Express buses are a very convenient means of transportation that <g>㉠</g><u>takes people to places where trains cannot go</u>. You can go to every city in the country by using the express bus. Express buses <g>㉡</g><u>run more frequently than trains</u> and <g>㉢</g><u>are cheaper than trains</u>. However, <g>㉣</g><u>it takes a lot of time because the roads are blocked during weekends, vacations, and holidays</u>.
g_text_translate.it:
Gli autobus espressi sono autobus che viaggiano attraverso le città di tutto il paese sulle superstrade. Gli autobus espressi sono un mezzo di trasporto molto conveniente<g>㉠</g><u>porta le persone in luoghi dove i treni non possono arrivare</u>. Puoi andare in ogni città del paese utilizzando l'autobus espresso. Autobus espressi<g>㉡</g><u>corrono più frequentemente dei treni</u> e<g>㉢</g><u>sono più economici dei treni</u>. Tuttavia,<g>㉣</g><u>ci vuole molto tempo perché le strade sono bloccate durante i fine settimana, le vacanze e le vacanze</u>.

#######

Express buses are buses that travel through cities across the country on expressways. Express buses are a very convenient means of transportation that <g>㉠</g><u>takes people to places where trains cannot go</u>. You can go to every city in the country by using the express bus. Express buses <g>㉡</g><u>run more frequently than trains</u> and <g>㉢</g><u>are cheaper than trains</u>. However, <g>㉣</g><u>it takes a lot of time because the roads are blocked during weekends, vacations, and holidays</u>.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les bus express sont des bus qui traversent les villes du pays sur les autoroutes. Les bus express sont un moyen de transport très pratique qui<g>㉠</g><u>emmène les gens là où les trains ne peuvent pas aller</u>. Vous pouvez vous rendre dans toutes les villes du pays en utilisant le bus express. Bus express<g>㉡</g><u>roulez plus fréquemment que les trains</u> et<g>㉢</g><u>sont moins chers que les trains</u>. Cependant,<g>㉣</g><u>cela prend beaucoup de temps car les routes sont bloquées pendant les week-ends, les vacances et les jours fériés</u>.

#######

Express buses are buses that travel through cities across the country on expressways. Express buses are a very convenient means of transportation that <g>㉠</g><u>takes people to places where trains cannot go</u>. You can go to every city in the country by using the express bus. Express buses <g>㉡</g><u>run more frequently than trains</u> and <g>㉢</g><u>are cheaper than trains</u>. However, <g>㉣</g><u>it takes a lot of time because the roads are blocked during weekends, vacations, and holidays</u>.
g_text_translate.es:
Los autobuses expresos son autobuses que recorren ciudades de todo el país en autopistas. Los autobuses expresos son un medio de transporte muy conveniente que<g>㉠</g><u>lleva a la gente a lugares donde los trenes no pueden ir</u>. Puede ir a todas las ciudades del país utilizando el autobús expreso. Autobuses expresos<g>㉡</g><u>correr con más frecuencia que los trenes</u> y<g>㉢</g><u>son más baratos que los trenes</u>. Sin embargo,<g>㉣</g><u>lleva mucho tiempo porque las carreteras están bloqueadas durante los fines de semana, vacaciones y feriados</u>.

#######

Express buses are buses that travel through cities across the country on expressways. Express buses are a very convenient means of transportation that <g>㉠</g><u>takes people to places where trains cannot go</u>. You can go to every city in the country by using the express bus. Express buses <g>㉡</g><u>run more frequently than trains</u> and <g>㉢</g><u>are cheaper than trains</u>. However, <g>㉣</g><u>it takes a lot of time because the roads are blocked during weekends, vacations, and holidays</u>.
g_text_translate.id:
Bus ekspres adalah bus yang melakukan perjalanan melalui kota-kota di seluruh negeri di jalan tol. Bus ekspres adalah sarana transportasi yang sangat nyaman<g>㉠</g><u>membawa orang ke tempat-tempat yang tidak bisa dilalui kereta api</u>. Anda dapat pergi ke setiap kota di negara ini dengan menggunakan bus ekspres. Bus ekspres<g>㉡</g><u>lari lebih sering daripada kereta api</u> dan<g>㉢</g><u>lebih murah daripada kereta api</u>. Namun,<g>㉣</g><u>membutuhkan banyak waktu karena jalan diblokir selama akhir pekan, liburan, dan hari libur</u>.

#######

Express buses are buses that travel through cities across the country on expressways. Express buses are a very convenient means of transportation that <g>㉠</g><u>takes people to places where trains cannot go</u>. You can go to every city in the country by using the express bus. Express buses <g>㉡</g><u>run more frequently than trains</u> and <g>㉢</g><u>are cheaper than trains</u>. g_text_translate.hi:
एक्सप्रेस बसें वे बसें हैं जो एक्सप्रेसवे पर देश भर के शहरों से होकर यात्रा करती हैं। एक्सप्रेस बसें परिवहन का एक बहुत ही सुविधाजनक साधन हैं<g>㉠</g><u>लोगों को उन जगहों पर ले जाता है जहां ट्रेनें नहीं जा सकतीं</u>। एक्सप्रेस बस से आप देश के हर शहर में जा सकते हैं। एक्सप्रेस बसें<g>㉡</g><u>ट्रेनों की तुलना में अधिक बार चलें</u> और<g>㉢</g><u>ट्रेन से सस्ते हैं</u>। तथापि,<g>㉣</g><u>इसमें बहुत समय लगता है क्योंकि सप्ताहांत, छुट्टियों और छुट्टियों के दौरान सड़कें अवरुद्ध रहती हैं</u>।

g_text_translate.ne: एक्सप्रेस बसहरू एक्सप्रेसवेमा देश भरका शहरहरूमा यात्रा गर्ने बसहरू हुन्। एक्सप्रेस बसहरू यातायातको एक धेरै सुविधाजनक माध्यम हो जसले <g>㉠</g><u>मानिसहरूलाई रेलहरू जान नसक्ने ठाउँहरूमा लैजान्छ</u>। तपाईं एक्सप्रेस बस प्रयोग गरेर देशको हरेक शहरमा जान सक्नुहुन्छ। एक्सप्रेस बसहरू <g>㉡</g><u>रेलहरू भन्दा धेरै पटक चल्छन्</u> र <g>㉢</g><u>रेलहरू भन्दा सस्तो छन्</u>। यद्यपि, <g>㉣</g><u>यसले धेरै समय लिन्छ किनभने सप्ताहन्त, बिदा र बिदाहरूमा सडकहरू अवरुद्ध हुन्छन्</u>।
g_text_translate.km: រថយន្តក្រុង Express គឺជារថយន្តក្រុងដែលធ្វើដំណើរឆ្លងកាត់ទីក្រុងនានាទូទាំងប្រទេសនៅលើផ្លូវល្បឿនលឿន។ ឡានក្រុងរហ័សគឺជាមធ្យោបាយធ្វើដំណើរដ៏ងាយស្រួលដែល <g>㉠</g><u>នាំមនុស្សទៅកន្លែងដែលរថភ្លើងមិនអាចទៅបាន</u>។ អ្នកអាចទៅគ្រប់ទីក្រុងទាំងអស់ក្នុងប្រទេសដោយប្រើឡានក្រុងលឿន។ ឡានក្រុងរហ័ស <g>㉡</g><u>រត់ញឹកញាប់ជាងរថភ្លើង</u> ហើយ <g>㉢</g><u>មានតម្លៃថោកជាងរថភ្លើង</u>។ ទោះជាយ៉ាងណាក៏ដោយ <g>㉣</g><u>វាត្រូវការពេលវេលាច្រើន ដោយសារផ្លូវត្រូវបានបិទក្នុងអំឡុងពេលចុងសប្តាហ៍ វិស្សមកាល និងថ្ងៃឈប់សម្រាក</u>។
g_text_translate.si: සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් යනු අධිවේගී මාර්ග ඔස්සේ රට පුරා නගර හරහා ගමන් කරන බස් රථ වේ. සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස්රථ යනු <g>㉠</g><u>දුම්රිය යා නොහැකි ස්ථාන වෙත මිනිසුන් රැගෙන යන</u> ඉතා පහසු ප්‍රවාහන මාධ්‍යයකි. ශීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථයෙන් ඔබට දිවයිනේ සෑම නගරයකටම යා හැකිය. සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථ <g>㉡</g><u>දුම්රියවලට වඩා නිතර ධාවනය වේ</u> සහ <g>㉢</g><u>දුම්රියවලට වඩා ලාභදායී වේ</u>. කෙසේ වෙතත්, <g>㉣</g><u>සති අන්ත, නිවාඩු සහ නිවාඩු දිනවල මාර්ග අවහිර වී ඇති නිසා</u>ට බොහෝ කාලයක් ගතවේ.
g_text_translate.bn: এক্সপ্রেস বাসগুলি এমন বাস যা এক্সপ্রেসওয়েতে সারা দেশের শহরগুলির মধ্যে দিয়ে ভ্রমণ করে। এক্সপ্রেস বাসগুলি পরিবহনের একটি খুব সুবিধাজনক মাধ্যম যা <g>㉠</g><u>লোকদের এমন জায়গায় নিয়ে যায় যেখানে ট্রেন যেতে পারে না</u>। আপনি এক্সপ্রেস বাস ব্যবহার করে দেশের প্রতিটি শহরে যেতে পারেন। এক্সপ্রেস বাস <g>㉡</g><u>ট্রেনের তুলনায় বেশি চলে</u> এবং <g>㉢</g><u>ট্রেনের চেয়ে সস্তা</u>। যাইহোক, <g>㉣</g><u>এটি অনেক সময় নেয় কারণ সাপ্তাহিক ছুটির দিনে, ছুটিতে এবং ছুটির দিনে রাস্তাগুলি অবরুদ্ধ থাকে</u>৷
g_text_translate.my: အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကားများသည် နိုင်ငံတစ်ဝှမ်းရှိ မြို့များကို အမြန်လမ်းများပေါ်တွင် ဖြတ်သန်းသွားလာသော ဘတ်စ်ကားများဖြစ်သည်။ အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကားများသည် <g>㉠</g><u>လူများကို ရထားမသွားနိုင်သောနေရာများသို့ ပို့ဆောင်ပေးသော အလွန်အဆင်ပြေသော သယ်ယူပို့ဆောင်ရေးနည်းလမ်းတစ်ခုဖြစ်သည်။ အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကားဖြင့် နိုင်ငံအတွင်းရှိ မြို့တိုင်းသို့ သွားနိုင်သည်။ အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကား <g>㉡</g><u>သည် ရထားများထက် မကြာခဏ ပြေးဆွဲသည်</u> နှင့် <g>㉢</g><u>များသည် ရထားများထက် စျေးသက်သာသည်</u>။ သို့သော်၊ <g>㉣</g><u>စနေ၊ တနင်္ဂနွေ၊ အားလပ်ရက်များနှင့် အားလပ်ရက်များတွင် လမ်းများပိတ်ဆို့နေသောကြောင့် အချိန်များစွာယူရပါသည်</u>။

1. සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථ සෑම විටම දුම්රියට වඩා වේගවත් වේ.
2. සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථ දුම්රියට වඩා මිල අධිකයි.
3. අධිවේගී බස් රථවලට යා හැක්කේ විශාල නගරවලට පමණි.
4. අධිවේගී මාර්ග හරහා අධිවේගී බස් ධාවනය වේ.
----------------------
<g>고속버스:</g> සීඝ්‍රගාමී බස් රථය

1. အမြန်ဘတ်စ်ကားများသည် ရထားများထက် အမြဲမြန်သည်။

general.g_text: 한국의 은행들은 보통 일주일에 5일만 열고, 토요일과 일요일에는 문을 닫습니다. 오전 9시부터 오후 4시까지 엽니다. 은행 문이 닫힌 시간에는 자동 입출금기 (돈을 넣거나 뺄 수 있는 기계)를 이용하면 됩니다. 자동 입출금기 이용 시간은 은행에 따라 조금씩 다르지만, 보통 오전 7시나 8시부터 밤 10시나 11시까지입니다. 그러나 24시간 편의점에 있는 자동 인출기 (돈을 뺄 수만 있는 기계)는 언제든지 24시간 이용이 가능합니다.

g_text_translate.vi: Các ngân hàng ở Hàn Quốc thường chỉ mở cửa năm ngày một tuần và đóng cửa vào thứ Bảy và Chủ nhật. Ngân hàng mở cửa từ 9 giờ sáng đến 4 giờ chiều. Khi ngân hàng đóng cửa, bạn có thể sử dụng máy rút và nạp tiền tự động (máy cho phép bạn nạp hoặc rút tiền). Giờ mở cửa của máy rút tiền tự động khác nhau một chút tùy theo ngân hàng, nhưng thường là từ 7 hoặc 8 giờ sáng đến 10 hoặc 11 giờ tối. Tuy nhiên, máy rút tiền tự động (máy chỉ có thể rút tiền) trong các cửa hàng tiện lợi hoạt động 24/24.
g_text_translate.en: Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.it:
Le banche in Corea di solito sono aperte solo 5 giorni a settimana e chiuse il sabato e la domenica. Sono aperti dalle 9:00 alle 16:00. Quando la banca è chiusa, puoi utilizzare uno sportello automatico (ATM - una macchina che ti consente di inserire o prelevare denaro). Gli orari di apertura degli sportelli automatici variano leggermente da banca a banca, ma di solito sono tra le 7:00 e le 8:00 e le 22:00 o le 23:00. Tuttavia, i bancomat (macchine che possono solo prelevare denaro) nei minimarket aperti 24 ore su 24 sono disponibili 24 ore al giorno.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les banques en Corée ne sont généralement ouvertes que 5 jours par semaine et fermées le samedi et le dimanche. Ils sont ouverts de 9h à 16h. Lorsque la banque est fermée, vous pouvez utiliser un guichet automatique (ATM - une machine qui vous permet d'insérer ou de retirer de l'argent). Les heures d'ouverture des guichets automatiques varient légèrement d'une banque à l'autre, mais sont généralement comprises entre 7 ou 8 heures et 22 ou 23 heures. Cependant, les distributeurs automatiques de billets (machines qui ne peuvent que retirer de l'argent) dans les magasins de proximité ouverts 24h/24 sont disponibles 24h/24.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.es:
Los bancos en Corea generalmente solo abren 5 días a la semana y cierran los sábados y domingos. Están abiertos de 9 a. m. a 4 p. m. Cuando el banco está cerrado, puede usar un cajero automático (ATM, una máquina que le permite ingresar o retirar dinero). El horario de apertura de los cajeros automáticos varía ligeramente de un banco a otro, pero suele ser entre las 7 u 8 a. m. y las 10 u 11 p. m. Sin embargo, los cajeros automáticos (máquinas que solo pueden retirar dinero) en las tiendas de conveniencia 24 horas están disponibles las 24 horas del día.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.id:
Bank di Korea biasanya hanya buka 5 hari dalam seminggu dan tutup pada hari Sabtu dan Minggu. Mereka buka dari jam 9 pagi sampai jam 4 sore. Saat bank tutup, Anda dapat menggunakan anjungan tunai mandiri (ATM - mesin yang memungkinkan Anda memasukkan atau menarik uang). Jam buka anjungan tunai mandiri sedikit berbeda dari bank ke bank, tetapi biasanya antara pukul 7 atau 8 pagi dan 10 atau 11 malam. Namun, mesin ATM tunai (mesin yang hanya dapat menarik uang) di minimarket 24 jam tersedia 24 jam sehari.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.ja:
韓国の銀行は通常、週に 5 日しか営業しておらず、土曜日と日曜日が休みです。午前9時から午後4時まで営業しています。銀行が閉まっているときは、現金自動預け払い機 (ATM - お金を出し入れできる機械) を使用できます。現金自動預け払い機の営業時間は銀行によって多少異なりますが、通常は午前 7 時または 8 時から午後 10 時または 11 時までです。ただし、24時間営業のコンビニエンスストアのキャッシュディスペンサー（現金の引き出しのみ可能な機械）は、24時間利用可能です。

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.de:
Banken in Korea sind normalerweise nur 5 Tage die Woche geöffnet und samstags und sonntags geschlossen. Sie sind von 9 bis 16 Uhr geöffnet. Wenn die Bank geschlossen ist, können Sie einen Geldautomaten (ATM - ein Automat, mit dem Sie Geld einzahlen oder abheben können) benutzen. Die Öffnungszeiten der Geldautomaten variieren leicht von Bank zu Bank, liegen aber in der Regel zwischen 7 oder 8 Uhr und 22 oder 23 Uhr. Geldautomaten (Maschinen, die nur Geld abheben können) in 24-Stunden-Convenience-Stores sind jedoch 24 Stunden am Tag verfügbar.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
韩国的银行通常每周只营业 5 天，周六和周日休息。他们的营业时间为上午 9 点至下午 4 点。当银行关闭时，您可以使用自动取款机（ATM - 一种允许您存入或取款的机器）。自动柜员机的营业时间因银行而异，但通常在早上 7 点或 8 点到晚上 10 点或 11 点之间。但是，24小时营业的便利店的提款机（只能取钱的机器）是24小时营业的。

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
韓國的銀行通常每週只營業 5 天，週六和周日休息。他們的營業時間為上午 9 點至下午 4 點。當銀行關閉時，您可以使用自動取款機（ATM - 一種允許您存入或取款的機器）。自動櫃員機的營業時間因銀行而異，但通常在早上 7 點或 8 點到晚上 10 點或 11 點之間。但是，24小時營業的便利店的提款機（只能取錢的機器）是24小時營業的。

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.ru:
Банки в Корее обычно открыты только 5 дней в неделю и закрыты по субботам и воскресеньям. Они открыты с 9:00 до 16:00. Когда банк закрыт, вы можете воспользоваться банкоматом (банкоматом – автоматом, позволяющим вносить или снимать деньги). Часы работы банкоматов немного различаются от банка к банку, но обычно это период с 7–8 утра до 22–23 часов. Однако банкоматы (автоматы, которые могут только снимать деньги) в круглосуточных магазинах доступны 24 часа в сутки.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.th:
ธนาคารในเกาหลีมักจะเปิดเพียง 5 วันต่อสัปดาห์ และปิดให้บริการในวันเสาร์และวันอาทิตย์ เปิดให้บริการตั้งแต่ 9.00 น. ถึง 16.00 น. เมื่อธนาคารปิด คุณสามารถใช้เครื่องถอนเงินอัตโนมัติ (ATM - เครื่องที่ให้คุณใส่หรือถอนเงิน) เวลาเปิดทำการสำหรับเครื่องถอนเงินอัตโนมัติจะแตกต่างกันไปเล็กน้อยในแต่ละธนาคาร แต่โดยปกติจะอยู่ระหว่าง 7.00 น. หรือ 8.00 น. ถึง 22.00 น. หรือ 23.00 น. อย่างไรก็ตาม ตู้กดเงินสด (เครื่องที่สามารถถอนเงินได้เท่านั้น) ในร้านสะดวกซื้อที่เปิดตลอด 24 ชั่วโมงเปิดให้บริการตลอด 24 ชั่วโมง

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.pt:
Os bancos na Coreia geralmente abrem apenas 5 dias por semana e fecham aos sábados e domingos. Eles estão abertos das 9h às 16h. Quando o banco estiver fechado, você pode usar um caixa eletrônico (ATM – máquina que permite inserir ou sacar dinheiro). O horário de funcionamento dos caixas automáticos varia ligeiramente de banco para banco, mas geralmente é entre 7h ou 8h e 22h ou 23h. No entanto, os caixas eletrônicos (máquinas que só podem sacar dinheiro) nas lojas de conveniência 24 horas estão disponíveis 24 horas por dia.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.hi:
कोरिया में बैंक आमतौर पर सप्ताह में केवल 5 दिन खुले रहते हैं और शनिवार और रविवार को बंद रहते हैं। वे सुबह 9 बजे से शाम 4 बजे तक खुले रहते हैं। जब बैंक बंद हो तो आप स्वचालित टेलर मशीन (एटीएम - एक मशीन जो आपको पैसे डालने या निकालने की अनुमति देती है) का उपयोग कर सकते हैं। स्वचालित टेलर मशीनों के खुलने का समय अलग-अलग बैंकों में थोड़ा भिन्न होता है, लेकिन आमतौर पर सुबह 7 या 8 बजे से रात 10 या 11 बजे के बीच होता है। हालाँकि, 24-घंटे सुविधा स्टोर में कैश डिस्पेंसर (मशीनें जो केवल पैसे निकाल सकती हैं) 24 घंटे उपलब्ध हैं।

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kore'deki bankalar genellikle haftanın sadece 5 günü açık olup cumartesi ve pazar günleri kapalıdır. Sabah 9'dan akşam 4'e kadar açıktırlar. Banka kapalıyken, otomatik para çekme makinesini (ATM - para eklemenizi veya çekmenizi sağlayan bir makine) kullanabilirsiniz. Otomatik para çekme makinelerinin çalışma saatleri bankadan bankaya biraz farklılık gösterir, ancak genellikle sabah 7 veya 8 ile 22 veya 23:00 arasındadır. Ancak 24 saat açık marketlerde bankamatikler (sadece para çekebilen makineler) 24 saat hizmet vermektedir.

#######

Banks in Korea are usually only open 5 days a week and closed on Saturdays and Sundays. They are open from 9 a.m to 4 p.m. When the bank is closed, you can use an automatic teller machine (ATM - a machine that allows you to insert or withdraw money). Opening hours for automatic teller machines vary slightly from bank to bank, but are usually between 7 or 8 a.m. and 10 or 11 p.m. However, cash dispensers (machines that can only withdraw money) in 24-hour convenience stores are available 24 hours a day.
g_text_translate.ne: कोरियामा बैंकहरू सामान्यतया हप्ताको 5 दिन मात्र खुल्छन् र शनिबार र आइतबार बन्द हुन्छन्। तिनीहरू 9 बजे देखि 4 बजे सम्म खुला छन्। बैंक बन्द हुँदा, तपाईंले स्वचालित टेलर मेसिन (ATM - पैसा घुसाउन वा झिक्न अनुमति दिने मेसिन) प्रयोग गर्न सक्नुहुन्छ। स्वचालित टेलर मेशिनहरू खुल्ने समयहरू बैंकबाट बैंकमा थोरै फरक हुन्छन्, तर सामान्यतया बिहान 7 वा 8 बजे र 10 वा 11 बजेको बीचमा हुन्छन्। यद्यपि, नगद डिस्पेन्सरहरू (मेसिनहरू जसले पैसा मात्र निकाल्न सक्छ) 24-घण्टा सुविधा स्टोरहरूमा दिनको 24 घण्टा उपलब्ध छन्।
g_text_translate.km: ធនាគារនៅប្រទេសកូរ៉េជាធម្មតាបើកត្រឹមតែ 5 ថ្ងៃក្នុងមួយសប្តាហ៍ ហើយបិទនៅថ្ងៃសៅរ៍ និងថ្ងៃអាទិត្យ។ ពួកគេបើកពីម៉ោង 9 ព្រឹកដល់ម៉ោង 4 រសៀល។ នៅពេលដែលធនាគារត្រូវបានបិទ អ្នកអាចប្រើម៉ាស៊ីនប្រាប់ដោយស្វ័យប្រវត្តិ (ATM - ម៉ាស៊ីនដែលអនុញ្ញាតឱ្យអ្នកបញ្ចូល ឬដកប្រាក់)។ ម៉ោងបើកសម្រាប់ម៉ាស៊ីនប្រាប់ដោយស្វ័យប្រវត្តិប្រែប្រួលបន្តិចបន្តួចពីធនាគារមួយទៅធនាគារ ប៉ុន្តែជាធម្មតានៅចន្លោះម៉ោង 7 ឬ 8 ព្រឹកដល់ម៉ោង 10 ឬ 11 យប់។ ទោះយ៉ាងណាក៏ដោយ ម៉ាស៊ីនចែកចាយសាច់ប្រាក់ (ម៉ាស៊ីនដែលអាចដកប្រាក់បាន) នៅក្នុងហាងងាយស្រួល 24 ម៉ោងគឺអាចរកបាន 24 ម៉ោងក្នុងមួយថ្ងៃ។
g_text_translate.si: කොරියාවේ බැංකු සාමාන්‍යයෙන් සතියකට දින 5ක් පමණක් විවෘතව පවතින අතර සෙනසුරාදා සහ ඉරිදා දිනවල වසා ඇත. ඒවා පෙරවරු 9 සිට සවස 4 දක්වා විවෘතව පවතී. බැංකුව වසා ඇති විට, ඔබට ස්වයංක්‍රීය ටෙලර් යන්ත්‍රයක් භාවිතා කළ හැකිය (ATM - ඔබට මුදල් ඇතුළු කිරීමට හෝ ආපසු ගැනීමට ඉඩ සලසන යන්ත්‍රයක්). ස්වයංක්‍රීය ටෙලර් යන්ත්‍ර සඳහා විවෘත වේලාවන් බැංකුවෙන් බැංකුවට තරමක් වෙනස් වන නමුත් සාමාන්‍යයෙන් උදේ 7 හෝ 8 සහ රාත්‍රී 10 හෝ 11 අතර වේ. කෙසේ වෙතත්, පැය 24 පුරා පවතින පහසුව සඳහා ගබඩාවල මුදල් බෙදාහරින්නන් (මුදල් ලබා ගැනීමට පමණක් හැකි යන්ත්‍ර) දවසේ පැය 24 පුරාම පවතී.
g_text_translate.bn: কোরিয়ার ব্যাঙ্কগুলি সাধারণত সপ্তাহে মাত্র 5 দিন খোলা থাকে এবং শনিবার এবং রবিবার বন্ধ থাকে। এগুলি সকাল 9 টা থেকে বিকাল 4 টা পর্যন্ত খোলা থাকে। যখন ব্যাঙ্ক বন্ধ থাকে, আপনি একটি স্বয়ংক্রিয় টেলার মেশিন ব্যবহার করতে পারেন (এটিএম - একটি মেশিন যা আপনাকে টাকা ঢোকাতে বা তুলতে দেয়)। স্বয়ংক্রিয় টেলার মেশিন খোলার সময় ব্যাঙ্ক থেকে ব্যাঙ্কে সামান্য পরিবর্তিত হয়, তবে সাধারণত সকাল 7 বা 8 টা এবং 10 বা 11 টার মধ্যে হয়। যাইহোক, 24-ঘন্টা সুবিধার দোকানে ক্যাশ ডিসপেনসার (যে মেশিনগুলি শুধুমাত্র টাকা তুলতে পারে) 24 ঘন্টা উপলব্ধ।
g_text_translate.my: ကိုရီးယားရှိ ဘဏ်များသည် အများအားဖြင့် တစ်ပတ်လျှင် ၅ ရက်သာ ဖွင့်ကြပြီး စနေနှင့် တနင်္ဂနွေများတွင် ပိတ်သည်။ မနက် ၉ နာရီကနေ ညနေ ၄ နာရီအထိ ဖွင့်ပါတယ်။ ဘဏ်ပိတ်သောအခါတွင် သင်သည် အလိုအလျောက် ငွေထုတ်စက် (ATM - ငွေထည့်ရန် သို့မဟုတ် ထုတ်ယူနိုင်သည့် စက်) ကို အသုံးပြုနိုင်သည်။ အလိုအလျောက်ပြောသူစက်များအတွက် ဖွင့်ချိန်သည် ဘဏ်တစ်ခုမှတစ်ခုသို့ အနည်းငယ်ကွဲပြားသော်လည်း အများအားဖြင့် နံနက် ၇ နာရီ သို့မဟုတ် ၈ နာရီမှ ၁၀ နာရီ သို့မဟုတ် ၁၁ နာရီကြားဖြစ်သည်။ သို့သော်၊ ၂၄ နာရီ အဆင်ပြေသော စတိုးဆိုင်များတွင် ငွေသားရေစင်များ (ငွေကိုသာ ထုတ်ယူနိုင်သော စက်များ) သည် တစ်ရက်လျှင် ၂၄ နာရီ ရရှိနိုင်ပါသည်။

--------------------
<g>이용기간:</g> រយៈពេលនៃការប្រើប្រាស់

<g>N +에 따라:</g> អាស្រ័យ​លើ។
explain.si:
මෙම පාඨයේ අන්තර්ගතයට සමාන එකක් තෝරන්න.

1. හවස 4න් පස්සේ සල්ලි ගන්න බෑ.
2. කොරියාවේ බැංකු සඳුදා සිට සෙනසුරාදා දක්වා විවෘතව පවතී.
3. ස්වයංක්‍රීය ටෙලර් යන්ත්‍රය විවෘත කරන වේලාවන් බැංකුව අනුව වෙනස් වේ.
4. සෑම බැංකුවකටම විවිධ විවෘත කිරීම් සහ වැසීමේ වේලාවන් ඇත.
----------------------
<g>이용 기간:</g> භාවිත කාලය

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> මත පදනම්ව.
explain.bn:
এই পাঠ্যের বিষয়বস্তুর অনুরূপ একটি চয়ন করুন৷

1. আপনি বিকাল 4 টার পর টাকা তুলতে পারবেন না।
2. কোরিয়ার ব্যাঙ্কগুলি সোমবার থেকে শনিবার খোলা থাকে৷
3. স্বয়ংক্রিয় টেলার মেশিন খোলার সময় ব্যাঙ্ক অনুসারে পরিবর্তিত হয়।
4. প্রতিটি ব্যাঙ্ক খোলার এবং বন্ধ করার সময় আলাদা।
------------------
<g>이용 기간:</g> ব্যবহারের সময়কাল

<g>N + 에 따라:</g> এর উপর নির্ভর করে।
explain.my:
ဤစာတို၏ အကြောင်းအရာနှင့် ဆင်တူသည့်တစ်ခုကို ရွေးပါ။

1. ညနေ 4 နာရီနောက်ပိုင်းတွင် ငွေထုတ်ယူ၍မရပါ။
2. ကိုရီးယားရှိ ဘဏ်များကို တနင်္လာနေ့မှ စနေနေ့အထိ ဖွင့်ပါသည်။
3. အလိုအလျောက်ပြောသူစက်အတွက်ဖွင့်ချိန်သည် ဘဏ်အလိုက်ကွဲပြားသည်။
4. ဘဏ်တစ်ခုစီတွင် အဖွင့်နှင့်ပိတ်ချိန် မတူညီပါ။
--------------------
<g>이용 기간:</g> အသုံးပြုသည့်ကာလ

<g>N +에 따라:</g> ပေါ်မူတည်သည်။
